Place in oiled bowl and cover to let proof until double in size for about an hour.

title: Baker's Percentage for Pizza Dough
steps: Dissolve the fresh yeast in the water.
Combine the all-purpose flour, high gluten flour, and salt in the bowl of a mixer fitted with the dough hook.
Add the yeast water and olive oil.
Mix on low speed until the ingredients come together, then knead on medium speed until the dough is smooth, elastic, and clears the sides of the bowl.
Round the dough into a ball, place in a lightly oiled covered container, and bulk ferment at room temperature until nearly doubled.
Divide the dough into portions and round each tightly into a ball.
Place the balled dough in lightly oiled, covered containers and retard under refrigeration, or hold at room temperature until ready to use.
Bring the dough to room temperature before shaping.
Stretch each ball by hand into a round, working from the center out and leaving a slightly thicker rim for the crust.
Top as desired and bake in a very hot oven until the crust is browned and blistered and any cheese is melted and bubbling.

title: Biga for Pizza
steps: Mix all the ingredients together, cover and set aside.
Leave at room temperature overnight or refrigerate for up to 5 days.

title: Boston Cream Doughnuts
steps: Warm milk to 100 °F and whisk yeast into milk.
Add flours, sugars, eggs, salt, and butter.
Mix on low speed using the hook attachment for about 10 minutes. (Dough will be very soft).
Cover, refrigerate and let rise 1 hour, until double in size. Pastry Cream Filling vanilla bean 1 bean split
In a medium saucepan, heat 3/4 of the milk, vanilla bean and half the sugar over medium heat and bring to a boil.
Sift together the remaining sugar and cornstarch. Whisk in the remaining milk and yolks. Once the milk has boiled, temper with the cornstarch yolk mixture.
Pour all the liquid back into the pan whisking continuously. Return to medium heat while mixing and bring back to a boil.
Cook for about two minutes after it boils.

Place the cream in a bowl of a standing mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. Add the butter and continue to mix until the pastry cream reaches room temperature. Set in the refrigerator to cool until ready to use.

title: Brioche Dough
steps: Pour milk into a mixing bowl. Add yeast, eggs, salt, sugar, and flour.
Mix together on 1st speed for 3 minutes with dough hook attachment.
Mix together on 2nd speed for 3 minutes.
Place butter between parchment paper and beat down to flatten. Break into small pieces and add to dough while mixing.
Continue to mix for 12-15 minutes. Near the end, scrape down the sides.
Scrape into bowl and cover with plastic wrap.
Allow the dough to sit for 1 hour at room temperature and then transfer to the refrigerator and store overnight.
notes: NOTE: all ingredients must be cold.

title: Bun Dough
steps: Add flour and nutmeg to mixing bowl with paddle attachment
Add butter, sugar and salt to mixing bowl and mix for 3 minutes on low speed. Continue to mix until dough is sandy.
Warm milk to 100°F and whisk in yeast.
Whisk eggs in a separate bowl to break them, and then whisk into yeast mixture.
Add the yeast/milk mixture, eggs and lemon zest to the mixing bowl. Switch to a dough hook and mix for 3 minutes on low speed. Increase to second speed and mix for an additional 3 minutes.
Mix for 3 additional minutes on high speed.
Place in bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let rest until doubled in size.

title: Cecini (Flatbread)
steps: Place two sheet pans in a pre-heated 450 ° F oven. Allow them to get hot.
Combine all the ingredients together in the bowl of a standing mixer. Beat using a paddle until the batter is smooth. The batter will be extremely wet.
Pour enough oil on each pan to grease it generously.
Divide the batter between the two pans and smooth it evenly with a spatula.
Bake for 10 to 15 minutes until golden brown and set.
Serve warm.

title: Chelsea Buns
steps: Place dough in slightly oiled bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let dough rest until doubled in size. Preheat oven to 400 °F
Brush melted butter over the remainder of the dough, generously.
Mix cinnamon and brown sugar together; make sure there are no lumps. Sprinkle it generously over dough but not where the egg/water wash is.
Sprinkle drained soaked currants over dough on top of cinnamon sugar.

Roll top over towards the egg washed end, and tuck under and keep rolling. Seal at end. Elongate slightly to make sure roll is even but don't stretch dough. Roll should be about 2 ½ inches in diameter
Cut slices about 1 ½ inches apart and place on parchment lined sheet pan, arranged 4 X 5 close together.
Brush tops generously with melted butter.

Bake in oven for 30 to 35 minutes.

title: Chocolate Brioche
steps: Pour water into a mixing bowl. Add yeast, cocoa powder, salt, sugar, flour, and eggs.
Mix together on low speed for 3 minutes with dough hook attachment.
Mix together on medium speed for 3 minutes.
Turn the mixer off and scrape down the bowl. Continue mixing to develop gluten and until dough forms a ball around the hook attachment.

Place butter between parchment paper and beat down to flatten. This will soften the butter and enable it to incorporate into the dough more easily. Break the butter into small pieces and add it quickly to the dough while the machine is mixing.
While the dough is mixing, roughly chop the chocolate into 1/2-inch pieces.

Continue to mix the dough for 12-15 minutes. Towards the end of mixing, turn off the machine and scrape down the sides of the bowl again. The dough is ready when it is smooth and shiny and comes away from sides of the mixer.
Add the chocolate chunks until just combined.
Scrape into bowl and cover with plastic wrap.
Allow the dough to sit for 1 hour at room temperature and then transfer to the refrigerator and store overnight.
When ready to bake, preheat the oven to 350°F

title: Crispy Pizza Dough
steps: Mix the first 4 ingredients and allow it to sit for 10 minutes.
Add flour and salt and knead dough with a dough hook for 5 to 7 minutes.
Bench proof covered for 10 minutes and 5 ounce pieces for individual pizzas.
Round perfectly and make sure the seam is on the bottom. Place in an oiled cake pan and cover dough with additional oil. Wrap well, making sure the wrap is touching the surface of the dough.
For best results refrigerate for a minimum of one hour, or ideally overnight.

title: Dead Dough
steps: Combine all ingredients in saucepan and bring to a rolling boil for about 2 minutes.
Pour into a bowl over an ice bath and let cool. Red Brown Dead Dough
Sift beet powder over syrup.
Whisk in chili powder.
Using a dough hook add flours and mix on low speed until all incorporated. The dough will be crumbly.
Turn onto the bench and knead wearing rubber gloves until it comes together and color is evenly distributed.
Form dough into a disk and triple wrap in plastic, store at room temperature up to 3 days. For longer storage refrigerate. Yellow Dead Dough and whisk.
Add flours to mixture. Mix on low speed using the paddle attachment until incorporated.
Put on rubber gloves to prevent hands from turning yellow and work dough together on surface until smooth and color is evenly distributed.
Form dough into a disk and triple wrap in plastic, store at room temperature up to 3 days. For longer storage refrigerate.

title: Farm House White
steps: Place the pre-ferment and water in the bowl of a mixer and break up the pre-ferment until loosened.
Add the high gluten flour, whole wheat flour, SAF yeast, and salt.
Mix on low speed with the dough hook until the ingredients come together, then knead on medium speed until the dough is smooth, elastic, and clears the sides of the bowl.
Round into a ball, place in a lightly oiled covered container, and bulk ferment until doubled, folding the dough once or twice during fermentation to build strength.
Divide the dough into loaf-sized portions, pre-shape into rounds, and let rest.
Shape each piece into a loaf and place seam-side down in greased loaf pans.
Proof, covered, until the dough crowns above the rim of the pan.
Bake until deep golden brown and the loaves sound hollow when tapped.
Turn out onto racks and cool completely before slicing.

title: Gluten Free Bread Sticks
steps: Mix all dry ingredients in the bowl of a standing mixer fitted with a paddle attachment and beat to combine well.
Place all the liquid ingredients in another bowl and add them all at once to the dry ingredients. Mix on low speed until batter is dough is uniform for about 5- 7 minutes.
Divide dough into approximately 24 pieces (or more for thinner breadsticks) Roll into a thin cylinder on the bench to elongate. Place on a parchment
Cover loosely with oiled plastic wrap and allow to proof 20 to 30 minutes.
Brush with beaten egg white or water and strew with salt and/or seeds.
Bake in a preheated 375°F oven until firm.

title: Hot Cross Buns
steps: Preheat oven to 400 °F.
Mix all ingredients into bun dough by machine or by hand. Place dough in a bowl and cover with plastic wrap. Let rest until doubled in size.
Once doubled, cut dough into about 24 70 gram pieces.
Perform the 1-2-3-4 dough shaping method on each piece. Line the pieces on a parchment paper lined pan. Ideal is to have 4x6.
Proof in proof box for about 10 minutes.

While buns are proofing prepare Hot Cross Bun Paste. Mix the flour and oil together in a bowl of a standing mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. Add the water and mix on low speed until well combined. Adjust if necessary.
Remove buns from the proof box.
Place a number 801 tip in a pastry bag and fill it with Hot Cross Bun Paste.
Draw horizontal and vertical lines across pan in center of buns to make a cross on each bun.
Bake for about 35 minutes until golden and well risen.
While baking, prepare the simple syrup: 500 grams water and 500 grams sugar in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and cook for 3 minutes to thicken slightly. Brush tops of buns with simple syrup. bowl.

steps: Heat milk to 100 °F.
Whisk yeast into milk.
Add flours, sugar, eggs, salt, butter, lemon extract and lemon zest.
Mix on low speed for 10 minutes.
Cover, refrigerate and let rise 1 hour, until double in size.

title: Pizza Variations: Calzone Base
steps: Combine the ricotta, grated parmesan, and shredded mozzarella in a bowl.
Chop the fresh herbs and fold into the cheese mixture; season with salt and pepper.
Divide the prepared pizza dough into rounds and stretch each into a circle.
Place half of the cheese filling on one half of each round, leaving a border for sealing.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon and press the edges together to seal.
Crimp or fold the sealed edge to lock it, then dock the top a few times to vent steam.
Transfer to a parchment-lined sheet pan.
Bake in a hot oven until the crust is golden brown and the filling is heated through and molten.

title: Pizzeria-Style Pizza Sauce
steps: Heat oil in a large saucepan and add onion.
Sauté until transparent, about 15 minutes. Add garlic and cook briefly.
Add tomatoes and bouquet. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat and simmer about 1 hour, or until reduced by one quarter.

title: Plain White Sourdough
steps: Combine the high gluten flour and water and let autolyse, covered, until fully hydrated.
Add the active starter and salt and mix by hand or on low speed until incorporated.
Knead or slap-and-fold until the dough is smooth and elastic and passes the windowpane test.
Bulk ferment, covered, performing a series of stretch and folds at intervals during the first hours of fermentation.
Continue bulk fermentation until the dough has increased noticeably in volume and shows surface bubbles.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface, divide if needed, pre-shape into a round, and let rest.
Shape into a boule or batard and place seam-side up in a floured banneton.
Proof at room temperature until nearly doubled, or retard under refrigeration overnight for flavor development.
Turn the dough out onto parchment and score the top.
Bake with steam in a preheated hot oven, then reduce heat and continue baking until the crust is deep golden brown.
Cool completely on a rack before slicing.

title: Poolish for Baguettes
steps: Place water in the bowl of a 60 quart or smaller mixer depending on batch size.
Sprinkle yeast on top. Allow it to stand for 2 to 3 minutes and whisk to combine.
Add the flour to the water.
Beat with the paddle attachment until all the flour is absorbed.
Cover and set aside for 12 to 24 hours.
For longer storage, refrigerate the poolish immediately.

title: Pre-Ferment for Farm House White
steps: Sprinkle yeast on top. Allow it to stand for 2 to 3 minutes and whisk to combine.
Mix the salt into the flour and add it to the water.
Beat with the paddle attachment until all the flour is absorbed.
Cover and set aside for 12 to 24 hours (ideal time 17 hours).
For longer storage, refrigerate the pre-ferment immediately.

title: Pre-ferment
steps: Mix all ingredients together on low speed.
Cover with plastic and let rest overnight. Grains & Seeds
Boil water*.
Combine all grains and seeds in a bowl. Pour boiling water on top.
Cover and let rest overnight. Combine all grains and seeds in a bowl and pour cold measured water on top. Leave at room temperature until ready to use, up to 1 week.
notes: *If soaking grains and seeds for more than one day, do NOT boil water.

title: Pretzel Dough
steps: Add water and olive oil to mixing bowl.
Whisk in yeast.
Add flour and salt on top.
Mix on low speed using a hook attachment for about 5 minutes. Transfer to a lightly floured surface and then take out and knead by hand for 2-3 minutes.
Place in oiled bowl and cover to let proof until double in size for about an hour. Shaping Pretzels:

Poach pretzels in simmering water for 15-20 seconds. Place on oiled pan.
Sprinkle with coarse salt.
Place in 350°F oven for about 20 minutes.

title: White French Bread
steps: Place water in a mixing bowl.
Sprinkle the yeast on top and allow it to stand for approximately 3 minutes.
Whisk water until the yeast is dissolved.
Add the flours and sprinkle the salt on top of the flour.
Stir until all the flour is absorbed.

Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and begin kneading the dough by hand. The kneading process is -- stretch, fold, rotate and repeat, until you have a smooth, elastic dough. Or knead dough using the hook attachment of the mixer on low speed, mixing until smooth and elastic.
Lightly oil a mixing bowl, place dough in the bowl, cover with plastic wrap and let rest.
After thirty minutes turn the dough. Cover and allow to rest again until double in size.

steps: Add water to the 60 quart mixing bowl and whisk in yeast.
Add flours and salt. Cut up and add pre-ferment.
Mix on low speed for 3 minutes. Then mix on 2nd speed for 5 minutes.
Add soaked grains and seeds. Mix to incorporate on 1st speed for about 5 to 10 minutes.
Pour into oiled bin or leave in 60 quart bowl. Turn the dough. Cover and let proof. 30 minutes later turn the dough. After another 30 minutes start shaping.

title: Whole Wheat Sourdough
steps: Combine the high gluten flour, whole wheat flour, and water and autolyse, covered, allowing extra time for the bran to fully hydrate.
Add the active starter and salt and mix by hand or on low speed until incorporated.
Knead or slap-and-fold until the dough is smooth and moderately elastic.
Bulk ferment, covered, performing stretch and folds at intervals to build strength.
Continue bulk fermentation until the dough feels airy and has risen visibly.
Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface, divide if needed, pre-shape into a round, and let rest.
Shape gently into a boule or batard to preserve gas and place seam-side up in a floured banneton.
Proof at room temperature until nearly doubled, or retard under refrigeration overnight.
Turn the dough out onto parchment and score the top.
Bake with steam in a preheated hot oven, then reduce heat and continue baking until deeply colored and hollow-sounding.
Cool completely on a rack before slicing.

title: Zeppole Doughnuts
steps: Warm milk to 100 °F.
Whisk yeast into milk.
Add flours, sugar, and salt.
Mix on low speed using the hook attachment for about 10 minutes. (Dough will be very soft).
Cover and let rise at room temperature for about 1 hour, until double in size. Shaping Doughnuts
Heat the canola oil to 375° F
Carefully dip two metal tablespoons in the hot oil.
Take a large spoonful of the dough and use the other spoon to gently push it into the oil.
Repeat until there are several zeppoles in the hot oil. Flip them during the frying process to make sure both sides are colored evenly.
Drain on towel lined pans and while they have cooled but still slightly warm, toss them into a bowl of confectioners sugar.

title: Apricot Glaze
steps: Combine apricot preserves, and water in a heavy saucepan;
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring.
Strain mixture into another saucepan. Reduce mixture until it coats the back of a metal spoon.

title: Brown Sugar Icing or Glaze
steps: Heat the butter and brown sugar to dissolve the brown sugar.
Add the milk and salt and bring to a boil.
Allow to cool. Add in the extract or bourbon. Add and mix in confectioners' sugar to create a glaze or about 225 grams to make a frosting.

title: Chocolate Glaze
steps: Bring the sugar, water, and corn syrup to a boil making sure that all the sugar crystals are dissolved. Pour the boiled mixture over the chocolate. Whisk until smooth making sure not to get too many bubbles in the glaze.
With a cornet, write “Gustav Klimt” on the cake.

title: Chocolate Glaze for Eclairs
steps: Bring water and corn syrup to a boil
Pour over chopped chocolate. Whisk until smooth being careful not to incorporate too many air bubbles. Strain if necessary.

title: Coffee Buttercream
steps: Combine egg whites and sugar in mixing bowl. Stir well with whisk and then place over simmering water.
Heat, beating with whisk, until egg whites are hot to the touch (approximately 140 ºF) and all the sugar is dissolved.
Remove from the heat and place bowl on mixer. Whisk until cool and stiff.
Switch to the paddle attachment of the mixer if desired.
Add the butter gradually and continue beating until there are no lumps of butter and the mixture is completely smooth. Add flavoring.

title: Cream Cheese Icing
steps: Beat butter with paddle on medium speed until soft and light making sure there are absolutely no lumps. Beat in cream cheese and continue beating just until smooth. Add 10x (sifted if necessary) and beat just until smooth. Make sure not to overbeat. Use immediately.

title: Decorator'S Buttercream
steps: In a KitchenAid, beat the butter and shortening to remove any lumps.
Gradually add the sifted 10X and salt. Once incorporated, add the liquids.
Add the meringue powder and mix for several minutes until light and fluffy. Keep bowl covered at all times to reduce any crust forming from the meringue powder.

title: Decorator'S Buttercream
steps: Combine butter, shortening and salt and beat with a paddle until smooth.
Add extract and beat until smooth.
Gradually add the sifted confectioner's sugar making sure to scrape down completely.

Beat in the meringue powder, then the milk, and continue to mix for several minutes until the mixture is light and fluffy. crust from forming. and green for leaves and stems. Place over ice bath to cool or place in the freezer in a hotel pan.
While the mixture is cooling, beat the butter with a paddle until there are no lumps.
Gradually add the vanilla and the heavy cream.
Once the vanilla and heavy cream are incorporated, gradually add the cooled milk mixture.
Mix on low speed to incorporate ingredients. Increase speed as mixture begins to thicken. Continue to beat for 7 to 10 minutes or until butter is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth.

Warm over double boiler until the sugar is dissolved and mixture is warm.
Whip until ribbon stage. Add the extract.

Bake in a 375 F. convection oven for 8-10 minutes. Suggested flavorings: vanilla extract, almond extract, coffee extract, lemon oil, orange

steps: Over a hot water bath, melt the chocolate just to the point where it is completely melted. Make sure not to get the chocolate too hot.
Off the heat, add the light corn syrup and mix until thoroughly combined.
Set aside to cool and store for future use.

title: Fondant
steps: Combine sucrose, glucose and water in a small pot. Cook to 117˚C/243˚F making sure to wash down the sides of the pot and to minimize stirring as to avoid recrystallization.

Sprinkle some cold water onto marble slab; carefully pour syrup onto marble and sprinkle with additional water. Allow syrup to cool to approximately 66˚C/150˚F folding the outside inwards as to cool the syrup evenly.

Transfer the syrup to the bowl of an electric stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. Add the fondant and blend the syrup on medium speed until a thick, white crystallized paste has developed and continue to blend until cool enough to handle. Wrap and store for future use. sugar crystals.

title: Fondant Icing
steps: Place the fondant in a bowl. Using a metal spoon, work the corn syrup into the fondant.

Gently heat the fondant over simmering water to warm it slightly, Do not allow it to get too hot or it will lose its sheen. The ideal temperature for the fondant during heating is 100° F. During the heating, work the fondant continuously.

title: Marzipan
steps: Combine almond paste and 10 X with a paddle. The mixture will appear crumbly.
Add corn syrup and fondant and mix to combine. If the marzipan is dry, continue adding small amounts of corn syrup to slightly moisten.
Remove marzipan from the bowl and knead the marzipan smooth by hand and double wrap it in plastic. Fraisier Assembly:

title: Mint Fondant - Dark Chocolate Bonbon Mint Fondant
steps: Gently heat the fondant to soften; combine with the compound, egg white powder, salt, mint, citric acid and enough water to create a smooth semi liquid paste (typically 10-15g).

Allow mixture to cool to 32˚C/90˚F and deposit the mint fondant directly into the pre- cast molds - roughly one-fourth full - either with a pastry bag or a pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface. Allow the centers to set at room temperature.
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Quick Gumpaste
steps: Add the Tylose CMC to the rolled fondant. Rub the vegetable shortening into your palms and begin kneading the paste for a full 3 to 5 minutes to help activate the chemical.

Store airtight when not in use, and well covered while in use. eat. This chemical is white in color and thus produces a snow-white paste. When added to rolled fondant it creates a strong, pliable, and flexible gumpaste that can be rolled petal-thin and can be used immediately. Some Cake Decorators choose to make their own gumpaste and will typically use another chemical, Gum Tragacanth. Gum Tragacanth is beige in color and thus produces a slightly off-white paste. Although both chemicals perform the same function, gumpaste made with Gum Tragacanth typically needs to be aged for several hours or overnight to work properly. As a little gumpaste can go a long way, particularly when making items petal thin, it is best not to make too much gumpaste in advance as it can harden and develop an unusable crust over several days. in very humid days.
notes: Note: Tylose CMC - This is a natural chemical and is found in some of the foods we
Note: Gumpaste items can be placed in a proof box with no humidity to assist in drying

title: Royal Icing
steps: In a Kitchen Aid bowl with a paddle, combine the ingredients and beat until the mixture is very white and holds its shape completely.
Keep covered with a damp towel. embroidery, cornelli lace, marbling, Swiss dots, etc.
notes: Note: Use this icing for flood work, outlining, (butterfly etc.), monograms, brush

title: Smoked Almond Financier, Goat Cheese Fondant, Red Wine Caramel, Caramelized Cherry Smoked Almond Financier
steps: Combine the flours and gently smoke in a covered container for one hour, relighting the brick every 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the butter over medium heat to a medium brown color, whisking occasionally to avoid scorching. Reserve warm.
Combine smoked flours with the 10X sugar. In a large mixing bowl, manually whip egg whites just until frothy and yellow color dissipates. Whisk in the flour mixture.
Slowly whisk in warm brown butter, ensuring complete emulsification. Allow to rest, chilled, at least one hour.

title: White Chocolate Plastiq
steps: Over a hot water bath, melt the chocolate just to the point where it is completely melted. Alternatively, melt the chocolate in a microwave. Place the chocolate in a large bowl. Make absolutely sure not to get the chocolate too hot!
Off the heat, add warmed light corn syrup by drizzling it over the white chocolate. Mix for a maximum of 25-30 strokes.
Set aside to cool and store for future use. time to begin to cool. cooled and kneaded pliable. Compound chocolate can be substituted for white chocolate in this preparation.
notes: Note: To use white chocolate plastiq the same day, it can be refrigerated for a short
Optional: Add 400-450 grams of rolled fondant to white chocolate plastiq once it is

title: ‘Black-And-White' Bonbon Chocolate Fondant
steps: Gently heat the fondant, salt and water to soften over a bain marie. Add the melted chocolate to create a smooth semi liquid paste.

Allow mixture to cool to 35˚C/95˚F and stir in the butter. Deposit the fondant directly into the pre-cast molds - roughly one-fourth full - either with a pastry bag or a pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface. Allow the centers to set at room temperature.
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Almond Pear Cake
steps: Utilizing a paddle, cream the almond paste with sugar and butter making sure there are absolutely no lumps.
Add eggs and vanilla gradually to combine. Add flour and mix just until smooth.
Place the batter in two (10”) parchment lined round cake pans.

Dry the pears. Cut the pears to fan them and place them evenly, 8 halves for each cake, rounded side up in the batter. Place the pears evenly in the batter making sure to give space in between to allow the cake to bake properly. Bake at 350 F.
Once unmolded and cooled, slice the cakes in two layers and spread the bottom layer with desired jam and reassemble. Brush only the pears with nappage and sprinkle the cake heavily with 10X.
notes: Utilize the Pain de Gene mixing method
**Important: Prepare poached pear recipe below, first, before preparing cake

title: Almond Sponge
steps: In a large bowl, combine almond flour, 10X and flour.
Meanwhile, combine cream and milk in a small saucepan. Bring just to a boil
Stir the cream and milk mixture into dry ingredients to create a thick paste.
Combine sucrose, dry egg whites, and salt. Begin whipping egg whites and slowly add the sucrose mixture. Continue to whip to a firm peak.
Fold a small amount of meringue into the sponge base to lighten. Add the lightened base to the egg whites and gently fold until thoroughly combined.

Transfer to a pastry bag fitted with a plain tip and pipe appropriate sized circles onto parchment, or spread evenly into a prepared half sheet pan. Dust surface with confectioner's sugar. Bake in a convection oven at 180˚C/350˚F for five minutes, and then reduce heat to 150°C/300°F and bake an additional ten minutes or until golden brown. Remove circles with parchment from sheet pan and allow to cool on a rack. Trim the circles slightly smaller than the base of the large hemisphere molds.

title: Angel Food Cake

ingredients.name: sugar, granulated, processed in a Robot Coupe
sugar, granulated, processed in a Robot Coupe

steps: Begin whipping the egg whites with the lemon juice, salt and vanilla extract and make an ordinary meringue with the sugar (1). Whip until soft peaks, being careful not to over whip the whites.
Combine the sugar (2) with the flour and fold in, in several additions just until incorporated.
Place in a tube pan with a removable bottom that has ONLY the bottom greased and lined with parchment paper. Do not grease the sides of the pan.
Bake at 350 F.

Remove from oven and carefully turn upside down to cool. Variations: Citrus: Add 8 grams of citrus zest when folding flour into meringue Green tea: Add 5 grams green tea powder to flour/sugar mixture when folding into meringue Cocoa: Add 9 grams of cocoa powder to flour/sugar mixture when folding into meringue

title: Basic Chocolate Cupcake
steps: With a paddle, combine the first 6 ingredients.
Add half of the combined liquid ingredients and mix until there is no butter visible. Mix for two additional minutes.
With the mixer on, add remaining liquid in a slow steady stream. Mix for an additional two minutes.
Place in muffin pans and bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the High Ratio mixing method

title: Basic Vanilla Cake
steps: With a paddle, combine the first 6 ingredients.
Add half of the combined liquid ingredients and mix until there is no butter visible. Mix for two additional minutes.
With the mixer on, add remaining liquid in a slow steady dream. Mix for an additional two minutes.
notes: Utilize the High Ratio mixing method

title: Basic White Cupcake
steps: With a paddle, combine the first 6 ingredients.
Add half of the combined liquid ingredients and mix until there is no butter visible. Mix for two additional minutes.
With the mixer on, add remaining liquid in a slow steady stream. Mix for an additional two minutes.
Place in muffin pans and bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the High Ratio mixing method

title: Basic Yellow Cake
steps: Cream the butter and sugar.
Add the eggs and vanilla gradually.
Alternate the combined flour and baking powder and the milk in several additions.
Spread on parchment lined sheet pan and bake at 350 F.
Ice as instructed utilizing flavored buttercream and jam as a striping layer if desired.
Finish with buttercream piping. Variations: Add 60 grams of colored sprinkles to batter to create a confetti cake.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method

title: Biscuit Cuillere Espresso Syrup Zabaione Filling Biscuit Cuillere
steps: Whip yolks and sugar (1) until ribboned, set aside.
Make an ordinary meringue with the whites and sugar (2).
Gently fold meringue and ribboned yolks together three quarters of the way. Complete the folding by folding in sifted AP flour.
Using a piping bag, pipe sticks of biscuit (lady fingers) next to each other onto two (1/2) sheet pans lined with parchment paper.
Dust with confectioner's sugar 2 times.
Cook at 400 degrees for 5 minutes. Be careful not to overbake.
notes: Utilize the Biscuit mixing method

title: Bourbon Pecan Pound Cake
steps: Combine the dry ingredients and butter. Beat with a paddle until well combined.
While the dry ingredients and butter are mixing, combine all the remaining batter ingredients together in a mixing bowl until well combined.
Add approximately one third of the liquid. Mix for several minutes making sure to scrape the bowl well. Repeat this process with the other thirds of the liquid mixture.
Toss the pecans with the 6g of flour and fold into the mixture.
Place in metal pans (which have been buttered or sprayed and lined with parchment) or paper loaf pans.
Bake at 325 F.
Cool the cake in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes, and then remove from the pan to finish cooling the cake before glazing.
notes: Utilize the High Ratio Mixing Method

title: Brown Butter Madeleines
steps: Brown the butter with the scraped vanilla bean and cool until room temp, but still liquid. Strain.
Whip eggs, sugar, brown sugar, and honey to heavy ribbon.
Sift the dry ingredients and fold into ribboned mixture.
Fold in butter.
Chill batter overnight.
Pipe two-thirds full into small, sprayed madeleine molds.
Bake 375F.

title: Carrot Cake Layer
steps: In a KitchenAid bowl, whisk together the first 3 ingredients.
Sift together the next 5 (dry) ingredients and add to the KitchenAid bowl. Mix until just combined.
Add the grated carrots and chopped walnuts and mix until incorporated.
Spread the batter on a half sheet pan lined with parchment and bake at 350 ºF.
Cool and wrap for use in the next lesson.
notes: Utilize the One Step mixing method.

title: Chocolate Biscuit
steps: Whip egg yolks and sugar until thick and ribbon stage. Transfer to a wide bowl and set aside.
Meanwhile, sift dry ingredients and set aside.
Whip egg whites and gradually add the sugar. Whip until stiff peak.
Fold the whipped egg whites into the ribboned yolks by first folding 1/3 of the whites to lighten the mixture before folding in the remaining whites.
Fold in the sifted dry ingredients.
Spread batter evenly onto a very flat parchment-lined half sheet pan, making sure to get enough in the corners.
Bake for approximately 10 minutes in a 375 ºF. convection oven, rotating the sheet pans half- way through baking.
notes: Utilize the Biscuit mixing method.

title: Chocolate Biscuit
steps: Thoroughly combine the almond and rice flours and reserve.
Combine the melted chocolate and softened butter, followed by the egg yolks.

Meanwhile, begin a French-style meringue with the egg whites, adding the sucrose in stages to achieve firm peaks. Fold the flours into the chocolate mixture, followed by the meringue. arranged on a Silpat-lined sheet pan. Insert the frozen ganache centers and top with an additional 15g of the base. *Freeze.

Bake the cakes from the frozen state on a double Silpat-lined sheet pan at with low or no fan, for approximately 13 minutes, turning the pan halfway during the baking process. Allow to cool slightly before unmolding.
notes: *Or alternatively, bake the cakes fresh and reheat for service.

title: Chocolate Cake
steps: Cream butter and sugar.
Gradually add liquid until incorporated. The mixture will appear broken.
Add dry ingredients.
Bake on a parchment-lined half sheet pan at 350ºF. When cool, cut out four 5 ½” discs per sheet. Reserve the remaining cake to assemble individual mousses if needed.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Mixing Method

title: Chocolate Cinnamon Chiffon Cake

ingredients.name: instant espresso powder or Trablit extract

steps: Sift the cocoa into a small mixing bowl and stir in the boiling water until. Whisk until cocoa is smooth. Set aside to cool.
Separately, combine the next 4 dry ingredients and stir well to mix.
In another bowl stir together the oil, egg yolks and vanilla. Stir the liquid ingredients and the cooled cocoa mixture into the dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
In a KitchenAid bowl, make an ordinary meringue with the egg whites, sugar and salt and whip to a soft peak.
Fold the combined dry/liquid batter into the meringue making sure to scrape the bottom of the bowl.
Place in metal pans (which have been buttered or sprayed and lined with parchment) or
Bake at 350 F.
While the cake is baking, prepare the ganache. Bring the cream, corn syrup and butter to a boil.
notes: Utilize the Chiffon mixing method

title: Chocolate Cupcakes
steps: Whisk together hot liquid and cocoa powder until smooth. Let cool.
In a kitchen aid bowl with paddle attachment, cream butter and sugar together until light and fluffy.
Add the eggs gradually, scraping the bowl often.
In a separate bowl whisk together all the dry ingredients.
Alternate the liquid and dry ingredients, mixing until the batter is well emulsified.
Portion into prepared cupcake pans, 1 rounded medium scoop of approximately 65 grams each.
Bake at 350 F.
Reserve and freeze for future lessons.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method

title: Chocolate Genoise Cake (Genoise)
steps: Place the eggs, sugar and the salt in bowl of an electric mixer, and whisk well. Place bowl over a pot of simmering water and heat while stirring constantly with hand whisk at the bottom until mixture is lukewarm and the sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat, place on mixer and whip on high speed until cool, increased in volume and very thick (ribbon stage).

While the mixture is whipping, sift the flour and cocoa powder together. Remove egg mixture from mixer and sift flour/cocoa mixture again over the egg mixture, efficiently and quickly folding with a rubber spatula making sure to scrape the bottom. Finish by folding in cooled melted butter just until incorporated. until just firm in the center.
notes: Utilize the Genoise mixing method.

title: Coconut Cake
steps: Cream butter and sugar.
Add eggs gradually.
Alternate the dry ingredients with the wet ingredients making sure to scrape thoroughly in between additions.
Add the shredded coconut and mix until just combined.
Divide the batter into 4 (6”) buttered or sprayed cake pans lined parchment.
Bake at 350º F.
Unmold, cool and wrap for the next lesson.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Mixing Method.

title: Financier

ingredients.name: almonds, sliced for sprinkling on top

steps: Combine the butter, water or rum, and the vanilla extract and set aside
Make an ordinary meringue from the whites, salt and sugar (1) and whip until soft peak.
Alternately, fold the liquid ingredients and the combined dry ingredients (with the sugar (2) into the meringue just until combined.

title: Gluten Free Chocolate Cupcakes
steps: Over a double boiler melt chocolate and then whisk in vegetable oil.
In a separate bowl, combine the cocoa powder, gluten-free flour blend, baking powder, xanthan gum, salt and baking soda.
In a KitchenAid mixing bowl with whisk attachment, whisk together the eggs, vanilla and sugar
On low speed add the cooled melted chocolate/oil and milk and mix to combine.
Gradually add the dry ingredients and mix until thoroughly combined and smooth.
Portion into cupcakes pans and bake at 325 F. in a deck oven until just set.
Reserve and freeze for future lessons.

title: Gluten-Free Vanilla Cupcakes
steps: Over a double boiler melt white chocolate and then whisk in vegetable oil.
In a separate bowl combine the gluten-free flour blend, baking powder, xanthan gum, salt and baking soda.
In a KitchenAid mixing bowl with whisk attachment, whisk together the eggs, sugar, sour cream and vanilla until well combined and pale in color.
On low speed add the cooled melted chocolate/oil mixture and mix to combine.
Gradually add the dry ingredients and mix until thoroughly combined and smooth.
Portion into cupcakes pans and bake at 325 F. in a deck oven until just set.
Reserve and freeze for future lessons.

title: Gluten-Free Vanilla Loaf Cake
steps: Combine dry ingredients in the KitchenAid bowl with a whisk.
Whisk liquids together and add to dry ingredients with the machine running on low speed.
Stop the machine and scrape the bowl. Resume mixing for an additional 4-5 minutes.
Pour batter into a sprayed, parchment-lined metal loaf pan, and bake in a preheated 325°F oven for approximately 50 minutes or until just set.
notes: Utilize the One Step mixing method.

title: High-Ratio Buttermilk Pound Cake
steps: Combine the dry ingredients and butter. Beat with a paddle until well combined.
While the dry ingredients and butter are mixing, combine all the remaining batter ingredients together in a mixing bowl until well combined.
Add approximately one-third of the liquid. Mix for several minutes making sure to scrape the bowl well. Repeat this process with the other thirds of the liquid mixture.
Place in paper loaf pans.
Bake at 325 F.
notes: Utilize the High Ratio mixing method

title: High-Ratio Fresh Ginger Pound Cake with Lemon Glaze
steps: Combine the dry ingredients and butter. Beat with the paddle until well combined.
While the dry ingredients and butter are mixing, combine all the remaining batter ingredients together in a mixing bowl until well combined.
Add approximately one third of the liquid. Mix for several minutes making sure to scrape the bowl well. Repeat this process with the other thirds of the liquid mixture.
Place in metal pans (which have been buttered or sprayed and lined with parchment) or paper loaf pans. Bake at 325 F.
Cool the cake in the pan on a rack for 10 minutes, and then remove from the pan to finish cooling the cake before glazing.
To make the glaze, combine the sugar, lemon juice, and water or milk in a saucepan and heat over a very low temperature, whisking until smooth and just lukewarm. Drizzle over cake.
notes: Optional: chopped candied ginger for top
Utilize the High Ratio Mixing Method

title: Klimt-Torte
steps: Cream the butter and sugar (1).
Beat in the yolks gradually until the mixture is very light and smooth. Add the lemon zest and vanilla extract.
Fold in the hazelnut flour and bread crumbs.
Make an ordinary meringue with the egg whites and sugar (2).
Lighten the creamed butter mixture with 1/4 of the meringue, and then fold in the remaining meringue
Unmold the cake to cool.
notes: Utilize the Modified Creaming mixing method.

title: Lemon Pound Loaf Cake
steps: In a kitchen aid bowl with a paddle, cream the butter, sugar and lemon zest.
Add eggs gradually, scraping down often. Add the milk and the combined dry ingredients alternately until just combined.
Place in paper loaf pans, filling about 2/3 full. Bake at 350 F.
While baking, whisk together 10x sugar and lemon juice.
When loaves are baked and still hot, brush the 10x sugar/lemon juice mixture very liberally on top of loaves. After 5 minutes, brush liberally again. Allow loaves to cool.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Dough mixing method:

title: Lemon Ricotta Sponge
steps: In a KitchenAid bowl, combine the sugar (1) and lemon zest. Mix for several minutes with a paddle to release the oils of the zest into the sugar.
Add the room temperature butter and cream on high until light and fluffy.
Slowly add the yolks into the creaming mixture, scraping down the bowl often.
In a separate bowl whisk together the ricotta and lemon juice and add to the creamed mixture and mix until incorporated.
Add the almond and cake flour, and mix and until incorporated. Set aside in a large bowl for folding.
Make an ordinary meringue from the whites and sugar (2) and whip to soft peak.
Stir about 1/3 of the meringue into the cake batter to lighten the batter, then gently fold in the remaining 2/3 of meringue.
Divide onto two flat half sheet pans lined with parchment paper and spread evenly.
Bake 15-18 minutes at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Modified Creaming method

title: Light or Dark Linzertorte
steps: Reserve about one-third of the Linzer dough for the lattice top and refrigerate.
Line a tart pan with the remaining Linzer dough.
Spread the raspberry preserves evenly over the dough.
Roll the reserved dough and cut into strips; arrange over the preserves in a lattice pattern.
Scatter the sliced almonds over the lattice.
Bake at 350 F until the crust is golden brown.

title: Pistachio Sponge
steps: Thoroughly combine all ingredients; pass through a fine mesh sieve.
Transfer the mixture to a one-pint foam siphon and load two gas chargers, per manufacturer's instructions.
Dispense the mixture into paper cups, into which a few regularly-spaced holes are punched.
Place in microwave and cook on high power for 30-40 seconds.

title: Plain Genoise
steps: Place the eggs, sugar and the salt in bowl of an electric mixer, and whisk well. Place bowl over a pot of simmering water and heat while stirring constantly with hand whisk until mixture is lukewarm and the sugar is dissolved. Remove from heat, place on mixer and whip on high speed until cool, increased in volume and very thick (ribbon stage).

While the mixture is whipping, sift the flour. Remove egg mixture from mixer and sift flour again over the egg mixture, efficiently and quickly folding with a rubber spatula making sure to scrape the bottom. Finish by folding in cooled melted butter just until incorporated. until just firm in the center.
notes: Utilize the Genoise mixing method

title: Red Velvet Cake
steps: Place sugar and oil in a Kitchen Aid mixer and mix utilizing the whisk attachment.
Add the eggs gradually and mix well.
Add the food coloring carefully.
Add the sifted dry ingredients, alternately with the buttermilk and vanilla until just combined.
In a separate bowl, combine the vinegar with the baking soda and quickly combine it with the rest of the batter and mix for about 10 seconds to ensure that it is well distributed.
Divide the batter evenly into 2 (6”) cake pans that have been buttered or sprayed and the bottoms lined with parchment.
Bake at 350 F.

title: Sacher Torte
steps: In KitchenAid mixer, whip egg yolks and sugar (1) until ribboned.
Sift together AP flour and baking powder, fold into the ribboned yolks.
Fold in melted butter and chocolate.
Make an ordinary meringue with the egg whites and sugar (2).
Fill the center with 200 grams of apricot or raspberry jam and spread the remaining jam on the
Place in the fridge or freezer to set while you prepare the ganache.
Prepare ganache, pour over the set cake.
Place in fridge to set ganache.
Using a chocolate cornet, pipe “Sacher” on top.

title: Salted Chocolate Cake
steps: In a KitchenAid bowl over a bain marie, heat the chocolate and water until melted and warm.
Transfer the bowl to the mixer and whisk in the next 4 (wet) ingredients.
In a separate bowl, mix together the remaining 4 (dry) ingredients.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet and mix until combined.
Place in paper loaf pans filling about 2/3 full. Sprinkle lightly with Fleur de Sal.
Bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the One Step mixing method.

title: Strawberry Upside Down Cakes
steps: Melt a portion of the butter in the bottom of the cake pans and sprinkle with a portion of the sugar to form a caramel layer.
Arrange sliced strawberries over the caramel in the pans.
Cream the remaining butter and sugar with the lemon zest and lemon extract until light.
Add the egg whites gradually, beating well after each addition.
Alternate the sifted cake flour, baking powder, and salt with the milk, adding in several additions.
Spread the batter evenly over the strawberries in the pans.
Bake at 350 F until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool briefly in the pans, then invert onto plates while still warm.

title: Torta Caprese
steps: Cream the butter and sugar.

Meanwhile, melt the chocolate over a bain marie. Remove the chocolate from the heat before it is all completely melted and allow the residual heat to melt the rest of the chocolate so the mixture does not get too hot.
Add the melted chocolate to the creamed butter and sugar. Gradually add the yolks and mix until emulsified.
Add the ground walnuts and flour and mix until just combined.
Separately, make an ordinary meringue from the whites and sugar and whip to a soft peak.
Lighten the batter with some of the meringue and then fold in the remaining meringue.

title: Tres Leches Syrup
steps: Combine all of the ingredients.
Poke holes into the cooled unmolded cakes that have been put in a larger container such as a
Cover the cake and refrigerate it overnight to allow enough time for the syrup to soak in.

title: Vanilla Biscuit
steps: Whip the egg yolks with the sugar on high speed until pale and thick, ribbon stage.
In a separate bowl, whip the egg whites, gradually adding the remaining sugar, to soft to medium peaks.
Fold one third of the meringue into the yolk mixture to lighten, then fold in the rest.
Sift the cake flour over the mixture and fold in gently until just combined.
Spread evenly onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan.
Bake at 425 F until light gold and springs back to the touch, 8 to 10 minutes.

title: Vanilla Chiffon Cake

ingredients.name: vegetable oil, such as corn or canola

steps: Combine the first 3 dry ingredients and stir well to mix.
In another bowl stir together the oil, egg yolks, water and vanilla. Add the liquid ingredients into the dry ingredients and whisk until smooth.
In a KitchenAid bowl, make an ordinary meringue with the egg whites, sugar (2) and salt and whip to a soft peak.
Fold the combined dry/liquid batter into the meringue making sure to scrape the bottom of the bowl.
Place in metal pans (which have been buttered or sprayed and lined with parchment) or paper loaf pans.
Bake at 350 F. for about 55 to 60 minutes.
notes: Utilize the Chiffon mixing method

title: Vanilla Cupcakes
steps: Combine egg yolks, first measurement of milk and vanilla. Set aside.
In a KitchenAid bowl with paddle attachment, combine the cake flour, sugar, baking powder and salt.
Add the softened butter and the second measurement of milk into the dry ingredients. Mix on low speed until combined.
Add the egg yolk mixture in 3 additions, making sure to mix on high speed for 1 minute in between each addition to develop structure.
Portion into prepared cupcake pans: 1 level large scoop or 50 grams each.
Bake 15-20 minutes at 350 F.
Reserve and freeze for future lessons.
notes: Utilize the High Ratio mixing method

title: Walnut Chiffon Cake
steps: Combine the first 4 dry ingredients and stir well to mix.
In another bowl stir together the oil, egg yolks, water and vanilla. Add the liquid ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
In a KitchenAid bowl, make an ordinary meringue with the egg whites, sugar and salt and whip to a soft peak.
Fold the combined dry/liquid batter into the meringue making sure you scrape the bottom of the bowl.
Fold in the walnut pieces.
Place in metal loaf pans (which have been buttered or sprayed and lined with parchment) or paper loaf pans.
Bake at 350 F. Cool and drizzle with Brown Sugar Glaze
notes: Utilize the Chiffon mixing method

title: Yellow Buttermilk Cake
steps: Cream the butter and the sugar.
Combine the dry ingredients.
Combine the liquid ingredients.
Alternately add the liquid and the dry ingredients to the creamed butter and sugar.
Portion into the prepared pans lined with parchment two thirds full.
Bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Mixing Method

title: Crumb Mixture for Cheesecake
steps: Mix the ingredients to combine well.
Use this crumb mixture for the New York and Sour Cream Cheesecake recipes.

title: Mascarpone Cheesecakes
steps: Butter and line 16 aluminum mise en place cups with a disk of buttered parchment on the bottom.
Beat room temperature cream cheese and sugar until smooth. Do not over mix. Gently beat in mascarpone. Add eggs and vanilla until incorporated making sure to scrape down to the bottom.
Fill aluminum cups with batter. Place in bain marie. Bake at 325° F for approximately 30 minutes or until set.

While batter is baking, roll out Pate Sable dough on the following page and cut out to the size of the base of the cheesecakes. Bake until very lightly golden on the edges. Reserve until the cheesecakes are ready to be unmolded and can be used for the base when unmolded.
Chill cheesecakes overnight before unmolding.

title: New York Cheesecake
steps: Press the crumb mixture into the pan(s).
Beat the cream cheese on low speed until smooth and free of lumps.
Add the sugar and beat until combined, scraping down the bowl.
Add the vanilla extract and lemon juice; mix until smooth.
Add the eggs gradually on low speed, mixing just until incorporated; do not overmix or incorporate excess air.
Pour the batter over the crumb crust in the pans.
Bake in a water bath at 325 F until the edges are set and the center still wobbles slightly.
Cool gradually at room temperature, then refrigerate until fully chilled before unmolding.

title: Pate Sablee Crust for Mascarpone Cheesecake Bases
steps: Cream butter, salt, confectioners' sugar, and vanilla in mixer with paddle and mix until well combined and smooth.
Beat in yolks, until incorporated.
Add all the flour, mixing to combine.
Remove bowl from mixer and empty dough onto floured work surface and roll to cut out bases.
Bake at 350 until just golden around the edges.

title: Sour Cream Cheesecake
steps: Press the crumb mixture into the pans.
Beat the cream cheese on low speed until smooth.
Add the sugar and beat until combined.
Add the sour cream and vanilla extract; mix until smooth.
Add the eggs and yolks gradually on low speed, mixing just until incorporated.
Pour the batter over the crumb crust in the pans.
Bake in a water bath at 325 F until set around the edges but still slightly wobbly in the center.
Cool gradually at room temperature, then chill thoroughly before unmolding.

title: Tarte Au Fromage Blanc
steps: Line the tart pan with the rolled Pasta Frolla.
Soak the raisins in warm water to plump, then drain well.
Beat the farmer cheese until smooth.
Add the sugar and beat until combined.
Add the eggs, yolks, and vanilla extract; mix until smooth.
Fold in the raisins.
Pour the filling into the lined shell.
Bake at 350 F until set and lightly golden.

title: Torta Di Ricotta
steps: Line the tart pan with the rolled Pasta Frolla.
Beat the ricotta until smooth.
Add the sugar and beat until combined.
Add the eggs and yolk gradually, mixing until smooth.
Add the vanilla extract, anisette, and ground cinnamon; mix to combine.
Pour the filling into the lined shell.
Bake at 350 F until set and golden.

title: Armagnac Truffles

ingredients.name: dark chocolate, very finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the chocolate and invert sugar in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter and Armagnac. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.

Transfer the crystallized ganache to a pastry bag fitted with a large, plain tip (# 804 or 806) and pipe uniform mounds onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan; the individual mounds should weigh approximately 10-15g each and piped into evenly spaced in rows.
Briefly chill the pan of piped ganache until slightly firm. Quickly roll each mound into a spherical shape if necessary and arrange on a fresh parchment-lined half sheet pan.
notes: For enrobing truffles in the next lesson:

title: Banana Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80 pc 530 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and vanilla bean in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the invert sugar and the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and discard the vanilla bean; gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the banana, butter, and rum. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered white or milk chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.
Using a lined half sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Biscuit Jaconde Coffee Syrup Coffee Buttercream Chocolate Ganache Biscuit Jaconde
steps: Combine the 10X, almond flour, and the AP flour in a KitchenAid bowl with the paddle attachment. Mix just to combine.

While mixing, add the eggs gradually, occasionally scraping down the sides of the bowl with a rubber spatula. It is important that before adding more eggs the mixture is beaten for a couple of minutes in between additions until it is completely emulsified. After all of the eggs have been added, beat for several minutes longer to ensure that as much air as possible has been incorporated. Transfer to a large clean bowl.
In a clean KitchenAid bowl, make a French meringue with the whites and sugar. Whip to soft peak being very careful not to over whip.
Fold the meringue quickly and gently into the egg mixture. When the mixture is about 9/10ths folded, pour in the cooled melted butter as you complete the folding.
notes: Utilize the Biscuit mixing method

title: Caramel Truffles
steps: Place the sucrose in a large saucepan and dry-cook to medium dark caramel. Remove from heat and deglaze with water, and then add the cream. Bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the chocolate and glucose syrup in a large bowl. Remove the cream mixture from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.

Transfer the crystallized ganache to a pastry bag fitted with a large, plain tip (# 804 or 806) and pipe uniform mounds onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan; the individual mounds should weigh approximately 10-15g each and piped into evenly spaced in rows.
Briefly chill the pan of piped ganache until slightly firm. Quickly roll each mound into a spherical shape if necessary and arrange on a fresh parchment-lined half sheet pan.
notes: For enrobing truffles in the next lesson:

title: Chocolate Clusters

ingredients.name: lightly toasted nuts, cooled, or other

steps: In a bowl, combine inclusions, and stirring with a rubber spatula, quickly add tempered chocolate silpat lined sheet pan and allow to set.
As a variation, deposit the clusters directly into paper cases.

title: Chocolate Ganache
steps: Make ganache and allow to cool for use as a filling.

title: Cinnamon Pistachio Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80 pc 561 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and cinnamon in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the invert sugar, pistachio paste and the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release. Take care to avoid damage due to excessive force or prolonged refrigeration when releasing the chocolates
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Dark Chocolate Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc 540 grams

ingredients.name: dark chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the invert sugar and the dark chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds on top of the mint fondant, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered dark chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half-sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release.

title: Dark Chocolate Spray
steps: Combine the melted cocoa butter and dark chocolate and transfer to a chocolate spray gun, according to manufacturer's instructions. Remove the unmolded gianduja parfait from the freezer and apply a light coat of the spray. Reserve for assembly.

title: Ganache Center
steps: Combine the cream, sucrose, salt and invert sugar and bring to a boil. Remove from heat and gradually incorporate into the chocolate and homogenize with an immersion blender.
Transfer the ganache center to mini silicon dome forms and freeze.
When frozen, place two half spheres together for use.

title: Ganache Filling
steps: Combine the combine cream and glucose in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil.
Place the melted chocolate in a large bowl and gradually pour hot cream over it in small additions, stirring until smooth.
Allow to cool to 35˚C/95ºF and add the butter.
Arrange tart shells on a clean baking sheet and line the bottom of each shell with crushed peanuts, about half full; reserve additional peanuts for final presentation.

Warming, if necessary, spoon the caramel filling into each shell, just to cover the peanuts. The shells at this stage should only be half to two thirds full, allowing ample space for the ganache layer. Reserve any leftover caramel in a plastic squeeze bottle.

Briefly freeze the partially filled tarts for up to 30 minutes to set the caramel. Fill the remainder of each tart with the chocolate ganache, warming if necessary. Allow tarts to stand an additional 30 minutes to one hour, at room temperature, to fully set the ganache.

title: Ganache Filling for Bon Bons

ingredients.name: semisweet chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and milk in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the light corn syrup and the semisweet and milk chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream/milk from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

title: Ganache Glaze
steps: Heat cream to a rolling boil. Pour over chocolate.

Let sit for 3 minutes then whisk together to form ganache. Finished Doughnuts • Boston Cream - Filled with pastry cream and topped with chocolate ganache • Zeppole - Tossed in confectioners sugar or cinnamon sugar • Lemon - Covered in lemon sugar glaze and white sprinkles or rainbow. Or dipped in very light loose fondant and sprinkled with toasted shredded coconut • Cinnamon and Coffee - Instant coffee mixed with white fondant and white sprinkles or dipped in chocolate ganache glaze (Boston cream), (Lemon) and (Cinnamon Coffee)
notes: Notes:

title: German Chocolate Filling Fffff Fill Fil Cake (Genoise)
steps: Combine the heavy cream, butter, sugar (1) and salt in a sauce pan and bring to a boil. Over medium heat, simmer for 15 minutes to reduce and thicken the mixture.
After the 15 minutes, stir in the pecans and coconut.
In a bowl, whisk together sugar (2), yolks and vanilla.
Temper the hot cream mixture into the yolk mixture. Return everything to the sauce pan and simmer for 3 minutes.
Refrigerate the mixture for use.

title: Gianduja Parfait
steps: Combine the water, nonfat dry milk, egg yolk and glucose in a small saucepan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until mixture begins to thicken slightly. Remove from heat.
Transfer this mixture to the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with the whip attachment. Whip the mixture on high speed until cool.
Fold in the dissolved gelatin, followed by the melted chocolates.
Fold in the whipped cream.
Transfer the mixture to pastry bags and dispense into the silicon forms. Apply a previously baked pâte sucrée base onto each, and freeze.

title: Gianduja Parfait Vanilla Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk and vanilla in a sauce pot. Whisk in the dry milk to rehydrate and add the first measurement of sucrose (1) and glucose. Bring to a boil.
Meanwhile, combine sucrose (2) and stabilizer. Whisk into egg yolks.
Temper hot milk into yolk mixture. Return to low heat and cook, stirring, to

title: Ginger Truffles

ingredients.name: milk chocolate, very finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the ginger in a saucepan and cover with cold water; bring to a boil and drain.
Combine the blanched ginger and cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Remove from heat, cover, and allow to infuse for 15 minutes.

Meanwhile, place the chocolate and invert sugar in a large bowl. Strain the cream and return to a boil. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter and ginger liqueur. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.
Transfer the crystallized ganache to a pastry bag fitted with a large, plain tip (#804 or 806) and pipe uniform mounds onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan;
Briefly chill the pan of piped ganache until slightly firm. Quickly roll each mound
To enrobe, coat each truffle with a thin layer of tempered dark chocolate, allow to
notes: For enrobing truffles in the next lesson:

title: Green Tea Truffles

ingredients.name: dark chocolate, very finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and matcha in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the chocolate and invert sugar in a large bowl. Remove the cream mixture from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.

Transfer the crystallized ganache to a pastry bag fitted with a large, plain tip (#804 or 806) and pipe uniform mounds onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan; the individual mounds should weigh approximately 10-15g each and piped into evenly spaced in rows.
Briefly chill the pan of piped ganache until slightly firm. Quickly roll each mound into a spherical shape if necessary and arrange on a fresh parchment-lined half sheet pan.

notes: For enrobing the truffles in the next lesson:

title: Hazelnut Praline Bar
steps: Add the melted cocoa butter and chocolate to the praline paste. Heat the whole mixture to 45°C/113°F.
Pour mixture onto a marble, working briefly with an offset spatula to cool to 30°C/ 86°F.
Transfer mixture to a plastic lined 12cm frame and allow to crystallize overnight before enrobing.

title: Honey Lavender Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc 510 grams

ingredients.name: dark chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and lavender in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Remove from heat, cover, and allow to infuse for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused cream and add water to bring the amount of liquid back up to 145g and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the honey and the dark chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the infused cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter and orange flower water. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional dark chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.
Using a lined half sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Lemon - Praline Bonbon Praline Cream
steps: Bloom gelatin in water and reserve.
Place the cream in a sauce pan and heat to 50ºC/120ºF. Remove from heat and add the gelatin, stirring to dissolve.

Place the praline paste in a mixer bowl fitted with paddle attachment; slowly incorporate cream while beating on low speed, scraping the sides of the bowl after each addition. The mixture may ‘break', but will properly emulsify with the addition of all the cream.

Allow to cool to 35˚C/95˚F and deposit the praline cream directly into the pre-cast molds - roughly one-third full - either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface. Allow the centers to set slightly at room temperature. Excess praline cream should be stored under refrigeration.
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Lemon Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc. 485 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream, lemon zest, and vanilla bean in a saucepan and bring to a boil and allow to steep for 5 minutes.

Meanwhile, place the invert sugar and the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat, strain, and discard the vanilla bean; gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the lemon juice and butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds on top of the praline cream, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered white chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half-sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release.

Meanwhile, place the chocolate and invert sugar in a large bowl. Remove the cream mixture from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter and bourbon. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.

notes: For enrobing the truffles in the next lesson:

title: Mendiant
steps: Line half sheet pan with parchment paper and stencil guideline or use Silpat.
Transfer 200g tempered chocolate to a cornet or disposable pastry bag and deposit onto disc template, approximately 5g per piece.
Working quickly, affix one piece each hazelnut, pistachio, dried cherry, and orange peel to each disc before it sets.
Allow mendiants to set completely before removal and wrapping.

title: Milk Chocolate Filling for Bon
steps: Place the cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the light corn syrup and milk chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc 550 grams

Meanwhile, place the glucose and the milk chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered milk chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

title: Muscadine

ingredients.name: milk chocolate, very finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter, Grand Marnier, and kirshwasser. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.
Transfer the crystallized ganache to a pastry bag fitted with a large, plain tip (# 804 or 806) and pipe straight, uniform cylinders onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan.
Briefly chill the pan of piped ganache until slightly firm. Cut the cylinder into individual 4cm lengths (approximately 10g each) and arrange on a fresh parchment-lined half sheet pan.

title: Ordinary Ganache
steps: Cut chocolate finely.
Bring cream to a boil over medium heat; remove from heat.
Add chocolate and set aside 5 minutes.
Whisk until smooth; strain into a clean, dry bowl.
Cool to room temperature or cool quickly over cold water.
Cool to room temperature and use for glazing cakes or pastries.

title: Passion Fruit Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc 505 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and passion fruit purée in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the glucose and the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream mixture from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter and liqueur. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered dark chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release. Take care to avoid damage due to excessive force or prolonged refrigeration when releasing the chocolates.
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Pistachio Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80 pc 560 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the invert sugar, pistachio paste and the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds on top of the mandarin gelée, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered dark chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release. Take care to avoid damage due to excessive force or prolonged refrigeration when releasing the chocolates.

title: Praline Cream
steps: Place the cream in a sauce pan and heat to 49˚C/120˚F. Remove from heat and add the bloomed gelatin.
