Place the praline paste in mixer bowl fitted with paddle attachment; slowly incorporate cream. Mixture may ‘break', but will emulsify with the addition of all the cream.
Transfer to a squeeze bottle and chill.

title: Rich Ganache
steps: Cut chocolate finely.
Bring cream to a boil over medium heat; remove from heat.
Add chocolate and set aside 5 minutes.
Whisk until smooth; strain into a clean, dry bowl.
Cool to room temperature or cool quickly over cold water.
Once the ganache has set, beat with a paddle on medium speed briefly to lighten.

title: Salted Caramel - Milk Chocolate Bonbon Salted Caramel Cream
steps: Into a heavy sauce pan, combine sucrose, water, glucose, and lemon juice. Cook to a medium amber caramel. Meanwhile, gently heat the cream until warm.
Remove caramelized sugar from heat and deglaze with the cream. Return to low heat to completely dissolve sugar. Continue to cook until the mixture reaches
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Star Anise Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc 550 grams

ingredients.name: milk chocolate, finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and star anise in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Remove from heat, cover and allow to infuse for 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, place the glucose and the milk chocolate in a large bowl. Strain the cream and return to a boil. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered dark or milk chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release. Take care to avoid damage due to excessive force or prolonged refrigeration when releasing the chocolates.
notes: For the pre-cast shells:

title: Strawberry Truffles

ingredients.name: white chocolate, very finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the strawberry purée, sorbitol, glucose, and invert sugar in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the strawberry mixture from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter.
Transfer the ganache to a pastry bag or sauce funnel and immediately deposit the ganache into the hollow shells, allowing a small gap for sealing; allow to crystallize at room temperature.
Using a cornet or a pastry bag, apply a small amount of tempered white chocolate to the hollow shells to adequately seal. Allow to crystallize.
To enrobe, coat each truffle with a thin layer of tempered white chocolate, and
Allow the truffles to set and garnish with a dusting of freeze-dried strawberry
notes: For enrobing the truffles in the next lesson:

title: Sucettes

ingredients.name: dark, milk, or white chocolate, tempered

steps: Line marble with parchment, acetate or silpat and arrange 8 lollipop sticks.
Transfer tempered chocolate to a cornet and pipe abstract, but tight concentric circles or similar design over the end of each stick.
Working quickly, garnish each sucette with desired inclusions.
Allow the fragile sucettes to set completely before removal from the work surface.
To remove, once set, pull the parchment, acetate or silpat away from the lollipops to avoid breakage.

title: Traditional Chocolate
steps: Gently heat cocoa nibs in a large sauté pan for 3-4 minutes. The cocoa nibs must be very warm.
Transfer nibs to a metate, mortar and pestle, or food processor. Begin to grind the beans.
Once a coarse powder has developed, add the sugar, vanilla, and cinnamon. Continue to grind the mixture to a sticky paste.
Portion and roll the cocoa paste into approximately 40 25g balls. Allow several hours or overnight for the cocoa paste to dry and set.
To make hot chocolate, dissolve two balls of cocoa paste into 250g hot water or milk and forth using a traditional molinillo or immersion blender.

title: Truffle Ganache
steps: Cut chocolate finely.
Bring cream to a boil over medium heat; remove from heat.
Add chocolate and set aside 5 minutes.
Whisk until smooth; strain into a clean, dry bowl.
Cool to room temperature or cool quickly over cold water.
Add any flavorings
Once the ganache has set, beat with a paddle on medium speed briefly lighten. Variation: Ganache Praline - Beat 170 grams praline paste into ganache.

title: White Chocolate Filling for Bon
steps: Place the cream in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the light corn syrup and white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

title: White Chocolate Ganache
yield: Total Yield: approx. 2 molds or 80pc. 470 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and vanilla in a saucepan and bring to a boil.

Meanwhile, place the invert sugar and the white chocolate in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat, strain, and discard the vanilla bean; gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.

Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter, then rum. Deposit the ganache directly into the pre-cast molds on top of the chocolate fondant, either with a pastry bag or pastry funnel with a minimum of air pockets and a flat surface, with no portion exceeding the top of the cavity. Allow a small gap to provide a sealing base and allow the centers to crystallize at room temperature.

When the centers have fully crystallized, seal the mold with additional tempered chocolate: fill the remaining space of each cavity and scrape away the excess to produce a clean, smooth base and allow to crystallize undisturbed.

Using a lined half-sheet pan, carefully invert the mold to release the finished bonbons; this may require a slight tap or in some cases, the molds may require a brief chill in order achieve full contraction and release.

title: White Chocolate Spray
steps: Combine the melted cocoa butter and white chocolate, strain through a fine strainer and transfer to a chocolate spray gun. Remove the yuzu parfait from the freezer and apply a light coat of the spray. Reserve for assembly.

Assemble each parfait onto the sucrée base and allow to temper. Place each onto a plate and garnish with the sorbet, sponge (dusted with 10X sugar), ground pistachio, and décor. Finish with the mandarin coulis.

title: White/ Milk Chocolate Spray
steps: Melt the desired chocolate and the cocoa butter separately over bain marie to approximately 95 F.
Combine the chocolate and cocoa butter, being careful that no water gets into the mixture.
Strain through a fine strainer and store or place in a spray gun for use. Keep mixture/spray gun at approximately 95F for optimal use.
Test spray before spraying on final piece and spray at a distance to ensure an even coat. for at least 10-15 minutes before spraying.
notes: Note: A velvet like texture can be achieved by placing the finished piece in the freezer

title: ‘Quatre Epice' Truffles
yield: Total Yield: approx. 100 g pieces 1,000 grams

ingredients.name: white chocolate, very finely chopped or slightly melted

steps: Place the cream and spice blend in a saucepan and bring to a boil

Meanwhile, place the chocolate and honey in a large bowl. Remove the cream from heat and gradually add to the chocolate in three additions; with each addition, stir the mixture with a rubber spatula in tight circles, working from the inside of the mass toward the outside, until each addition is incorporated. The resulting ganache should be smooth, supple, and slightly elastic with glossy shine.
Allow the ganache to cool to 35˚C/95ºF. Stir in the butter and rum. Transfer the ganache to a shallow third pan and allow to crystallize at room temperature.

Transfer the crystallized ganache to a pastry bag fitted with a large, plain tip (# 804 or 806) and pipe uniform mounds onto a parchment-lined half sheet pan; the individual mounds should weigh approximately 10-15g each and piped into evenly spaced in rows.
Briefly chill the pan of piped ganache until slightly firm. Quickly roll each mound into a spherical shape if necessary and arrange on a fresh parchment-lined half sheet pan.
notes: For enrobing truffles in the next lesson:

title: Apricot Pate De Fruit
steps: Place the apricot purée in a medium, heavy saucepan, and heat to104˚F.
Combine the first measurement of sugar (1) and the pectin and whisk into the purée. Bring to a boil while stirring continually.
Add the remaining sugar (2) and glucose syrup in three additions. Continue stirring the mixture and cook to 223˚F. while on low heat.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in citric acid, previously dissolved in the water.
Immediately pour the mixture into a plastic-lined frame or silicon forms and allow to set at room temperature.
To finish the pate de fruit, cut or unmold as necessary and toss the individual pieces in sucrose*. weight of the sucrose used for coating.
notes: *To add acidity to the coating of the finished pieces, combine 2% citric acid to the

title: Black Currant Pate De Fruit
steps: Place the black currant and pear purées in a medium, heavy saucepan, and heat to 104˚F.
Combine the first measurement of sugar (1) and the pectin and whisk into the purées. Bring to a boil while stirring continually.
Add the remaining sugar (2), glucose syrup and invert sugar in three additions. Continue stirring the mixture and cook to 223˚F. while on low heat.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in citric acid, previously dissolved in the liqueur.
Immediately pour the mixture into a plastic-lined frame or silicon forms and allow to set at room temperature.
To finish the pate de fruit, cut or unmold as necessary and toss the individual pieces in sugar*. weight of the sugar used for coating.
notes: * To add acidity to the coating of the finished pieces, combine 2% citric acid to the

title: Butter Crunch
steps: Melt the butter in a saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in sucrose, glucose, and water. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 150˚C/300˚F. Remove from heat, stir in almonds, and pour out on a half sheet pan lined with silpat and spread evenly with an offset spatula coated with pam if needed.

For the coating, melt and temper the chocolate. Spread or brush on one side of butter crunch and sprinkle with half the chopped almonds. Allow it to set, and repeat coating on other side. After both coatings have set, break into coarse piece

title: Chocolate Walnut Fudge
steps: In a small saucepan, combine the cocoa powder, sugar and milk and mix until very well combined. Add water, chocolate, butter and corn syrup. Cook on medium heat stirring occasionally until the sugar has completely dissolved. Bring mixture to a simmer using the lowest heat setting possible that will maintain the simmer. Make sure not to stir any further. Occasionally, wash down sides of pan with water. Cook to 238° F.

Remove pan from heat and pour fudge onto marble that has been sprinkled with a little bit of water. Let stand. When the fudge can be touched in the center without sticking to your finger, add the vanilla gradually and stir the fudge with bench scrapers until the fudge begins to lose its sheen and starts to maintain its shape. The mixing can take approximately 15-20 minutes.

When the fudge I ready, quickly, stir in the nuts and even quicker, place the fudge in paper on the bottom and up two sides past the top so the fudge can be removed easily once it sets. Make sure to smooth the top of the fudge with a spatula.

title: Citrus Caramel
steps: Combine sucrose and water to moisten in a saucepan. Cook to a very light amber color.

Remove from heat, and slowly add mandarin and lemon juices. Return to heat and cook to dissolve any hardened bits of sugar. Continue to cook to 105˚C/221˚F. Allow to cool and transfer to a squeeze bottle.

title: Coconut Caramel
steps: Combine the first 4 ingredients in a large pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium low; simmer for about 30 minutes (or longer if necessary), stirring occasionally until mixture becomes caramel in color.
Stir in rum and water. If caramel thickens too much upon cooling, thin with more coconut milk.

title: Lollipops
steps: In a medium saucepan combine the corn syrup, water and sugar. Place over medium heat and stir occasionally until it comes to a boil. If necessary, wash down the sides of the pan with a clean pastry brush. Once the mixture comes to a boil, cook without stirring until the mixture reaches 300° F
When the mixture reaches 300° F, immediately remove it from the heat and add vanilla and additional flavoring and color.

Pour small circles onto a Silpat sheet and immediately press a stick into each lollipop or pour into supplied lollipop molds. Flavor options  Vanilla: add additional seeds of 1 vanilla bean when the mixture is removed from the heat.  Lemon: add 4 grams of lemon oil, 1.25 grams of citric acid and yellow food color if desired.  Herb or herb flowers: place herbs such as lilac or mint inside the molds before pouring or flowers such as basil flowers onto the lollipops after they have been poured.

title: Lychee Pate De Fruit
steps: Place the lychee purée in a large, heavy saucepan, and heat to 104˚F.
Combine the first measurement of sugar (1) and the pectin and whisk into the purée. Bring to a boil while stirring continually.
Add the remaining sugar (2), and glucose in three additions. Continue stirring the mixture and cook to 223˚F. at low heat to make sure the mixture is sufficiently reduced.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in citric acid, previously dissolved in the water.
Immediately pour the mixture into a Silpat-lined frame or silicon forms and allow to set at room temperature.
To finish the pate de fruit, cut or unmold as necessary and toss the individual pieces in sugar. weight of the sugar used for coating.
notes: *To add acidity to the coating of the finished pieces, combine 2% citric acid to the

title: Marshmallow
steps: Place the first measurement of water (1) in a Kitchen Aid bowl; sprinkle the gelatin over the water and let stand about 5 minutes to bloom and soften.
Separately, combine the sugar, corn syrup, and water (2) in a saucepan and begin to heat to 230˚F.
Meanwhile, add the second measurement of corn syrup to the already bloomed gelatin.

Once the cooked sugar mixture reaches 230˚F, pour the mixture into the Kitchen Aid bowl and whip at medium speed until lightened or to a temperature of 86˚F, about 5-7 minutes. Immediately transfer the mixture to a sprayed and plastic-lined quarter sheet pan. Make sure to also spray the plastic wrap on the surface. Gently smooth the surface of the marshmallows and cover with sprayed plastic wrap. Set for 1 hour at room temperature.

Remove the marshmallow slab from the pan by inverting the pan onto a cutting board dusted with dextrose or a mixture of cornstarch and 10X. Cut the slab into 3cm/1in squares and toss in additional dextrose or 10X mixture. Store in a sealed container.
Vanilla: Add the seeds of one split and scraped vanilla bean to mixture before whipping.
Coffee: Add 6g instant espresso to the mixture before whipping.
Lemon: Instead of 85 grams of the first measurement of water, replace it with 90g of lemon juice for blooming the gelatin; add yellow food coloring as needed.
notes: Optional flavorings:

title: New Orleans Pralines
steps: Combine all ingredients and cook to the soft ball stage (240 F), making sure not to stir once the mixture begins to boil.
Remove from heat and stir until mixture begins to thicken and turns cloudy. Quickly, spoon out on parchment paper and allow to cool.

title: Nougatine

ingredients.name: sliced almonds, slightly toasted or warmed

steps: Combine the sugar and lemon juice in a saucepan and stir until thoroughly mixed - the resulting mixture should have an appearance like wet sand.

Place the pan over medium heat and make a dry caramel. When the sugar is caramelized to a light amber color, while still on the heat, immediately stir in the almonds to coat with the caramel completely.

Immediately pour the nougatine onto a lightly oiled marble surface. Press it flat rapidly with an oiled spatula and roll it into a thin sheet, using an oiled rolling pin. (Do not oil the ends of the rolling pin where your hands are going to be placed).

title: Nougatine
steps: Combine sucrose and lemon juice in a saucepan and stir until thoroughly mixed. The resulting mixture should have an appearance like wet sand.

Place the pan over medium heat and make a dry caramel. When the sucrose is caramelized to a light amber color, while still on the heat, immediately stir in the almonds to coat with the caramel completely.

Immediately pour the nougatine onto an oiled marble surface. Press it flat with an oiled spatula and roll it into a thin sheet, using an oiled rolling pin. (Do not oil the ends of the rolling pin where your hands are going to be placed)

Cut out desired forms and rapidly shape as needed, or alternatively, shape the nougatine into a bowl for display. Allow nougatine to cool and harden. together. Scraps of nougatine can be further ground and used to add texture and flavor to other confections.
notes: For complex constructions of several pieces, prepare more caramel to adhere pieces

title: Orange Salt Water Taffy
steps: Mix the sugar and cornstarch together in a small pan. Stir in the light corn syrup, butter, water and salt. Cook over medium heat, without stirring to 250 F. while occasionally washing down the sides of the pan with water.
When the mixture reaches 250 F. remove the pot from the heat and stir in the vanilla and orange oil. Pour the mixture onto a very slightly sprayed marble.
After a minute, utilizing a sprayed bench scraper or spatula bring the outside of the taffy in towards the center continuously to cool the taffy evenly.
When the taffy is cool enough to handle, pull and fold the taffy continuously to aerate and cool.
Once shiny and cool enough to hold its shape, roll into thin or twisted cylinders and cut with a sprayed scissor into desired size pieces. Wrap in candy wrappers for storage.

title: Passion Fruit Caramel
steps: Combine sucrose and water to moisten in a saucepan. Cook to a very light amber color.

Remove from heat, and slowly add the passion fruit purée. Return to heat and cook to dissolve any hardened bits of sugar. Continue to cook to 105˚C/221˚F. Allow to cool and transfer to a squeeze bottle.

title: Peanut Brittle
steps: Place sugar, corn syrup, water, butter and salt in saucepan, mix to combine.
Over medium heat bring syrup to a boil. Wash sides of pan with a pastry brush dipped in water.
Insert candy thermometer and cook without stirring.
When temperature reaches 300° F, remove from heat.
Stir in vanilla and peanuts, and allow to stand for about 15 to 20 seconds.
Stir in baking soda.
Pour onto silpat lined sheet pan and spread with a sprayed off-set spatula as thin as possible.
After cooled, break into pieces.
notes: Note: Baking soda is added to aerate the brittle and makes it a delicate candy

title: Pear Pate De Fruit
steps: Place the pear purée in a medium, heavy saucepan, and heat to 104˚F.
Combine the first measurement of sugar (1) and the pectin and whisk into the purée. Bring to a boil while stirring continually.
Add the remaining sugar (2) and glucose syrup in three additions. Continue stirring the mixture and cook to 223˚F. while on low heat.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in citric acid, previously dissolved in the liqueur.
Immediately pour the mixture into a plastic-lined frame or silicon forms and allow to set at room temperature.
To finish the pate de fruit, cut or unmold as necessary and toss the individual pieces in sugar*. weight of the sugar used for coating.
notes: *To add acidity to the coating of the finished pieces, combine 2% citric acid to the

title: Pecan Caramel
steps: Bloom powdered gelatin with the 25 grams of water.
Caramelize sugar (1) and stir in warm pecans.
Cool on a silpat, then robot coupe to make a praline paste.
Combine sugar (2) and heavy cream in a small sauce pan. Cook until 218
Remove from heat and stir in praline paste and bloomed gelatin.
Allow to cool to 100 F. Add butter and blend with an immersion blender until smooth.
Spread onto dacquoise and refrigerate.

title: Raspberry Soft Caramel
steps: In a heavy saucepan, combine the cream, glucose, invert sugar, salt, raspberry purée, and sucrose; gently heat to a simmer to dissolve the sugars.
Increase the heat and cook, stirring, to 119°C/246°F.

Remove from heat and quickly stir in the cocoa butter and citric acid. Pour the caramel into a 12cm frame lined with plastic film; cool several hours or overnight, before cutting and wrapping, or enrobing. The purée may be substituted for a variety of other flavors for endless variations, when available, including apricot, black currant, peach, etc.

title: Red Wine Caramel
steps: Combine sucrose and water to moisten in a saucepan. Cook to a light amber color.
Remove from heat, and slowly add wine. Return to heat and cook to dissolve any hardened bits of sugar. Continue to cook to 105˚C/221˚F. Allow to cool and transfer to a squeeze bottle.

title: Red Wine Caramel
steps: Combine sucrose and water to moisten in a saucepan. Cook to very light caramel, just until the sugar begins to turn color.
Remove from heat, and slowly add the red wine. Return to heat and cook to dissolve any hardened bits of sugar. Continue to cook to 105˚C/221˚F. Allow to cool and transfer to a squeeze bottle.

title: Roasted Pears and Dried Figs in Caramel

ingredients.name: dried figs, soaked, stemmed and halved

steps: Place all ingredients in roasting pan and toss to mix.
Roast at 500 degrees for about 30 minutes.
Toss regularly during baking to ensure even caramelization.
Serve, garnished with pear chips.

title: Salted Cashew Brittle
steps: Combine the first 4 ingredients in a medium sized pot.
Over medium heat, cook the combined ingredients to 295 F. Do not stir once the mixture comes to a boil.
At 295 F. remove the mixture from the heat and quickly stir in the cashews.
Pour the entire mixture onto a silpat sheet or on a sheet pan lined with parchment that has been sprayed.

title: Strawberry Pate De Fruit
steps: Place the strawberry purée in a medium, heavy saucepan, and heat to 104˚F.
Combine the first measurement of sugar (1) and the pectin and whisk into the purée. Bring to a boil while stirring continually.
Add the remaining sugar (2) and glucose in three additions. Continue stirring the mixture and cook to 223˚F. while on low heat.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in citric acid, previously dissolved in the water.
Immediately pour the mixture into a plastic-lined frame or silicon forms and allow to set at room temperature.
To finish the pate de fruit, cut or unmold as necessary and toss the individual pieces in sugar*. weight of the sugar used for coating.
notes: *To add acidity to the coating of the finished pieces, combine 2% citric acid to the

title: Tart Tatin Caramel
steps: Place sugar and water in a small pot. Place on heat to cook and caramelize.
Combine the butter with the warm water and set aside.
When sugar/water mixture begins to caramelize and turns golden, add butter/water mixture to stop the caramel from darkening. Combine until bottom of the pan is covered.

title: Torrone
steps: Butter and line quarter sheet pan with parchment, allowing parchment to cover sides and bottom. Place one sheet of edible wafer paper on top of parchment and set aside.
Place water, sugar, and honey in a sauce pan, mix to combine.
Place on medium heat and bring to a boil. Insert candy thermometer and cook without stirring.
When syrup reaches the soft ball stage, begin whipping the egg whites until soft peaks.
Continue cooking syrup until it reaches 286°F.
Remove from heat. While mixing, gradually pour syrup down side of bowl.
Whip until stiff peaks. While meringue is still very warm quickly fold in vanilla and warm nuts.

Press into prepared pan and place sheet of edible wafer paper on top. Press to flatten. If Torrone is firm, you can remove it from the pan and flatten with a rolling pin. If Torrone is too soft to remove from the pan, use the back of another quarter sheet pan to flatten it.
Once set use chef knife to cut into desired size pieces.

title: Almond Paste Cookies Macaroons
steps: Combine almond paste with sugar and 10x in the bowl of a mixer with the paddle. Mix until crumbly. Pour in egg whites very gradually scraping down the bowl frequently, making sure there are no lumps of almond paste. macaroon.
Sprinkle macaroons with granulated or 10x. Bake at 375 ºF about 10 minutes until slightly golden. The macaroons should be crisp on the outside and soft on the inside.
Allow macaroons to fully cool before removing from tray.

title: Apricot Almond Rugelach
steps: For the cream cheese pastry: In a food processor pulse the flour, butter and cream cheese until there are no lumps and the dough forms a ball. Remove the dough from the bowl and divide into

For the filling: Stir the preserves to loosen and in a separate bowl combine the almonds, sugar and zest and set aside. the almond mixture. Using a pizza wheel, cut the circle into 12 equal wedges. Roll each piece and shape into small crescents.
For the topping: Beat the egg in a bowl to use an egg wash. In another bowl, mix the sugar and almond flour. Brush the tops of each rugelach with egg wash and then sprinkle with almond sugar.
Bake at 325 ºF on silpat sheets until golden.

title: Basic Sugar Cookie
steps: Cream the butter and sugar.
Add the eggs and vanilla.
Add the combined dry ingredients until just incorporated.
Chill before rolling.
Roll, cut as instructed. Freeze before baking to maintain the best shape.
Bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method

title: Biscotti alla Mandorla
steps: Place the almonds on a baking pan and toast them at 350 degrees about 10 minutes, until they are very lightly colored. Cool the almonds.

Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, cinnamon and salt in a mixing bowl and stir to mix well. Rub in the butter by hand until the mixture is very fine and powdery and no pieces of butter remain visible. Add the cooled almonds.

Combine the egg, vanilla and milk in a bowl and whisk to mix. Stir the liquid into the dough with a fork, continuing to stir until the dough holds together. Turn the dough out on the work surface and knead lightly until smooth. Slide the logs onto a cookie sheet lined with a sheet of parchment and bake at 350 degrees about 20 minutes, until well risen and firm. Remove from oven and cool.

title: Cantucci Di Prato

ingredients.name: egg whites for brushing the tops of the logs

steps: In a bowl, by hand or machine, whisk together the sugar, whole eggs, yolks, and orange zest.
In a separate bowl, sift the flour and baking powder.
Add the flour to the egg mixture, stirring until completely incorporated. Add the almonds.
Scrape the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces. Shape
Bake at 350ºF until firm and golden brown.
Cool on the pan for 10 minutes. Using a serrated knife, cut on the diagonal into ¾ inch slices. Bake again at 350ºF until crisp.

title: Chef Dalia'S Coconut Macaroons
steps: Combine the sugar, coconut, flour and salt with a paddle.
Add the egg whites and mix until combined.
Melt together the butter, honey and scraped vanilla bean and add to the mixture to combine.
Store in hotel pan and refrigerate overnight.
Scoop and bake at 350ºF.

title: Chef Jenny'S Sprinkle Doodles
steps: Cream the butter and sugar.
Add eggs and mix until combined.
Add dry ingredients making sure to scrape from the bottom just until combined. Add the sprinkles and mix until just combined.
Scoop cookies with a 1-ounce scoop. Roll balls into granulated sugar.
Bake at 350º F.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Chef Scott'S Crinkle Cookies

ingredients.name: liqueur (Crème de Menthe, Grand Marnier, rum, or brandy)

steps: Whip eggs and sugar on high speed until heavy ribbon stage.
Melt chocolate with butter. Off the heat, whisk the liqueur into the chocolate mixture.
Stir the chocolate mixture into the whipped eggs.
Combine the dry ingredients and fold them into the chocolate mixture. Place in a hotel pan and refrigerate until firm.

Place sugar and 10x in separate bowls. With a half-ounce ice cream scoop, scoop chilled cookie batter and roll first in granulated sugar, then roll again 10x. (Make sure they are heavily coated with the 10x!)
Bake at 325F for about 20 minutes. The cookies should remain soft in the center.

title: Chocolate Cherry Biscotti
steps: Sift together the flour, cocoa, baking soda and salt into a bowl. Whisk in the sugar. Add the chocolate and cherries and stir to combine.
Beat the eggs lightly with the vanilla extract and add to the flour mixture. Mix the dough using a spatula or paddle attachment of a mixer. The dough will come together as you continue to mix it.
Turn the dough onto the table and divide into 4 equal pieces. By hand, mold and roll each each log.
Brush the tops of each log with beaten egg white. Sprinkle a little granulated sugar over the tops.
Bake at 325ºF degrees. Cool 10 minutes. Using a serrated knife slice the logs on the degrees until crisp.

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
steps: Cream butter and sugars together until very light, about 5 minutes. Add eggs and vanilla gradually. Scrape down bowl.
Combine flours, baking soda, baking powder and salt into a bowl. Reduce speed to low, add dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Add chopped chocolates.
Scoop onto parchment-lined baking sheet in desired size. Sprinkle lightly with sea salt. Bake at 350 ºF until golden brown but still soft.
notes: Utilize the Creaming method

title: Chocolate Sables
steps: Cream butter and sugar.
Beat in egg and yolks gradually.
Add dry ingredients and mix until just combined, being careful not to over mix.
Divide dough and shape each into logs or as instructed. Chill until firm.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Clove Scented Pecan Snowballs
steps: Cream the butter with the sugar and salt. Add vanilla and water. Add flour and mix to incorporate and then add pecans. Do not over mix.

Scoop dough with a small ice cream scoop. Place on parchment-lined sheet pan and bake at 350 ºF for about 15 minutes until firm. Remove from oven and immediately and carefully place in a hotel pan filled with the 10x (and cloves, if desired) and make sure to completely cover. Allow to cool in 10x overnight if possible. After completely cooled, remove from 10x and roll in fresh 10x.

title: Cocoa Tuile Cup
steps: Beat the butter on medium speed with the paddle attachment until soft and light.
Sift the confectioners' sugar into a bowl and beat it into the butter mixture. Continue beating until light.
Add the vanilla, followed by half of the egg whites.
Combine the cocoa powder and flour together and beat half into the butter-sugar mixture. Beat in the remaining egg whites and cream. Add the remaining flour-cocoa mixture.
Apply a thin layer of the batter onto Silpat-lined sheet pans using a stencil.
Bake at 205°C/400°F for about 6 minutes, or until just golden around the edges. Immediately remove the tuile from the pans and place each one in a small bowl or

title: Florentines
steps: Sift the flour into a large bowl. Add the fruit and nuts and toss until evenly distributed.
Melt the butter and sugar over medium heat. Remove from heat and add salt and cream.. Pour into the bowl of flour, fruit and nuts and stir until completely combined.
Using a small ice cream scoop, place about a 1/2-ounce of the batter on silpats, about 3 inches apart. Using a fork, flatten the batter as much as possible without leaving holes.
Bake at 350F for about 10-12 minutes until deep golden brown all over, then allow to cool on the tray.
Melt pate a glace over double boiler and brush the underside of each Florentine with pate a glace. Leave to cool and set. separately and combine.
notes: *pate a glace, for coating
Note: To make your own pate a glace if necessary, melt 2 parts chocolate and 1 part shortening

title: Nick Malgieri'S Supernatural Brownies
steps: Melt butter and chocolate over a double boiler.
Whisk eggs in a large bowl and whisk in salt, sugar, brown sugar and vanilla. Temper with the melted chocolate and butter mixture, then stir in flour.
Pour batter onto a buttered and parchment-lined half sheet pan and spread evenly. Bake for about 1 hour at 350ºF, until batter is moderately firm in the center. Chill before cutting. Cut

title: Oatmeal Cookies
steps: On medium speed beat together butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until well mixed. Add eggs gradually. Beat in vanilla.
Combine the flour, baking powder, salt, and rolled oats.
Add the flour and oat mixture to the creamed mixture and combine well. Add raisins, walnuts, and chocolate.
Using a small ice cream scoop, drop batter, well-spaced, onto parchment-lined sheet pans.
Flatten with the back of a fork. Chill if possible.
Bake at 350 ºF until well colored and firm.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Pizzele
steps: Combine half of the flour with remaining dry ingredients.
In a bowl whip the eggs by hand and beat in the butter and vanilla.
Add dry mixture to the egg mixture. Fold in the remaining flour.
Heat a pizzelle iron and place approximately one heaping tablespoon of batter on it. Close iron and cook 30 seconds to one minute.

title: Raspberry or Apricot Crumb Bars

ingredients.name: seedless raspberry jam or apricot preserves

steps: Spread the jam or preserves evenly on the dough and within the sheet pan extender.
Distribute the crumb topping evenly. Bake at 350ºF until the crumbs are well colored.

title: Salted Peanut Butter Cookies
steps: Cream together the shortening, peanut butter, sugar and brown sugar.
Add liquid ingredients, mix to combine
Add dry ingredients and mix until just combined.
Add peanuts mixed with salt.
Utilize a small ice cream scoop and bake at 350 F. until very slightly firm.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Spritz Cookies
steps: Mix the almond paste, butter and 10x with a paddle making sure there are absolutely no lumps. Add the eggs gradually, scraping the sides often. Cream all of these ingredients until light. Add the cake flour and mix just until combined.
Pipe as directed. Bake at 350ºF for 15 minutes or until there is color on the bottom of the cookie. These cookies should be very light in color
notes: Utilize the Creaming method

title: Tennessee Icebox Cookies
steps: Cream the butter, brown sugar and vanilla.
Beat in the eggs making sure to scrape down the sides.
Combine dry ingredients and add to creamed mixture. Mix until just combined, then add the almonds.
Scrape the dough onto a lightly floured work surface and shape into logs as instructed.
Chill the dough until firm.
Slice the cookies ¼” thick and arrange the cookies on parchment-lined sheet pans and bake at 350ºF.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: The Good Cook's Gingersnaps
steps: Cream the butter and sugar until light.
Beat in the egg and molasses.
Sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, ginger, cinnamon, and cloves.
Mix the dry ingredients into the creamed mixture until just combined.
Scoop and roll into balls, and place on parchment-lined sheet pans.
Bake at 350 F until cracked on top and set.

title: Tuile Cup
steps: Beat the butter on medium speed with the paddle attachment until soft and light.
Sift the confectioners' sugar into a bowl and beat it into the butter mixture. Continue beating until light.
Add the vanilla, followed by half of the egg whites.
Beat half of the flour into the butter-sugar mixture. Beat in the remaining egg whites and cream. Add the remaining flour.
Apply a thin layer of the batter onto Silpat-lined sheet pans using a stencil.

Bake at 205°C/400°F for about 6 minutes, or until just golden around the edges. Immediately remove the tuile from the pans and place each one in a small bowl or brioche tin. Place another mold, smaller that the first, over each tuile and press it into the bottom of the mold. Leave the tuile in the molds until cool and crisp.

title: Vanilla Sables
steps: Cream butter, sugar and salt.
Add the yolks and vanilla extract.
Add flour and mix until just combined, being careful not to over mix.
Shape into logs or as instructed. Chill until firm.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Venetian Rainbow Cookies
steps: With the paddle attachment, beat almond paste, sugar (1) and butter until light, fluffy and no lumps remain.
Add yolks and extract and beat until combined.
Add flour and salt and beat until combined.
Divide mixture into thirds in three separate bowls. Color one bowl, red. Color another green. Leave the remaining bowl uncolored. Set bowls aside.

Separately, whip egg whites, gradually adding sugar (2) to achieve a soft peak. Divide the meringue equally into each bowl of almond paste mixture, and then fold to combine. Spread each bowl of batter onto its own parchment-lined half sheet pan that has been slightly sprayed with pan spray. Make sure to use the flattest trays and to spread the batter as evenly as possible.
Bake at 350ºF until just set in the center. DO NOT OVERBAKE.
Chill layers before spreading apricot preserves in between. If possible, weigh down assembled layers and chill before covering with pate a glace. Cut as instructed.

title: Basque Cake Basque Cake Batter Pastry Cream Basque Cake Batter
steps: Cream the butter and sugar.
Add the eggs and vanilla gradually until emulsified.
Add the dry ingredients and mix until just combined.

With half the batter use a # 806 tip to pipe a continuous spiral of batter, starting from the center, to completely cover the bottom of the pan (* Do not overfill the bag as the batter is very stiff) Along the outer edge, pipe one complete ring to create a dam at the edge. (This will contain the pastry cream in the center which will be added next.)
Place the pastry cream in a pastry bag with a # 806 tip and pipe the pastry cream in the center of the dam and pipe a single continuous spiral until the dam that you have created has been filled.
notes: *Note: Prepare the pastry cream filling recipe first.
Utilize the Creaming Method

title: Bourbon Diplomat Cream

ingredients.name: heavy cream (36% fat), whipped to firm peaks

steps: Gently fold together the whipped cream, chilled vanilla pastry cream, and bourbon. Reserve chilled.

title: Bread Pudding
steps: Bring the raisins to a boil in a small pan of water and drain. Place raisins in a container just large enough to hold them and pour the rum over them (optional).
Scatter the raisins on the bottom of individual pudding dishes. Cut the bread into 1/4-cubes. Place the cubes in the individual containers.
Pour the basic custard mixture over the bread, making sure that the bread is evenly moistened. Allow the brioche a few minutes to absorb the custard and to see if more custard mix is needed.
Bake in a pan of water at 300 degrees for approximately 45 minutes or until custard has set. Serve the Bread Pudding warm. Options: add bananas, chocolate chips or nuts to the bread pudding.
notes: Note: Serve with Caramel Sauce if desired.

title: Caramel Pastry Cream
steps: Whisk the corn starch into the first measurement of milk (1) in a medium-sized mixing bowl, followed by the first measurement of sucrose (1), the whole eggs, egg yolks and salt.

Place the second measurement of sucrose (2) and water to cover in a medium saucepan. Cook to a medium amber caramel. Meanwhile, warm the milk and vanilla. Slowly add the warmed milk to caramel and bring to a boil.

Remove from heat and temper the warm milk mixture into the corn starch mixture. Return to heat and cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, just to a boil. Remove from heat (remove and the scraped vanilla pod) and whisk in the butter; transfer to a shallow container, cover with plastic film, and chill rapidly.

title: Chartreuse Mousseline
steps: Beat the pastry cream smooth to loosen it.
In a separate bowl, beat the Swiss buttercream until smooth.
Combine the pastry cream and buttercream, beating until fully homogeneous.
Add the green chartreuse and beat until evenly incorporated.
Chill until ready to pipe or spread.

title: Chocolate & Summer Berry Coulis Dome Lime Dacquoise Cake Summer Berry Coulis Insert Chocolate Mousse Vanilla Mousseline Lime Dacquoise Cake
steps: Combine the 10 X, almond flour and lime zest and set aside.
In a mixer with the whip attachment, whip egg whites and lime juice. Gradually add the granulated sugar to make an ordinary meringue and whip to a firm peak.
Remove from the mixer and fold in almond flour mixture.
Spread batter on a parchment lined half sheet pan and sprinkle with chopped almonds.
Bake at 375 F. for 10-12 minutes until a light golden brown.
Allow cake to cool, invert and remove parchment paper.
Cut into discs to fit bottom of 12 flexi molds.

title: Chocolate Cremeux
steps: Prepare a crème Anglaise: Boil the milk, cream and vanilla beans.
Meanwhile, whisk together yolks and trimoline.

Temper yolk mixture with hot liquid, return entire mixture to the pan and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens to nappe or reaches 180F. Remove from heat and add the bloomed gelatin.
Pour hot crème anglaise through a chinoise over the chocolates and stir until combined.
Cover with plastic wrap making sure it touches surface of the custard to prevent a skin. Set aside to cool to 75F and then use.

title: Citrus Curd Tart

ingredients.name: pastry cream, flavored Framboise or Kirsch

steps: Bake at 350 degrees for about 25 minutes, or until set. Remove paper and beans and continue baking shell until very lightly colored. Cool the baked shell.
Spread a small amount of curd evenly in tart shell. Cover with meringue topping and brûlée with a propane torch.

title: Coffee Creme
steps: Bloom gelatin with the 45 grams of water.
Bring the next 4 ingredients to a boil
Whisk together the egg yolks and sugar and temper with the boiled mixture.
Return to the heat and cook to 185 F., being careful not to overcook.
Add the bloomed gelatin, stir to combine, and chill over an ice bath.
When cooled, blend with an immersion blender until smooth.
Pour into the cake frame on top of the Pecan Caramel and refrigerate.

title: Coffee Creme Brulee
steps: Combine cream, Trablit, sugar and vanilla bean in a pan and bring to a simmer. Whisk yolks in a bowl and temper with hot mixture. Strain through chinoise or fine strainer and skim foam from surface if necessary.

Pour the custard mixture into crème brulee dishes. Remove excess foam with torch if necessary. Bake in a water bath at 350 degrees until set. Cool and place in freezer about 1 hour or in refrigerator overnight.
notes: For the next lesson:

title: Creme Anglaise
steps: Bring half and half to a boil with sugar and split vanilla bean.
Whisk yolks to break them up, then temper with some of the boiling milk.
Return the remaining milk to the heat and whisk in the yolk mixture. Stir mixture constantly, until thickened to nappe or 180° F.
Strain immediately into a clean bowl over ice, and whisk to cool. Cover and refrigerate.
notes: Note: Crème Anglaise is part of your practical exam on lesson 23.

title: Creme Anglaise Mousse Base
steps: Bring the cream and milk to a boil with the sugar and split vanilla bean.
Whisk yolks in a bowl to break them up, then temper with some of the boiling cream and milk.
Return the remaining cream and milk to the heat and whisk in the yolk mixture. Stir the mixture constantly, until thickened to nappé or 180° F.
Strain immediately into a clean bowl over an ice bath.
Scale for mousse recipe as needed and proceed with instructions.

title: Creme Brulee
steps: Combine cream, sugar and vanilla bean in a pan and bring to a simmer. Whisk yolks in a bowl and temper with hot mixture. Strain through chinoise or fine strainer and skim foam from surface if necessary.

Pour the custard mixture into shallow ovenproof dishes. Remove excess foam with torch if necessary. Bake in a water bath at 350 degrees until set. Cool and place in freezer about 1 hour or in refrigerator overnight.
notes: For the next lesson:

title: Creme Mousseline
steps: Beat butter on medium speed with paddle until soft and light and no lumps remain.
Beat in pastry cream, continuing to beat until smooth.
Beat in flavoring at end.

title: Creme Mousseline Pralinee
steps: Proceed as for Crème Mousseline, beating butter and praline paste together until smooth, before adding pastry cream.

title: Crepe Layers Pastry Cream Crepe Layers
steps: Place the first 6 ingredients in a bowl and blend well to combine.
Add the flour and salt and blend until distributed evenly. Place the mixture, half at a time in a blender and blend just until smooth.
Strain the mixture with a fine strainer and place in refrigerator for a minimum of one half hour.
To cook the crepes, heat a crepe pan over medium heat and brush lightly with the clarified butter. With a 2 ounce ladle, swirl the crepe batter onto the bottom of the pan.
Cook on the first side until the crepe is set and the edges are a light brown color.
Flip the crepes and cook briefly on the other side. Slide out of the pan onto a parchment lined pan.

title: Crunchy Choux, Chartreuse Mousseline, Strawberry Pate a Choux
steps: Place the milk, water, sucrose, salt, and butter in saucepan and bring to a rolling boil.
Remove from heat and stir in the flour until combined; return to heat and cook for 1-2 minutes until a smooth mass has formed.
Transfer to the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment. Beat the mixture until slightly cooled; incorporate the eggs gradually scraping as needed.
Transfer the paste to a pastry bag and deposit into 4cm silicon hemisphere forms. Freeze.

title: Crème Fraiche Whipped Cream
steps: Combine the cream, sucrose, vanilla extract, and crème fraiche in a mixer bowl.
Whip on medium speed until mixture holds a soft peak but will hold its shape while piping.
Use immediately or refrigerate until needed.

title: Cupcake Fillings Lemon Curd
steps: Bloom gelatin sheet in cold water.
Bring to a boil juice, butter, zest and sugar (1).
Whisk together sugar (2) and eggs.
Temper hot liquid into egg mixture.
Return to heat, bring to simmer and cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add gelatin, mix until dissolved. Strain the mixture through a chinois. Cool over ice bath or in blast freezer.

title: Dark Chocolate Cremeux
steps: Place the chocolate in a mixing bowl and reserve. Combine the cream with the sorbitol, glucose, and salt; bring to a boil.
Meanwhile combine water and agar in a small sauce pan and gently bring to a boil, holding a simmer for two minutes.
Once cream has boiled, remove from heat and gradually incorporate into the chocolate. Follow with the agar mixture. Add the gelatin and transfer to oval silicon forms. Freeze.

title: Diluted Caramel for Baked Custards
steps: Prepare a Dry Caramel with the sugar and lemon juice, to a light amber color or as instructed. When the caramel is ready, pour in the liquid. The caramel may splatter when the liquid is added if the liquid is cold or is added too quickly.

Allow the caramel to reheat if necessary to dissolve any caramel that may have cooled or solidified. Remove from the heat and pour into molds for use. Caramel sauce: To enrich the caramel and make it into a sauce for the bread pudding, substitute 175 grams of heavy cream for the water and omit the lemon juice.

title: Fruit Tart with Pastry Cream

ingredients.name: pastry cream, flavored Framboise or Kirsch

steps: Line tart ring(s) or pan with the pate brisee and dock the bottom.
Blind bake until golden brown and fully set; cool completely.
Beat the framboise- or kirsch-flavored pastry cream smooth.
Spread or pipe the pastry cream evenly into the baked shell.
Arrange fresh fruit decoratively over the pastry cream.
Warm the neutral glaze and brush over the fruit to finish.

title: Goat Cheese Cream

ingredients.name: heavy cream whipped to soft peaks

steps: In the bowl of a mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, combine the goat cheese and sucrose. Cream the mixture until smooth, light, and fluffy.
Meanwhile, gently warm the milk and add the gelatin to dissolve.
Combine goat cheese and milk mixture. Fold in whipped cream. Transfer to a container and chill until set.

title: Lemon Cream
steps: Whisk together sugar, eggs and lemon zest in a bowl and put aside.
Bring the lemon juice to a boil and temper the eggs and sugar mix with the hot juice.
Place the entire mixture on the stove and bring to a boil, whisking constantly.
Remove from heat, strain and cool over ice bath until mixture reaches 110
At this point, using an immersion blender, add the softened butter.

When a homogenous mixture has been attained, scale 555g of lemon cream for the mousse below. Reserve the remaining cream (covered with plastic wrap to prevent a skin from forming) and set aside for the cake assembly.

title: Lemon Cream
steps: In a heavy saucepan, whisk together eggs and sucrose, and then add the lemon juice.
On medium heat, bring mixture just to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and add the gelatin.
Allow the mixture to cool 35˚C/95˚F and emulsify the butter into the mixture. Reserve chilled.

title: Lemon Curd
steps: In a heavy saucepan, whisk together the eggs and sucrose, and then add the lemon juice.
On medium heat, bring mixture just to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool 35˚C/95˚F and emulsify the butter into the base.

title: Lemon Curd
steps: Combine the zest, juice and sugar in a saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat.

Whisk the yolks in a bowl until liquid. Strain out the zest and beat 1/4 of the boiling liquid into the yolks and return the remaining liquid to a boil over low heat. Whisk the yolk mixture into the boiling liquid and continue whisking until it thickens. Allow it to come to a simmer.
Add the softened butter and stir to combine.
Pour the lemon curd into a clean container, press plastic wrap against the surface and chill it.

title: Lemon Curd
steps: Bloom gelatin sheet in cold water.
Bring the juice, butter, zest and sugar (1) to a boil.
Whisk together sugar (2) and eggs.
Temper egg mix with hot liquid.
Return to heat and bring to simmer. Cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add bloomed gelatin, mix until dissolved. Strain through a chinois and cool over ice bath or in blast freezer. Reserve for next lesson.

title: Lemon Curd
steps: Bloom gelatin sheet in cold water.
Bring juice/, butter, zest and sugar (1) to a boil.
Whisk together sugar (2) and eggs.
Temper hot liquid into eggs.
Return to heat, bring to simmer and cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add bloomed gelatin, mix until dissolved and strain through a chinois. Cool over ice bath.

title: Lemon Curd
steps: Bloom gelatin sheet in cold water.
Bring to a boil the juice, butter, sugar (1) and zest.
Whisk together sugar (2) and eggs.
Temper egg mix with the hot liquid.
Return to heat, and cook to a simmer, stirring constantly.
Add bloomed gelatin, mix until dissolved. Strain through a chinois and cool over ice bath or on a plastic lined sheet pan.

title: Lemon Raspberry Roulade Biscuit Layer Lemon Curd Buttercream Biscuit Layer
steps: In a KitchenAid mixing bowl combine the eggs, yolks and the sugar (1).
Warm over double boiler until sugar is dissolved and mixture is warm.
Mix until ribboned.
Sift flour and corn starch together and add to ribboned eggs Add vanilla extract and whip for 1 more minute at high speed until light and fluffy.
Separately, make an ordinary meringue from the egg whites and sugar (2) and whip to a soft peak.
Fold together both mixes until just combined.
Spread evenly onto flat parchment lined half sheet pans.
Bake in a 375 F. convection oven.

title: Lemon Strawberry Pistachio Cake Pistachio Pain De Gene Lemon Ricotta Sponge Strawberry Gelee Lemon Cream Lemon Mousse Pistachio Pain De Gene
steps: In a small sauce pan, brown the butter over medium heat making sure not to burn the butter.
In a KitchenAid bowl with the paddle attachment, soften the almond and pistachio paste with 50 grams of egg until smooth, making sure to scrape down often.
Gradually add the second eggs and whip the mixture to ribbon stage.
Meanwhile, sift together the flour, baking powder and salt and set aside.
Take a small portion of the egg/nut paste mixture and whisk into the warmed brown butter.
Fold the sifted dry ingredients into remaining egg mixture.
Continue to mix while slowly adding the brown butter mixture.
Stir in finely chopped pistachios.
Spread onto a flat parchment lined sheet pan.
Bake 10-15 minutes at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Pain de Gene mixing method

title: Lightened Pastry Cream
steps: Whip heavy cream until soft peak.
Soak gelatin in cold water until softened. Squeeze gelatin to extract excess water. Melt gelatin in a bowl over gently simmering water.
Beat pastry cream with a rubber spatula to loosen slightly.
Remove some of pastry cream and temper into gelatin, make sure there are no lumps.
Add tempered gelatin mixture into the remaining pastry cream, whisking constantly. Fold in heavy cream by hand working quickly.
Cover surface with plastic wrap and refrigerate for longer storage.

title: Lime Cream
steps: In a heavy saucepan, whisk together eggs and sucrose, and then add the lemon juice.
On medium heat, bring mixture just to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and add the gelatin.
Allow the mixture to cool 35˚C/95˚F and emulsify the butter into the mixture. Reserve chilled.

title: Lime Curd
steps: Bloom gelatin sheet in cold water.
Bring to a boil juice, butter, zest and sugar (1).
Whisk together sugar (2) and eggs.
Temper hot liquid into egg mixture.
Return to heat, bring to simmer and cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add gelatin, mix until dissolved. Strain the mixture through a chinois. Cool over ice bath or in blast freezer.

title: Maple Panna Cotta
yield: (approximately 17 red oval flexi molds) Yield 1,110 grams
steps: Bloom gelatin and set aside.
Heat coconut milk, maple syrup and bourbon until just boiling. Turn off heat and add bloomed gelatin, pour mixture over the chocolate to melt. Whisk until completely smooth.
Pour into flexi molds ½ way, top with 1 oval of dacquoise.
Top with remaining panna cotta and top with pecan biscuit.
Freeze in blast freezer or place in the refrigerator overnight.

title: Maple Pecan Creme Brulee
steps: Combine cream, maple syrup, sugar and vanilla bean in a pan and bring to a simmer. Whisk yolks in a bowl and temper with hot mixture. Strain through a chinoise or fine strainer and skim foam from surface if necessary.
Scatter the pecans on the bottom of the crème brulee dishes
Pour the custard mixture into crème brulee dishes. Bake in a water bath at 350 degrees until set. Cool and place in freezer about 1 hour or in refrigerator overnight.
notes: For the next lesson:

title: Milk Chocolate Panna Cotta
steps: Place the 50g of the milk into a bowl and sprinkle the gelatin powder onto the surface making sure it gets hydrated. Allow it to stand for several minutes for the gelatin to bloom and soften.
Combine the cream, the second milk, and vanilla bean; bring to a boil. Remove from heat and infuse for 5 minutes.
Remove the vanilla bean from the cream mixture and return to a boil. Remove from heat and pour over the chopped milk chocolate; combine with the bloomed gelatin.
Homogenize well with an immersion blender.
Strain and deposit the mixture approximately two thirds high into the prepared containers and refrigerate until set.

title: Mixture for Baked Custards
steps: Bring half and half, and sugar to a boil.
Whisk eggs and yolks until liquid. Whisk in boiling milk in a thin stream. Strain into a clean bowl and skim foam from surface. Stir in vanilla extract.
Reserve and use the custard mixture for the crème renversee and bread pudding recipes.

title: Panna Cotta
steps: Place the 50g of the milk into a bowl and sprinkle the gelatin powder onto the surface making sure it gets hydrated. Allow it to stand for several minutes for the gelatin to bloom and soften.
Combine the cream, the second milk, sugar, vanilla bean, and lemon zest; bring to a boil. Remove from heat and infuse for 5 minutes.
Remove the vanilla bean from the cream mixture and return to a boil. Remove from heat and combine with the bloomed gelatin.
Homogenize well with an immersion blender.
Strain and deposit the mixture approximately two thirds high into the prepared containers and refrigerate until set.

title: Passion Fruit Curd
steps: Bloom gelatin sheet in cold water.
Bring to a boil juice, butter, zest and sugar (1).
Whisk together sugar (2) and eggs.
Temper hot liquid into egg mixture.
Return to heat, bring to simmer and cook until thickened, stirring constantly.
Add gelatin, mix until dissolved. Strain the mixture through a chinois. Cool over ice bath or in blast freezer.

title: Pastry Cream
steps: Dissolve cornstarch in 1/8 of the milk.
Combine the remaining milk with the sugar in a rondeau or saucepan; bring to a boil.
Whisk whole eggs, then the yolks into cornstarch mixture.
Pour 1/3 of the boiling milk into the egg mixture, whisking. Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil.
Whisk the egg mixture into the boiling milk, whisking until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil. Boil, whisking, about 1 minute.
Remove from heat and beat in the butter and vanilla.
Pour the cream into a stainless-steel pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface, and poke holes to vent. Chill immediately.

title: Pastry Cream
steps: Dissolve cornstarch with the first measurement of milk. Add the sugar, eggs and yolks, and whisk to combine.
Combine the second milk with the second sugar in saucepan; bring to a boil.
Temper the corn starch mixture with some of the boiling milk mixture.
Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the corn starch mixture into the boiling milk. Whisking until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil, stirring constantly

Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute or as instructed. Remove from heat and whisk in the butter and vanilla. Transfer to a bowl and cool over ice. Or alternatively, spread thinly on a plastic-lined sheet pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately. Variations: Chocolate: Add 175 grams of semisweet chocolate when adding butter. Coffee: Add 30 grams of Trablit extract (or to taste), or make a paste of water and instant espresso powder and add when adding butter. Praline or Nutella: Add 120 grams of praline or Nutella paste and 50 grams of chopped toasted hazelnuts (optional) when adding butter.

title: Pastry Cream
steps: Dissolve cornstarch in some of the milk. Add approximately ½ of the sugar, all of the eggs and yolks, and whisk to combine.
Combine the remaining milk with the other half of the sugar in saucepan; bring to a boil.
Temper the corn starch mixture with some of the boiling milk mixture.
Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the corn starch mixture into the boiling milk, whisking constantly until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil.

Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute or as instructed. Remove from heat and whisk in the butter and vanilla. Transfer to a bowl and cool over ice. Or alternatively, spread thinly on a plastic-lined sheet pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately.

title: Pastry Cream
steps: Combine the cornstarch with approximately ½ of the sugar and ½ of the milk. Whisk to remove lumps. Add all of the eggs, yolks and vanilla and whisk to combine.
Combine the remaining milk with the remaining sugar in saucepan; bring to a boil.
Temper the corn starch mixture with some of the boiling milk mixture.
Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the corn starch mixture into the boiling milk, whisking constantly until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil.

Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute or as instructed. Remove from heat and whisk in the butter. Transfer to a bowl and cool over ice. Or spread thinly on a plastic lined sheet pan and press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately.

title: Pastry Cream Large Medium Small Batch
steps: Dissolve cornstarch in some of the milk. Add approximately ½ of the sugar, all of the eggs and yolks, and whisk to combine.
Combine the remaining milk with the other half of the sugar in saucepan; bring to a boil.
Temper the corn starch mixture with some of the boiling milk.
Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the corn starch mixture into the boiling milk, whisking until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil.

Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute or as instructed. Remove from heat and whisk in the butter and vanilla. Transfer to a bowl and cool over ice. Or spread thinly on a plastic lined sheet pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately.
Flavoring options follow on the next page.

title: Pineapple Coconut Anglaise
steps: Into a heavy saucepan, combine the coconut purée, pineapple purée, cream, and rum. Bring to a boil over medium heat. Meanwhile whisk together sucrose and egg yolks.
Remove from heat and carefully temper the egg yolks.
Continue to cook on low heat to 85˚C/185˚F. Remove from heat and strain through a chinois. Chill over an ice water bath.

title: Praline Mousseline
steps: Combine the softened butter and praline paste in a mixer bowl and beat until thoroughly combined. Slowly add the pastry cream, scraping down the bowl to ensure the mixture is homogenous.
Transfer to a pastry bag fitted with a medium plain tip and pipe into the baked pâte croustade shells. Finish each tart with candied orange peel, caramelized hazelnuts, and chocolate garnish.

title: Raspberry Cremeux
steps: Combine the milk and glucose in a sauce pan and bring to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in the bloomed gelatin.
Whisk in the white chocolate and raspberry purée.
Add the cream and emulsify thoroughly with an immersion blender. Deposit onto already set gelee and chill to fully set.

title: Raspberry Cremeux
steps: Combine the milk and glucose in a sauce pan and bring to a boil. Remove from heat and stir in the bloomed gelatin.
Whisk in the white chocolate and raspberry purée.
Add the cream and emulsify thoroughly with an immersion blender. Deposit onto already set gelee and chill to fully set.

title: STRAWBERY FRAISIER (two GENOISE PASTRY CREAM MOUSSELINE SIMPLE SYRUP PLAIN SPONGE CAKE (GENOISE)
steps: Place the eggs, sugar and the salt in bowl of an electric mixer, and whisk well. Place bowl on a pan of simmering water and heat stirring constantly with hand whip until mixture is warm and the sugar is dissolved. Do not overheat. Remove from heat, place on mixer and whip until cool and increased in volume until the mixture is very thick. (It should hold its shape once you draw your finger across the batter).

While the mixture is whipping, sift the flour. Remove egg mixture from mixer and sift flour over the egg mixture, efficiently and quickly fold with a rubber spatula making sure to scrape the bottom. Finish by folding in cooled melted butter just until incorporated. (Do not over fold to prevent the cake from rising properly) Bake at 350 ºF.
notes: Utilize the Genoise mixing method.

title: Sweetened Whipped Cream
steps: Whip some of the heavy cream with all of the sugar until thickened.
Add the remaining heavy cream and whip to stiff peak.
Keep refrigerated until needed.

title: Tres Leches Cake Tres Leches Cake Layer Tres Leches Syrup Whipped Cream Tres Leches Cake Layer
steps: Cream the butter and sugar.
Gradually add the eggs and vanilla, and emulsify well.
Add the dry ingredients until just combined.
notes: Utilize the Creaming method

title: Vanilla Custard
steps: Whisk the corn starch into the first measurement of milk (1) in a medium-sized mixing bowl, followed by the first measurement of sucrose (1), the whole eggs, egg yolks and salt.
Combine the remaining milk (2), sucrose (2), and vanilla in a sauce pan; bring to a boil.

Remove from heat and temper the warm milk mixture into the corn starch mixture. Return to heat and cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, just to a boil. Remove from heat (remove the scraped vanilla pod) and whisk in the butter; transfer to a shallow container, cover with plastic film, and chill rapidly.

title: Vanilla Mousseline
steps: Bloom gelatin sheets in cold water and set aside.
Whip heavy cream to soft peak, reserve in refrigerator.
Make a pastry cream.
Add bloomed gelatin and butter to finished pastry cream off the fire.
Cool to room temperature then fold in whipped cream. Assembly:
Pipe layer of mousseline in mold, spreading evenly around sides.
Insert frozen berry coulis dome.
Top with thin layer of mousse and lime dacquoise and freeze.

title: Vanilla Pastry Cream
steps: Whisk the corn starch into the first measurement of milk (1) in a medium-sized mixing bowl, followed by the first measurement of sucrose (1), the whole eggs, egg yolks and salt.
Combine the remaining milk (2), sucrose (2), and vanilla in a sauce pan; bring to a boil.

Remove from heat and temper the warm milk mixture into the corn starch mixture. Return to heat and cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, just to a boil. Remove from heat (remove and the scraped vanilla pod) and whisk in the butter; transfer to a shallow container, cover with plastic film, and chill rapidly.

title: Whipped Cream
steps: Combine the heavy cream, crème fraîche, sugar, and vanilla extract in a chilled bowl.
Whip on medium-high speed to soft-to-medium peaks.
Use immediately or refrigerate until needed.

title: Whipped Cream
steps: Combine the cream and sugar in a mixer bowl.
Whip until stiff peak.
Use immediately or refrigerate.

title: Whipped Cream
steps: Sift the 10X sugar into the heavy cream.
Whip to soft peaks for folding or stiff peaks for piping, as the application requires.
Use immediately or refrigerate until needed.

title: Yogurt Panna Cotta Yogurt Panna Cotta
steps: Combine the cream, sucrose, and zest in a sauce pan, gently warming over medium heat.

Remove from heat and add the gelatin, stirring to dissolve. Temper into yogurt and strain through a chinois or fine mesh sieve. Transfer to silicon molds and place into a freezer. Allow at least two hours to freeze.

title: Almond Frangipane
steps: Combine almond paste, sugar and softened butter in mixer with the paddle attachment. Beat on medium speed until smooth.
Beat in the eggs, a few at a time. Mix until smooth.
Add the lemon zest and vanilla, stir to combine.
Combine flour and baking powder and add to the mixing bowl beating on low speed, just until the flour is absorbed.

title: Almond Frangipane
steps: Combine almond paste, sugar and 1 egg in mixer.
Beat with paddle on medium speed until smooth.
Beat in butter, lemon zest and vanilla, mixing until smooth.
Beat in the eggs, one at a time, until very smooth, scraping bowl and paddle several times.
Combine flour and baking powder.
On low speed, stir in flour until just absorbed.

title: Almond Pate Sucree
steps: Prepare using Creaming Dough Mixing Method. Chill until ready to use.
Beat butter and sugar in the bowl of a kitchen aid fitted with a paddle attachment.
Add flours, vanilla and eggs. Mix until just combined, making sure to scrape from the bottom.
Chill before using. Hazelnut flour can be substituted for almond flour Roll thinly for best results Fragile when cooked.
notes: Note:

title: Choux Sablee
steps: Place all ingredients in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with paddle; mix on low speed just until combined, adding a small amount of water if necessary.
Roll the dough between two sheets of acetate or parchment and cut to approximately 30cm by 40cm. Freeze. Cut into 5cm discs.

title: Cocoa Pate Sucree
steps: Combine and blend the butter, salt, 10X sugar, and almond flour in a food processor.
Add the whole egg and first measurement of flour (1); process just until incorporated.
Add the remaining flour (2) and cocoa powder; process just until incorporated. Wrap the dough and chill.

title: Cocoa Sablee
steps: Sift the flour, cocoa and baking soda and reserve. Place the butter in the bowl of a mixer fitted with a paddle attachment and beat at medium speed until the butter is soft and creamy. Add the sugars and salt and beat for another 1 or 2 minutes. Reduce the speed to low and slowly add the sifted dry ingredients. Mix until the dry ingredients are just incorporated.

Transfer the dough to a clean smooth work surface, divide in half and, working with one half at a time, shape the dough into a cylinder that is roughly 1 ½ inches in diameter. Wrap the cylinders in plastic wrap and chill them for at least 1 hour.

Unwrap the dough cylinders and slice the dough into individual cookies about ½ inch thick. Divide the cookies onto parchment or Silpat-lined baking sheets, allowing ample space between them. Bake at 160˚/320°F for 12-15 minutes. Remove from oven and cool completely before removing from baking sheet.
Use as cookies and also crumbled into pieces as garnish.

title: Crumb Topping
steps: Melt butter and cool.
Combine the remaining ingredients and stir butter in evenly.
Allow it to cool for 5 minutes.
Break into crumbs by hand.

title: Crumb Topping
steps: Melt butter and cool.
Combine the remaining ingredients and stir butter in evenly.
Allow it to cool for 5 minutes.
Break into crumbs by hand.

title: Crumb Topping for Bars Cookies
steps: Combine dry ingredients. Add butter and mix until crumbly.
Add nuts and oats and mix until evenly distributed.

title: Frangipane From Ground Nuts
steps: Beat ground nuts, sugar, and butter by machine w/ paddle on medium speed until light.
Beat in optional flavorings, then yolks, one at a time, beating until smooth.
Beat in flour until just absorbed.

title: Muffin Crumb Topping
steps: Place all of the ingredients into a mixing bowl and mix by hand or machine until a loose, crumb-like texture is achieved.
Liberally cover the surface of the muffins with the crumb topping and bake in a pre- heated 350˚F oven.

title: Pasta Frolla
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method. Chill until ready to use.

title: Pate Brisee
steps: Prepare using Flaky Dough Mixing Method. Chill until ready to use.

title: Pate Sablee
steps: Prepare using Creaming Dough Mixing Method; chill until ready to use.

title: Pate Sucree
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method. Chill until ready to use.

title: Pate Sucree
steps: Cream together the butter, sucrose, and almond flour.
Slowly add the eggs and yolks, scraping down the bowl.
Add the flour and baking powder, mixing until just combined.

Roll or sheet the dough very thinly. Transfer the dough to a half sheet pan and bake at 150°C/300°F for 10 minutes. Remove from the oven and cut the dough into 4cm squares. Return to the oven for 5-7 minutes, or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven and cool.

title: Pate Sucree
steps: In an electric mixer bowl fitted with the paddle attachment, cream the butter and sugar until light. Combine the almond flour and baking powder and add all at once to the creamed butter.
While mixer is on low speed, add egg, scraping down the bowl as needed.
Add the flour, mixing until thoroughly combined. Add water. Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface. Wrap, chill and allow to rest approximately two hours.
Roll dough to a thickness of 3mm, and cut bases with an 8cm (3”) round cutter.
Place dough on a parchment lined sheet pan and bake at 150˚C/300ºF for about 12 minutes, or until golden.

title: Pate a Choux
steps: Combine milk, butter, sugar and salt in a pan over medium heat; bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.
At a full boil, remove the pan from the heat; add the flour all at once. Stir vigorously removing any lumps of flour to a smooth consistency.
Return to the heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the paste forms a ball and a skin forms on the bottom of the pot.
Transfer the paste to the bowl of an electric mixer; beat on lowest speed with the paddle to cool the paste slightly, about 20 seconds. Add eggs gradually making sure to scrape down the bowl often.

title: Raspberry Sablee
steps: In a mixer bowl fitted with the paddle attachment, cream together butter and sucrose.
Slowly add egg yolk and raspberry compound, scraping down the bowl and mixing until thoroughly combined.
Add the flour, salt, baking soda and red color, mixing just until combined. Chill.

title: Shortbread Crust for Bars
steps: Cream together butter, sugar, light brown sugar and salt until smooth.
Gradually add the eggs and vanilla, scraping the bowl in between additions.
Add the flour all at once, lower speed, and mix until smooth.
Press or roll onto the bottom of a flat half sheet pan with parchment glued down with spray or butter. Use plastic wrap on top to smooth the crust, making sure it's an even thickness.
Press a sheet pan extender into the dough and refrigerate until chilled and firm.
Pre-bake the crust in a 350ºF deck oven until baked through and very light in color. Do not remove the extender until after baked with chosen filling.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Sour Cream Dough
steps: Prepare using Flaky Dough Mixing Method, using sour cream as liquid.

title: Walnut Frangipane
steps: Place walnuts and granulated sugar in work bowl of robot coup. Grind finely.
In mixing bowl, cream butter and brown sugar with paddle on medium speed until light.
Beat walnut mixture into creamed butter. Add cinnamon and dark rum.
Beat in the eggs, a few at a time.
Combine flour and baking powder and add to the mixing bowl beating on low speed, just until the flour is absorbed.

title: Almond Filling
steps: Combine almond paste and egg white in mixing bowl and beat with paddle on low speed until smooth.

title: Apple Filling
steps: Peel, core and cut apples into wedges.
Place apples and all other ingredients into a large rondeau, cooking over medium heat, covered, until apples begin to soften.
Uncover and continue to cook until liquid is evaporated.
Chill until ready to use.

title: Caramel Filling
steps: Combine the sucrose, water, lemon juice, and glucose in a medium sauce pan. Cook over medium heat to a dark amber color.
While sugar is cooking, warm heavy cream slightly.
Remove caramelized sugar from heat and deglaze with the cream. Return to low heat to completely dissolve sugar and to make sure no lumps remain.
Once smooth, remove from heat, cool slightly and emulsify butter into the caramel using a hand held mixer.

title: Caramel Hazelnut Filling
yield: Yield of filling (one half sheet pan) 1,196 grams
steps: Combine corn syrup and sugar in a sauce pan and mix well. Place on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the syrup comes to a boil.
At the boil, add the butter and cream and return to a boil.
Simmer the mixture for about 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove from heat and stir in vanilla and rum.
Combine the syrup and hazelnuts in a bowl and mix to combine.
Pour the filling into the sheet pan extender on the pre-baked crust and spread evenly.

Bake at 350ºF until the filling bubbles across the surface, approximately 20-25 minutes. Filling will still be soft but will set up after it is chilled. Be careful not to over bake the bar or the filling with be too hard when cooled.

title: Caramel Sauce
steps: Combine the sucrose, water, lemon juice, and glucose in a medium sauce pan. Cook over medium heat to a dark amber color.
While sugar is cooking, warm heavy cream slightly.
Remove caramelized sugar from heat and deglaze with the cream. Return to low heat to completely dissolve sugar and to make sure no lumps remain.
Once smooth, remove from heat, cool slightly and emulsify butter into the caramel using an immersion blender.

title: Cheese Filling
steps: Mix all ingredients together until smooth.
Roll out dough like croissants. Dough should be 1/8-1/4 inch thick. Trim
Egg wash opposite corners of the dough.
Fold egg washed corners in towards center and press down to seal. Brush
Place on tray before filling.
Place a dollop of filling (farmer's cheese or frangipane) in center. Place
Egg wash Danish and place in oven set at 350°F (actual temp is 375°F)
Let cool and brush center with heated apricot nappage.
Sprinkle tops with confectioners sugar.

title: Chocolate Almond Filling
steps: Paddle the almond paste on low speed to break it up and soften it.
Add the egg white gradually, beating until smooth, light, and free of lumps.
Fold in the finely grated bittersweet chocolate until evenly distributed.

title: Chocolate Sauce
steps: In a small saucepan, combine milk and glucose and bring to a boil.
Remove from heat and gradually pour over chocolate, stirring to combine.

title: Coffee Syrup
steps: Combine ingredients and bring to a boil.

title: Cooked Apple Filling
steps: Peel, core, and halve the apples. Slice each half into 5 pieces.
Combine apple slices with remaining ingredients in a heavy saucepan over medium heat.
Cover and cook for 10 minutes.
Uncover; lower heat and cook until water evaporates. Cool.

title: Cooked Apple Filling Cooked Apple Filling

ingredients.name: Granny Smith or Golden Delicious apples

steps: Peel, halve, core, and slice the apples as for a filling, cutting each half into five or six slices from stem to blossom end.
