Melt the butter in a wide pan and add the sliced apples, followed by the sucrose, cinnamon and lemon juice; stir well to combine. Cook over medium heat until the apples begin to ‘sizzle'. Lower the heat and cover the pan; steam for 10 minutes.
Remove the cover and continue cooking over low heat to evaporate the juices for an additional 10 minutes. The apples should remain tender and in distinct pieces.
Use immediately or transfer the filling to a stainless steel pan and cool to room temperature. Wrap tightly and refrigerate until needed, up to 5 days. cranberries.
notes: Optional: Substitute pears for apples for the filling. Add inclusions such as nuts or

title: Cooked Pear Filling
steps: Peel, core, and halve the pears; slice each half into 1/2-inch-thick slices.
Melt butter in a sauté pan over high heat; add pears and toss to coat. Add sugar, then lemon juice.
Cook over high heat until pears are just tender. Flame with pear alcohol. Cool.

title: Espresso Simple Syrup
steps: Bring sugar and water to a boil. Stir in instant espresso, let cool and add brandy.

title: Lemon Confit
steps: In a medium saucepan, cover sliced lemons with ample cold water and gently bring to a boil. Drain and discard water. Repeat this step once more.
Meanwhile, prepare a syrup with the sucrose and water.
Combine blanched lemons and the syrup and gently cook over low heat, reducing until the lemon is slightly translucent.
Allow to cool and coarsely chop.

title: Lemon Topping
yield: Yield of filling, (one half sheet pan) 1,494 grams
steps: While the base is baking, prepare the topping. Be careful not to over mix the topping or it will have coarse-textured foam on the top when baked. In a large mixing bowl, whisk the eggs just to break them up. Whisk in the sugar, followed by the lemon juice and zest.

As soon as the base is baked, remove it from the oven and pour the topping into the sheet pan extender. Immediately return the pan to the oven and continue baking the squares in a deck oven until the topping is completely set and firm and has darkened significantly.
Cool completely, either in the refrigerator or at room temperature (ideally overnight), before
Dust with 10x or snow sugar before serving.

title: Magic Bar Filling
yield: Yield of filling (one half sheet pan) 1,249 grams
steps: Whisk together the first 4 ingredients.
Stir in pecans and chocolates. Pour mixture evenly into sheet pan extender. Bake at 350ºF until filling is set and golden brown.

title: Mandarin Coulis
steps: Place the mandarin purée into a small sauce pan and bring to a simmer.
Whisk together sucrose and pectin and whisk into the puree mixture. Resume a simmer remove from heat, and allow to cool.

title: Passion Fruit Coulis
steps: Place the passion fruit purée in a sauce pan and gently bring to a boil.
Combine sucrose and pectin and whisk into the puree mixture. Resume boil, remove from heat, and cool.

title: Pineapple Filling
steps: Purée cooled poached pineapples.
Place puree in a saucepan and cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Cook until mixture thickens slightly, approximately 10 minutes.
Dissolve the cornstarch in the cold water.
Make a liaison between the pineapple purée and cornstarch mixture, return to saucepan and bring to a boil. Be careful not to scorch pineapple. Chill until ready to use.

title: Pineapple Filling Poached Pineapple
steps: Place in a 2-quart saucepan and add the remaining ingredients. Add more water if necessary to cover the pineapple by 1 inch.

Cut a round of parchment paper to fit inside the saucepan and make a 1 it down so that the paper is below the surface of the liquid. This will keep the fruit submerged in the syrup while it is cooking so that it cooks evenly.
Bring the pineapple to a boil over medium heat and simmer for 5 minutes. Allow it to cool in the syrup.

title: Poached Pears
steps: Peel, halve and core the pears. Place in a rondeaux with the sugar and water and place over heat.

Add split and scraped vanilla bean, fresh herbs if using, and position the pears so that the cut sides are up, if possible. (If any oxidation occurs, it will occur on the flat side which will eventually be positioned downward in a dessert).
Cut a round of parchment or wax paper to fit inside the saucepan and make a 1 pears submerged in the syrup so they do not develop dark spots).
Bring the pears to a boil, then immediately turn heat down and simmer until pears are knife tender. Remove from heat; cool, store pears in the syrup and refrigerate.

title: Poached Pears
steps: Peel, halve and core the pears. Place in a rondeaux with the sugar and water and place over heat.

Add split and scraped vanilla bean and position the pears so that the cut sides are up, if possible. (If any oxidation occurs, it will occur on the flat side which will eventually be positioned downward in a dessert). the center. Cover the pears with the paper, pressing it down so that the paper is below the surface of the liquid. (This will keep the pears submerged in the syrup so they do not develop dark spots.)
Bring the pears to a boil, then immediately turn heat down and simmer until pears are knife tender. Remove from heat, cool for use.

title: Poached Pears in Caramel Syrup
steps: Combine the first sugar and lemon juice in a rondeaux or large pot stirring until the sugar resembles wet sand.
Place on medium heat; heat without stirring until the sugar starts to melt and begins to caramelize.
Begin to stir the sugar. Continue heating, stirring occasionally, until the caramel is a clear amber color creating a dry caramel
Dilute the caramel with the water. Add the remaining sugar and vanilla bean which has been cut and scraped.
Peel, halve and core the pears and place into the caramel solution.
Position the pears so that the cut sides are up, if possible (if any oxidation occurs, it will occur on the flat side which will eventually be positioned downward in a dessert).
Cut a round of parchment or wax paper to fit inside the saucepan and make a 1 pears submerged in the syrup so they do not develop dark spots).
Bring the pears to a boil and immediately turn heat down and simmer until pears are knife tender. Remove from heat; cool, store pears in the syrup and refrigerate.

title: Poached Pears in Wine
steps: Combine the water, wine and sugar in a large saucepan. Peel, halve and core the pears and immerse into the wine liquid.

Add vanilla bean and cinnamon sticks and fresh herbs if using and position the pears so that the cut sides are up, if possible. (If any oxidation occurs, it will occur on the flat side which will eventually be positioned downward in a dessert).
Cut a round of parchment or wax paper to fit inside the saucepan and make a 1 pears submerged in the syrup so they do not develop dark spots).
Bring the pears to a boil, then immediately turn heat down and simmer until pears are knife tender. Remove from heat; cool, store pears in the syrup and refrigerate.

title: Poached Pineapple
steps: Bring the pineapple to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer 5 to 10 minutes. Turn off the heat and allow the pineapple to cool in the syrup. The heat in the syrup will finish cooking the pineapple.
If the pineapple is to be kept more than a few hours before use, hold the pineapple in the syrup, covered, in the refrigerator.

title: Port Sauteed Pear Filling
steps: Peel, core, and halve the pears; slice each half into 1/2-inch-thick slices.
Melt butter in a sauté pan over high heat; add pears and toss to coat.
Add sugar, then lemon juice and port.
Cook over high heat until pears are just tender. Cool.

title: Prune Filling
steps: In a medium saucepan, cover prunes with water, add cinnamon sticks and simmer until tender.
Remove the cinnamon sticks, drain and purée.
Add in other ingredients and mix until smooth.

title: Raspberry Almond Filling
steps: Combine preserves and almond paste in mixing bowl and beat with paddle on low speed until smooth.

title: Raspberry Coulis
steps: Combine puree and fresh raspberries in a saucepan. Bring to a simmer.
Whisk in the invert sugar and allow to cool.

title: Roasted Pears and Dried Figs
steps: Place all ingredients in roasting pan and toss to mix.
Roast at 500 degrees, turning every 10 minutes (lower heat if charring, but some charring is good).

title: Roasted Plums and Ginger
steps: Toss the wedged plums with the sugar, rum, ground ginger, grated ginger, salt, lemon zest, and vanilla extract.
Spread the plums in a single layer on a parchment-lined sheet pan.
Roast at 400 F until softened and caramelized at the edges.
Cool before using.

title: Simple Syrup
steps: Combine sugar and water and bring to a boil. Cool.

title: Simple Syrup
steps: Combine ingredients and bring to a boil. Cool and add flavoring (extract or alcohol).

title: Stock Syrup
steps: Combine sugar and water in saucepan bring to a boil. Cool.

title: Stock Syrup
steps: Combine water and sugar and bring to a boil. Cool for use.

title: Strawberry Salad

ingredients.name: strawberries, rinsed, stems removed and quartered

steps: Rinse, hull and quarter strawberries.
In a bowl, toss with sugar, balsamic vinegar and herbs.
Chill for one hour, covered in refrigerator. Toss at least once while in the refrigerator to ensure the strawberries are well coated.
Serve, garnished with sprigs of fresh herbs.

title: Summer Berry Coulis Insert
steps: In a small bowl, whisk to combine the sugar and pectin.
Bring purees and vanilla bean to a boil, then whisk in pectin/sugar mixture.
Cook over medium heat until well thickened.
Remove from heat and whisk in lime juice.
Portion into small dome flexi molds and freeze.

title: Walnut Filling
steps: Bring brown sugar, butter and honey to a boil in heavy saucepan over medium heat.
Boil for 3 minutes. Remove from heat.
Stir in walnuts.

title: Zabaione Filling
steps: Bloom the gelatin sheets in cold water until soft.
Combine the egg yolks, sugar, and Marsala in a bowl set over simmering water.
Whisk constantly until pale, thick, and ribboning (classic zabaione stage).
Whisk in the espresso powder and brewed coffee.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the hot zabaione until fully melted.
Cool the zabaione to room temperature, stirring occasionally.
Fold in the mascarpone until smooth and homogeneous.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peaks and fold into the mascarpone-zabaione mixture.
Chill until set.

title: Gruyere Flavoring
steps: Add seasoning and cheese to Flour-Based Soufflé at step 5. Note: Do not add sugar to whites in Flour-Based Soufflé in step 8; use grated parmesan in place of sugar for preparing ramekins.
notes: Breadcrumbs can be used in place of grated parmesan for coating the ramekins, if preferred.

title: Lemon Flavoring
steps: Add zest, juice and rum to Flour-Based Soufflé at step 5.

title: Lime Flavoring
steps: Add zest, juice and rum to Flour-Based Soufflé at step 5.

title: Orange Flavoring
steps: Add zest, juice and Grand Marnier to Flour-Based Soufflé at step 5.

title: Banana Citrus Sorbet
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.
Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the remaining sucrose (2), dextrose, and invert sugar and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat.
Chill and allow syrup to mature.
Combine measured base syrup with fruit purées and juice and briefly blend with an immersion blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.
Transfer finished sorbet to blast chiller to harden at minimum of -18˚C/0˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Banana Citrus Sorbet
steps: Whisk the first measurement of sugar (1) and stabilizer.

title: Basil Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, dextrose, invert sugar, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.

Blend the basil into a portion of the base mix using a high speed blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Basque Tart, Olive Oil Ice Cream, Almond, Apricot Basque Tart Dough
steps: Combine the butter, sucrose and salt in the bowl of the stand mixer; cream the mixture with the paddle attachment. Add the almond flour, and then slowly add egg yolks one at a time until mixed, scraping down the bowl between each addition. Lastly add flour and baking powder. Mix until just combined.

Refrigerate and rest a minimum one hour. Roll or sheet the dough to a thickness of 3mm (1/8"); cut equal number of discs with a 12cm (4 ½”) and 8cm (3') and line prepared 8cm (3”) rings. Deposit vanilla custard and then top with the smaller disc and seal.

title: Biscuit Coulant Chocolat Coffee Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk and ground coffee in a sauce pan. Whisk in the dry milk to rehydrate and add first measurement of sucrose (1), glucose, and invert sugar. Bring to a boil. Remove from heat and allow to stand five minutes. Strain.
Meanwhile, combine the second measurement of sucrose (2), stabilizer, and instant coffee. Whisk into egg yolks.
Temper hot milk into yolk mixture. Return to low heat and cook, stirring constantly to 85˚C/185˚F.

title: Black Currant Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, glucose, invert sugar and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Thoroughly blend the base mix and black currant purée. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Blackberry Sorbet
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.
Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the remaining sucrose (2), dextrose, and glucose and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat.
Chill and allow syrup to mature.
Combine the base syrup with fruit purée and briefly blend with an immersion blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.
Transfer finished sorbet to blast chiller to harden at minimum of -18˚C/0˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Bulk Sorbet Base (Acidic Fruit)
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.
Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the remaining sucrose (2) and dextrose and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat.
Chill and allow syrup to mature at least 4 hours.
Combine measured base syrup with fruit purée (and additional water, if necessary) and briefly blend with an immersion blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at

title: Bulk Sorbet Base (Sweet Fruit)
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.
Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the remaining sucrose (2), dextrose, and glucose and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat.
Chill and allow syrup to mature at least 4 hours.
Combine measured base syrup with fruit purée (and additional water, if necessary) and briefly blend with an immersion blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at

title: Caramel Crepe Cake, Roasted Pineapple, Banana-Citrus Sorbet Crepe
steps: Combine and sift sucrose, flour, and salt. Whisk together the whole eggs and yolks.
Add dry ingredients in to the eggs in two additions, mixing just until incorporated. Slowly add the vegetable oil, followed by the milk and rum.
Chill and allow to rest for several hours; strain if necessary.
Cook one-ounce portioned crepe batter in small non-stick pans. Cool, wrap, and reserve for assembly.

title: Caramel Ice Cream
steps: Place the sugar and water to cover in a medium sauce pan. Cook over medium heat to a medium dark caramel. Remove from heat and gradually add the cream.
Whisk in the milk and nonfat milk powder and return to medium heat. Add the, dextrose, invert sugar, stabilizer, and salt. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Champagne Granite
steps: Combine the sucrose and water and bring to a boil.
Add the sparkling wine (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer.

title: Chocolate Ice Cream “A”
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, glucose, stabilizer, and cocoa. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Chocolate Ice Cream “B”
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, invert sugar, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat, add the chocolate and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Chocolate Peanut Tart Praline Citrus Sorbet
steps: Whisk together the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.

Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in stabilizer mixture, then the remaining sucrose (2) and glucose, bring to a boil for about 30 seconds. Remove from heat and chill; allow syrup to mature for at least 4 hours.
Combine juices, praline paste and syrup and process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.

title: Cinnamon Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose (1), dextrose, invert sugar, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to

title: Cocoa Sorbet
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.

Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the milk powder, cocoa powder, remaining sucrose (2), and invert sugar and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat. Blend in the unsweetened chocolate with an immersion blender.
Chill and allow syrup to mature at least 4 hours.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.
Transfer finished sorbet to blast chiller to harden at minimum of -18˚C/0˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Coconut Sorbet
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.

Transfer finished sorbet to blast chiller to harden at minimum of -18˚C/0˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed

Add the coffee and extract (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer.

title: Coffee Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, dextrose, invert sugar, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat add the coffee extract; homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Custard Base (10% Milk Fat)
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the dextrose, and invert sugar. Meanwhile, whisk the sucrose and stabilizer into the egg yolks.
Carefully temper the warm base into the egg yolk mixture and continue to heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Dark Chocolate Cremeux Coconut Sorbet
steps: Whisk the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.

Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in stabilizer mixture, then remaining sucrose (2) and glucose powder, bring to just to a boil. Remove from heat and chill; allow syrup to mature for at least 4 hours if possible.
Combine purée and syrup and process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -

title: Earl Grey Tea Granite
steps: Combine the sucrose and water and bring to a boil.
Add the infusion, orange zest, and lemon juice (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer.
notes: *For the tea infusion, infuse 12g loose Earl Grey tea into 800g of boiled water.

title: Exotic Fruit Granite
steps: Combine the sucrose and water and bring to a boil.
Add the fruit purées (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer.

title: Frozen Chocolate Parfait
steps: Make a Crème Anglaise by placing the milk in a sauce pan and bring just to boil; meanwhile, whisk together the egg yolks and sucrose. Temper the milk into the yolk mixture and return to low heat and gently cook to 85°C/185°F or until nappe.
Remove from heat and transfer to the bowl of a stand mixer and whip until cool.
Fold a small amount of whipped cream into the melted chocolate, followed by half of the whipped Anglaise mixture. Gently fold in the remaining Anglaise mixture and the remaining whipped cream.
Transfer to prepared molds and freeze.

title: Frozen Ginger Parfait, Passion Fruit Sorbet, Meringue Frozen Ginger Parfait
steps: Place the ginger in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil and drain.
Divide the ginger evenly between the cream and the milk and allow to infuse, chilled, at least 12 hours if possible
Transfer the milk mixture to a saucepan and bring to a boil. Meanwhile, combine the egg yolks and sugar in a medium mixing bowl.
Strain and then temper the hot milk into the yolk mixture, return to low heat, and continue to cook just until slightly thickened. Add the gelatin.
Transfer the anglaise to a mixer bowl and whip at high speed until cool to the touch.
Strain the cream and whip to soft peaks. Fold into the whipped anglaise and deposit into low 8cm (3”) ring molds. Freeze.

title: Grapefruit Tarragon Granite
steps: Combine the sucrose and water and bring to a boil. Remove from heat, add the tarragon, cover, and infuse for 15 minutes.
Add the grapefruit and lemon juice (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer.

title: Hazelnut Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, dextrose, glucose, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat, add hazelnut paste and salt; homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Hazelnut Praline Semifreddo
steps: Heat the first measurement of cream (1) until just warmed; add the praline paste and whisk until smooth.
Combine the egg whites and sucrose in a KitchenAid bowl and heat over a double boiler, whisking constantly, to 55°C/130°F. Whip to medium peaks and cool.
Fold one third of meringue into the praline cream mixture before folding in remaining whites, folding until thoroughly combined. until smooth. Fold in the whipped cream.
Transfer to prepared molds and freeze.

title: Honey Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the honey, and glucose. Meanwhile, whisk the sucrose and stabilizer into the egg yolks.
Carefully temper the warm base into the egg yolk mixture and continue to heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Honey Pistachio Parfait
steps: Prepare an Italian-style meringue by bringing the honey to a boil and pouring into the whipping egg whites.
When cool, fold in the whipped cream and crushed nuts.

Transfer to prepared molds. Freeze. Suggested combinations of components for final dessert presentations:  Vanilla Ice Cream, Caramel Ice Cream, Chocolate Sauce, Tuile  Chocolate Ice Cream, Vanilla Ice Cream, Caramel Sauce, Caramelized Rice  Passion Fruit Sorbet, Honey Ice Cream, Passion Fruit Coulis, Caramelized Pistachio  Pistachio Ice Cream, Raspberry Ice Cream, Raspberry Coulis, Meringue Buttons  Black Currant Ice Cream, Basil Ice Cream, Lemon Curd, Meringue Buttons  Vanilla Ice Cream, Cinnamon Ice Cream, Red Wine Caramel, Caramelized Rice  Mango Ice Cream, Coconut Sorbet, Pineapple Coconut Anglaise, Pizzelle  Coffee Ice Cream, Hazelnut Ice Cream, Chocolate Shell, Pizzelle

title: Honey Semifreddo

ingredients.name: heavy cream whipped to soft peaks

steps: Prepare an Italian meringue by bringing the honey just to a boil and pouring into the whipping egg whites.

Fold in whipped cream and pipe into to prepared molds, filling roughly halfway. Insert one piece of the molded lemon sorbet and top with additional semifreddo and seal the mold with a disc of the almond sponge. Freeze.

title: Honey-Lemon Semifreddo Lemon Sorbet
steps: Whisk together first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.

Heat the water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in stabilizer mixture, then remaining sucrose (2), milk powder, glucose, and invert sugar; bring just to a boil. Remove from heat and chill; allow syrup to mature for at least 4 hours if possible.
Combine the lemon juice and syrup and process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.
Pipe the lemon sorbet into small hemisphere molds and place in blast freezer.

title: Lemon Granite
steps: Combine the sucrose and water and bring to a boil.
Add the lemon juice (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer.

title: Malt Milk Chocolate Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the malt powder. Meanwhile, whisk the sucrose and stabilizer into the egg yolks.
Carefully temper the warm base into the egg yolk mixture and continue to heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and add the chocolates; homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Mandarin Sorbet
steps: Whisk together the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.

Heat water to 50˚C/ 120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, then the remaining sucrose (2) and glucose powder and bring to a boil for about 30 seconds. Remove from heat, chill, and allow syrup to mature.
Combine syrup and purée and process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -

title: Mango Cheese Parfait
steps: Bloom the gelatin in full measurement of cold water. Gently heat to dissolve when needed.

In a mixer bowl, combine the sucrose, salt, and butter, creaming the mixture well. Slowly add the cream cheese, occasionally scraping down the sides of the bowl. Follow with the addition of the crème fraiche, mango purée, lime juice, and dissolved gelatin.
Fold into the whipped cream and portion into 5cm (2”) ring molds arranged on a flat acetate-lined sheet pan; top each mold with the previously baked coconut sablée. Freeze.

title: Mango Cheese Parfait Coconut Sablee
steps: In a mixer bowl fitted with the paddle attachment, cream together butter, coconut oil, sucrose, and dry coconut.
Slowly add egg yolks, followed by the rum, scraping down the bowl after each addition.
Add flour, salt and baking powder, mixing just until combined. Chill.
Roll or sheet dough thin and cut 4cm discs. Transfer to a parchment or Silpat-lined sheet pan and bake at 150˚C/300˚F for 10-12 minutes.

title: Mango Ice Cream
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, glucose, invert sugar and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Thoroughly blend the base mix and mango purée. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Melon Sorbet
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.
Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the remaining sucrose (2) and glucose and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat.
Chill and allow syrup to mature.
Combine base syrup with fruit purée and briefly blend with an immersion blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.

title: Olive Oil Ice Cream
steps: Combine the sucrose and ice cream stabilizer and set aside.
Combine the milk, cream, and milk powder and begin heating.
Add the sucrose and stabilizer, glucose, invert sugar and egg yolk at Heat base to 85˚C/185˚F and hold for two minutes.
Cool base to 4˚C/40˚F. At approx. 65˚C/150˚F add olive oil and homogenize base with a handheld immersion blender for approximately 45 seconds. Mature base at 4˚C/40˚F for 12-16 hours if possible.
Process in a batch freezer to temperature of -5˚C/23˚F and extract. Transfer finished ice cream to blast chiller to harden at minimum of -18˚C/0˚F.

title: Orange Liquor Souffle Glace
steps: Place the egg yolks in a stand mixer fitted with the whip attachment; meanwhile, combine the sucrose and water in a sauce pan and cook to 115°C/240°F while whipping the egg yolks.
Remove the syrup from heat and slowly pour into the whipping yolks; continue to whip until cool.
Fold into the whip cream; fold in the orange zest and Grand Marnier. Transfer to prepared molds and freeze.

title: Passion Fruit Sorbet
steps: Combine the first measurement of sucrose (1) and stabilizer.
Heat water to 50˚C/120˚F. Whisk in the stabilizer mixture, and then the remaining sucrose (2), and glucose and bring just to a boil. Remove from heat.
Chill and allow syrup to mature.
Combine base syrup with fruit purée and briefly blend with an immersion blender. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.
Transfer finished sorbet to blast chiller to harden at minimum of -18˚C/0˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

Remove from heat, add pistachio pastes and salt; homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours if possible.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

steps: Combine the sucrose and water and bring to a boil.
Add the base fruit and lemon juice (and gelatin, if used); strain.
Pour into shallow pans and begin to freeze, stirring every fifteen minutes with a fork.
Once the proper ice consistency is achieved, place granité in covered containers and store in the freezer

Thoroughly blend the base mix and raspberry purée. Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Vanilla Ice Cream (6% Fat)
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, cream, and vanilla in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, dextrose, invert sugar, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.
Remove from heat and homogenize with an immersion blender. Chill in an ice water bath. Allow the mix to mature at least 12 hours.
Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F.

title: WHITE BASE (14% MILK FAT) g oz
steps: Place the milk, nonfat milk powder, and cream in a sauce pan over medium heat. Add the sucrose, dextrose, invert sugar, and stabilizer. Heat, while whisking, to 85°C/185°F.

Process in batch freezer; extract the mix at -5˚C/23˚F. Alternatively, transfer to PacoJet canisters and freeze; process as needed.

title: Yuzu Parfait
steps: In a heavy saucepan, whisk together the eggs and sucrose, and then add the yuzu purée.
On medium heat, bring mixture just to a boil, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and add the gelatin.
Allow the mixture to cool 35˚C/95˚F and emulsify the butter into the base.
Once cooled, fold into whipped cream.

Pipe the mousse into silicon ‘stone' molds, filling approximately halfway. Insert a piece of the frozen pistachio cream into each mold and top with additional mousse to fill the mold completely. Freeze

title: Yuzu Parfait Pistachio Cream
steps: In a saucepan, combine milk, cream, and sucrose. Bring to a boil.
Remove from heat and carefully whisk in egg yolks. Return to low heat and cook, stirring, to 85ºC/185ºF.
Remove from heat, add gelatin, and blend in the pistachio paste. Strain through a chinois.
Portion into small hemisphere molds and place in blast freezer.

title: Apfelstrudel (Apple Strudel)
steps: Peel, halve and core the apples. Cut each half in 5 or 6 slices toss with the remaining filling ingredients, except the walnuts, in a wide pan. Cover and cook over medium heat about 15 minutes, until the apples are swimming in water. Uncover, lower heat and cook until the juices have evaporated, about 20 minutes. Stir in the walnuts and cool the filling.
Brush melted butter on to the stretched strudel dough, reserving a little butter for the outside of the strudel. Sprinkle the dough with the narrow ends of the dough.
Roll the strudel, folding in the ends as you roll.

Transfer the strudel to a half-sheet pan lined with parchment. Bend the strudel into a horseshoe shape. Butter the outside of the strudel and bake it at 400 degrees about 40 minutes. If the strudel is beginning to take on too much color, lower the temperature to 350 degrees and cover the strudel loosely with aluminum foil.
Cool the strudel on the pan, and dust the strudel lightly with confectioners' sugar before serving.
notes: For the dough

title: Apple Strip
steps: Roll the puff pastry into a long strip.
Spread the Almond Frangipane evenly down the center of the strip, leaving a border.
Arrange sliced apples over the frangipane.
Fold and crimp the pastry edges up around the filling to enclose it.
Brush the pastry with melted butter.
Bake at 375 F until the pastry is puffed and golden and the apples are tender.
Brush warm apricot glaze over the top to finish.
Cool before slicing.

title: Baklava
steps: Grind walnuts, almonds, sugar and cinnamon together, until finely processed.
Open package of phyllo dough, and cut entire package in half through the width (making two short rectangles).
Brush one half-piece lightly with melted clarified butter.
Spread 2 tablespoons of filling evenly over phyllo and drizzle the filling with clarified butter.
Fold in edges to cover filling, and then roll. Brush top with clarified butter.

title: Bourbon Mille Feuille, Lemon, Vanilla Ice Cream Vanilla Pastry Cream
steps: Whisk the corn starch into the first measurement of milk (1) in a medium- sized mixing bowl, followed by the first measurement of sucrose (1), the whole eggs, egg yolks and salt.
Combine the remaining milk (2), sucrose (2), and vanilla in a sauce pan; bring to a boil.

Remove from heat and temper the warm milk mixture into the corn starch mixture. Return to heat and cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, just to a boil. Remove from heat (remove the scraped vanilla pod) and whisk

Prepare Detrempe (12 points)
Prepare Beurrage (12 points)
Form Paton (12 points)

title: Classic Puff Pastry Dough (Detrempe)
steps: Sift the all-purpose flour and cake flour together; add the salt and sugar and combine.
Rub the soft butter into the flour mixture until evenly distributed and sandy in texture.
Make a well in the center and add the very cold water all at once.
Mix just until the dough comes together into a smooth, cohesive mass; stop as soon as it clears the bowl and do not overwork or develop the gluten.
Turn the dough out and gather into a smooth rectangle or disk.
Wrap tightly and refrigerate at least 30 to 60 minutes to relax and firm before rolling in the butter block for lamination.

title: Coques
steps: Allow dough to rest in the refrigerator about 1 hour.

title: Croissant Dough
steps: Add all ingredients except butter together in bowl.
Mix on low speed with dough hook attachment until it all comes together.
Knead on floured table a little but not too much. Shape into square roughly 8 X 8 and wrap in plastic.
Let rest in refrigerator. Whole Wheat Croissant Use Either Starter or Water NOT BOTH

title: Danish Dough
steps: Determine the correct size of dough for tête mold by rounding a piece of
Oil small classic brioche molds well.
1-2-3-4 dough shaping method. Cover with one hand and roll into a ball.
Turn on side and roll into width of hand. Dip the side of your hand in flour
Stand the dough upright with the head on top.
Use first two fingers from each hand and place them under the head.
Place in prepared molds. Place in proof box for about an hour until double
Egg wash tops and bake.

title: Galaktoboureko
steps: Heat ¾ of milk in sauce pan with vanilla bean
Combine remaining milk with semolina, sugar and eggs and set aside.
When milk and vanilla come to a boil, make a liaison with milk and semolina mixture.
Continue to cook mixture until it is thickened.

title: Machine Puff Pastry 20-Quart Machine Dough (Detrempe)
steps: Combine the all-purpose flour, cake flour, salt, and sugar in the bowl of a 20-quart mixer fitted with the hook attachment.
Add the soft butter and mix on low speed until worked evenly into the dry ingredients and the mixture looks sandy.
Add the very cold water and mix on low speed just until the dough comes together into a smooth, cohesive mass and clears the bowl.
Stop mixing as soon as the dough comes together; do not overmix or allow the dough to warm.
Remove the dough, divide, and shape into rectangles.
Wrap tightly and refrigerate until firm before laminating with the butter block.

title: Mille Feuille Layers
steps: Roll out Puff pastry layers thinly and sprinkle lightly with 10X. Bake between parchment and straight half sheet pans until fully baked.

title: Mozzarella and Prosciutto Turnovers
steps: Stir together the cheese, prosciutto, parsley and pepper.
Egg-wash dough lightly and divide filling among disks of dough, placing it off- center.
Fold over disks of dough and seal well with fingertips. Seal with tines of fork, dipping fork in flour to prevent sticking.
Egg-wash tops of turnovers and bake at 375 degrees about 20 minutes until well colored and crisp.
Cool the pastries on the pans and reheat for service.

title: Optional Danish Fillings Pastry Cream
steps: Dissolve cornstarch in 1/8 of the milk.
Combine the remaining milk with the sugar in a rondeau or saucepan; bring to a boil.
Whisk whole eggs, then yolks into cornstarch mixture.
Pour 1/3 of the boiling milk into the egg mixture, whisking. Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil.
Whisk the egg mixture into the boiling milk, whisking until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil. Boil, whisking, about 1 minute.
Remove from heat and beat in the butter and vanilla.
Pour the cream into a stainless steel pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface, and poke holes to vent. Chill immediatel

title: Paillettes
steps: Roll the puff pastry into a rectangle about 1/8 inch thick.
Combine the grated parmesan and paprika.
Sprinkle the cheese mixture evenly over one half of the dough.
Fold the other half of the dough over the cheese to enclose it, and roll gently to seal and adhere the layers.
Cut into strips about 1/2 inch wide.
Twist each strip several times and place on a parchment-lined sheet pan, pressing the ends down to secure.
Bake at 375 F until golden brown and crisp.
Cool on the pan before serving.

title: Palmiers
steps: Cut into 1/4-inch-thick slices. Dip surface that will go against pan in sugar.
Bake at 375 degrees for about 15 minutes, or until golden brown.

title: Parmesan and Paprika Palmiers
steps: Strew evenly with cheese, salt and paprika.
Fold each long end 1/3 of the way in toward the center. Repeat folds, folding to center, and then fold in half at center line. Wrap in plastic and chill until firm.

title: Plum and Cheese Strudel

ingredients.name: red plums, rinsed, halved, pitted & sliced

steps: Trim the crusts from the bread, soak it in the milk, and force it through a strainer. Force the cheese through a strainer over the bread. Set aside. Beat the cream cheese to lighten it then beat in the sugar and the yolks. Stir in the farmer cheese, the bread, and all other ingredients except the plums.
Stretch the strudel dough. Brush melted butter on to the strudel dough, reserving a little butter for the outside of the strudel. Sprinkle the dough with the breadcrumbs.
Spread the filling over 1/3 of the dough and scatter on the plums. Roll dough up, taking care to fold in the sides to prevent the filling from leaking out.
Transfer the strudel to a half-sheet pan lined with parchment. Bend the strudel into a horseshoe shape. Butter the outside of the strudel and
Bake it at 375 degrees about 40 minutes. If the strudel is beginning to
notes: For the dough

title: Praline Feuillete
steps: Melt the semisweet and milk chocolate together gently over a bain-marie, stirring until smooth.
Stir in the praline paste and salt until fully smooth and combined.
Fold in the Feuilletine flakes just until evenly coated, taking care not to crush them.
Divide the mixture between sheets of parchment or acetate and spread thinly into two 10-inch discs.
Chill until firm and set before using.

title: Quick Puff Pastry Dough

ingredients.name: lemon juice or distilled white vinegar

steps: Combine the flours in a bowl and stir well to mix. Rub 55 grams of butter into the flours until it disappears and is cool and powdery. Set aside.

title: Rectangular Mille Feuille

ingredients.name: heavy cream, whipped to soft peaks

steps: Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes, turning dough often and watching its progress. Remove dough from oven and cool between the pans to prevent warping. Assembly
Stir the cold pastry cream to soften slightly. Fold the whipped cream into the pastry cream. and reserving scraps.

Place first strip on cardboard; spread with 1/3 of the pastry cream. Top with second strip; spread with another 1/3 pastry cream; top with third strip and spread with remaining pastry cream. Top with last strip. Place cardboard over stacked pastry; press down to adhere interior together and to ensure that it is straight.
Cover top of pastry with vanilla fondant and finish with chocolate. Spread sides evenly with any pastry cream that flows out; adhere crumbs to sides.

title: Roquefort and Spinach Strudel
steps: Combine the milk and butter in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Add the flour all at once and stir vigorously over low heat until the mixture forms a smooth ball and pulls away from the sides of the pan.
Transfer to a mixer and beat on low speed briefly to cool slightly.
Add the eggs gradually, beating well after each addition until the paste is smooth and glossy.
Season the paste with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
Spread the paste thinly and evenly on a parchment-lined sheet pan.
Bake at 375 F until set, dry, and lightly golden.
While still warm and pliable, scatter sautéed spinach and crumbled Roquefort evenly over the surface.
Roll up jelly-roll style to enclose the filling; place seam-side down.
Return to the oven briefly to set and heat through.
Cool slightly, then slice and serve.

title: Sacristains
steps: Roll the puff pastry into a rectangle about 1/8 inch thick.
Combine the ground almonds and sugar.
Sprinkle the almond-sugar mixture evenly over the dough, pressing gently to adhere.
Cut into strips about 1/2 inch wide.
Twist each strip several times and place on a parchment-lined sheet pan.
Bake at 375 F until golden brown and crisp.
Cool on the pan before serving.

title: Smoked Salmon Mille Feuille
steps: Dock the dough well and place on a paper-lined pan. Cover dough with another piece of paper and pan and bake at 350 degrees about 30 minutes, turning dough often and watching its progress as it becomes golden. Remove dough from oven and cool between the pans to prevent warping.
While the dough is baking, prepare the filling. Beat together cream cheese and butter until smooth and light. Beat in lemon zest, salt and pepper. Stir in chives. each piece with half the filling.

Arrange the salmon to cover one of the pieces, and then invert the remaining piece onto the first piece, covering the salmon. Slide onto a pan and press to adhere. Refrigerate until firm, about 1 hour.

title: Spanikopita

ingredients.name: feta cheese, broken into large pieces

steps: Wash, drain and chop spinach, scallions and dill.
Sauté spinach in olive oil, until wilted. Add scallions and dill and continue to sauté briefly. Cool and drain off excess liquid.
Place spinach, scallions and dill in a large bowl and add the ricotta, eggs and broken feta. Stir well to mix.

Open package of phyllo dough, and cut entire package in half through the length (making two long rectangles). Brush one rectangular piece lightly with melted clarified butter and place second sheet on top, brushing again with clarified butter.

Place approximately one ounce of filling in lower right hand corner of phyllo dough. Fold dough in half to cover filling and make a long narrow rectangle. Fold filling upward twice, and then begin folding as you would a flag, creating triangular pieces.
Arrange on parchment lined pan, brush with melted clarified butter and bake at 350 until golden.

title: Strudelteig Strudel Dough
steps: Combine the flour and salt in a mixing bowl. Beat the eggs and oil with a whisk. Add enough warm water to make a total of 300 grams.
Stir the liquid into the flour with a rubber spatula, making sure that no flour sticks to the side of the bowl. The dough should be fairly soft.

Turn the dough out on a lightly floured surface and begin to develop the gluten. Pick the dough up and slam it against the surface forcefully. Fold the dough back on itself, and pick it up again from the side, detaching it from the surface with a bench scraper. Repeat this motion 100 times, until the dough is smooth and elastic.

Oil a small bowl, place the dough in the bowl and turn the dough over, so that the top is oiled. Press plastic wrap against the surface of the dough and rest the dough at room temperature about 1 hour, longer if possible. Refrigerate the dough if it is to be held more than half a day. If the dough was refrigerated, let it come to room temperature before proceeding.

title: Topfen Strudel
steps: Trim the crusts from the bread, soak it in the milk, and force it through a strainer. Force the cheese through a strainer over the bread. Set aside. Bring the raisins to a boil in water and drain them. Set aside. Beat the eggs with the sugar until light and stir in the cheese, the bread, raisins, and all other ingredients.
Butter and crumb the dough and spread the filling over 1/3 of the strudel dough and roll it up, taking care to fold in the sides to prevent the filling from leaking out.

Transfer the strudel to a half-sheet pan lined with parchment. Bend the strudel into a horseshoe shape. Butter the outside of the strudel and bake it at 375 degrees about 40 minutes. If the strudel is beginning to take on too much color, lower the temperature to 350 degrees and cover the strudel loosely with aluminum foil.
Cool the strudel on the pan, and dust the strudel lightly with confectioners' sugar before serving
notes: For the dough

title: Tresses
steps: Allow dough to rest in the refrigerator about 1 hour.
Cut into ¾ -inch wide strips. Braid 3 at a time.
Place 1 ½ inches apart on paper-lined pan.
Bake at 375 degrees for about 15 minutes, or until golden brown.

title: Tyrolean Strudel
steps: Bring the milk to a boil with half the sugar in a non-aluminum saucepan. Whisk the yolks in a bowl and beat in the remaining sugar. Sift in the flour and stir it in. Beat 1/3 of the boiling milk into the yolk mixture, and then return the remaining milk to a boil. Beat the yolk mixture into the boiling milk and continue beating until the cream thickens and returns to a boil. Cook the cream about 1 minute, beating constantly. Remove from heat and beat in the butter and vanilla.
Pour the cream into a clean, dry bowl and press plastic wrap against the surface. Refrigerate until cold.
Stir the dried fruit and walnuts into the cold pastry cream. Beat the egg whites until they hold a soft peak and fold into the filling.

Brush the strudel dough with melted butter, reserving a little butter for the outside of the strudel. Sprinkle the dough with the breadcrumbs. Spread the filling in half the dough and roll up the strudel, folding in the ends while rolling.
Transfer the strudel to a half-sheet pan lined with parchment. Bend the
notes: For the dough

title: Baked Meringue
steps: Begin whipping egg whites in mixer on medium speed. Slowly add sucrose in a few additions until firm peaks are achieved.
Transfer to a pastry fitted with a medium-sized straight plain tip and pipe small buttons onto a Silpat-lined sheet pan and dry in an oven at 93˚C/200˚F.
Store in an air-tight container. ASSEMBLY grams milk chocolate décor candied ginger, diced

Unmold each ginger parfait onto a cooled sucrée disc. Top with a scoop or quenelle of the passion fruit sorbet, meringue, candied ginger, and milk chocolate décor. Finish the plate with the passion fruit caramel.

title: Chocolate Meringue
steps: Combine egg whites and salt in mixing bowl.
Whip on medium speed until risen in volume and whites begin to hold shape around the whisk.
Add the sugar in a slow stream, beating until desired peak.
Fold in the combined and sifted confectioners' sugar and cocoa powder by hand.
Pipe as instructed. Bake at 300 F.

title: Chocolate Meringue
steps: Combine egg whites and salt in mixing bowl.
Whip on medium speed until risen in volume and whites begin to hold shape around the whisk.
Add the sugar in a slow stream, beating until stiff peak.
Sift the 10X and cocoa powder and fold in by hand.

Pipe in very thin lines (quickly from a distance from the tray to ensure thin lines) utilizing an 801plain tip. Bake at 300 F. until dried. Once meringue is baked and cooled, utilize on the side of the entremet.
notes: Utilize chocolate decorations or other decorations for the top.

title: Coconut Souffle
steps: Whisk flour into 100 grams of the coconut milk and set aside.
Combine the remaining coconut milk with the sugar in a small saucepan; bring to a boil.

Whisk whole eggs and yolks into flour milk mixture. Temper, by pouring 1/3 of the boiling milk into the egg mixture, whisking. Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the egg mixture into the boiling milk, whisking until the (pastry) cream thickens and returns to a boil.
Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute. Remove from heat and beat in the unsweetened coconut.
Pour the cream into a stainless steel pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately over ice or as instructed.
When cooled, whip whites until frothy and gradually add the remaining sugar; continue whipping until soft peaks are formed.
Fold whites into coconut pastry cream mixture and place into prepared molds.
Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 20 minutes.

title: Cofee & Pecan Petit Gateaux Pecan Dacquoise Pecan Caramel Coffee Crème Coffee Mousse Pecan Dacquoise
steps: Grind pecans and flour together in a Robot Coupe until fine. Combine with the almond flour and sugar (1) and set aside.
Make an ordinary meringue with the egg whites and sugar (2) and whip to firm peaks.
Gently fold the dry ingredients into the meringue.
Spread onto parchment lined sheet pan and dust with confectio10X.
Bake at 375 F. for 8-10 minutes or until golden brown and set.
Once cooled, invert onto parchment lined half sheet pan and trim to fit in a sheet pan extender.

title: Flavoring for Flour-Based Souffle Mocha Flavoring
steps: Add espresso and brandy to Flour-Based Soufflé at step 5.

title: Flour-Based Chocolate Souffle
steps: Bring milk to a boil.
Separately, mix butter and flour to a paste.
Add paste to boiling milk over low flame gradually and stir until base thickens and returns to a boil.
Remove from heat. Turn into a bowl. (If not using immediately cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate. Warm over simmering water to 100 degrees before using.)
While stirring, add chocolate all at once TO MELT COMPETELY.
Gradually add yolks to mixture, then liqueur.
Allow base to cool.
Whip egg whites to a very light peak. Add sugar gradually while continuing to whip to a soft peak, (making a French meringue).
Stir 1/4 of the whipped whites into the base.
Fold in the remaining 3/4 of the whites.
Place batter in eight 4- or 5-ounce buttered and sugared ramekins and bake at 400 degrees for approximately 15 minutes.
notes: Note: This soufflé recipe is part of your practical exam on Lesson 23

title: Flour-Based Souffle
steps: Bring milk to a boil.
Mix butter and flour to a paste.
Add paste to milk over low flame gradually and stir until base thickens and returns to a boil.
Remove from heat. Turn into a bowl. (If not using immediately cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate. Warm over simmering water to 100 degrees before using.)
While stirring, gradually add FLAVORING (as assigned in the next page) to mixture.
Gradually add yolks to mixture.
Allow base to cool.
Whip egg whites to a very light peak. For sweet soufflés add sugar slowly while continuing to whip to a soft peak; for savory soufflés, whip whites without sugar.
Stir 1/4 of the whipped whites into the base.
Fold in the remaining 3/4 of the whites.
Place batter in eight 4- or 5-ounce buttered and sugared ramekins for sweet soufflés or buttered and Parmesan cheese ramekins for savory soufflés. Bake at 400 degrees for approximately 15 minutes.

title: French (Ordinary) Meringue
steps: Combine egg whites and salt in mixing bowl.
Whip on medium speed until risen in volume and whites begin to hold shape around the whisk.
Add the sugar in a slow stream, beating until desired peak.
Fold in the second sugar by hand. Pipe as instructed. Bake at 225 F.

title: French Macarons
steps: Pulse the almond flour, 10x and salt in the bowl of a food processor 4 times for 4 seconds each. Sift.
Whisk the powdered egg whites and granulated sugar in an electric mixer bowl. Whisk in the aged egg whites, and whisk at medium-high speed until glossy stiff peaks form (about 11 minutes).
Fold the dry ingredients into the meringue until the mixture falls back into the bowl, with no stiffness, in one continuous drip. Add the coloring until just incorporated.

title: Honey Meringue
steps: Place the honey in a small sauce pan and begin to heat.

Place egg whites and sugar in a KitchenAid mixer bowl with whip attachment and start to mix on medium speed. Bring honey to boil and continue cooking to ; remove from heat and slowly pour the honey into egg whites. Continue to whip until cool and stiff.

Transfer the meringue into a pastry bag fitted with a medium plain tip and pipe spiked mounds onto each of the unmolded honey parfait hemispheres. Return to freezer. Lightly caramelize the meringue with a blowtorch before serving.

title: Italian Meringue Buttercream
steps: After meringue is whipped until cold and stiff, switch to the paddle attachment of the mixer if desired.
Add the butter while continuing to beat until all the butter is incorporated.
Beat until smooth.
Flavor the Italian Meringue buttercream at this point by adding flavorings of your choice.

title: Italian Meringue Italian Meringue
steps: Pour water and 340 grams of sugar into a small saucepan. Stir to combine.
Place the pan over low to medium heat.
Meanwhile, place the egg whites and salt in a Kitchen Aid bowl.
When the sugar syrup comes to a boil, begin whipping the egg whites. Whip whites until white and frothy. Gradually add the 110 grams of sugar to the whipping egg whites.

When the sugar syrup reaches 240° F, add sugar syrup in a slow stream to the whites while they are whipping. Be careful not to pour the sugar onto the whisk, but rather down the side of the bowl while it is whipping.
Whip until cooled.

title: Lime Meringue
steps: Combine the sucrose, Versawhip, and xanthan gum and blend into the liquids with an immersion blender. Chill for one hour.
Transfer to the bowl of an electric stand mixer and whip to a slightly firm peak.

title: Meringue Buttons
steps: Begin whipping egg whites in mixer on medium speed. Slowly add sucrose in a few additions until firm peaks are achieved.
Apply as desired and dry in a convection oven, low fan, between 82°C/180°F and

title: Pecan Dacquoise Layer
steps: Combine the pecans and all the dry ingredients in a Robot Coupe until the pecans are ground to fine flour. Set aside.
Combine all the liquid ingredients in one bowl. Set aside.
Meanwhile whip egg whites and sugar to make an ordinary meringue and whip until soft peak.
Add the dry ingredients and liquids alternately into the meringue.
Spread the meringue onto a parchment lined (1/2) sheet pan.
Bake at 350 F. Once cooled, cut cake to fit into oval flexi molds.

title: Pistachio Souffle
steps: Whisk flour into 100 grams of the milk and set aside.
Combine the remaining milk with the sugar in a small saucepan; bring to a boil.

Whisk eggs and yolks into flour milk mixture. Temper, by pouring 1/3 of the boiling milk into the egg mixture, whisking. Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the egg mixture into the boiling milk, whisking until the pastry cream thickens and returns to a boil.
Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute. Remove from heat and beat in the pistachio paste and chopped pistachios.
Pour the cream into a stainless steel pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately over ice or as instructed.
When cooled, whip whites until frothy and gradually add the remaining sugar, continue whipping until soft peaks are formed.
Fold whites into pistachio pastry cream mixture and place into prepared molds.
Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 20 minutes.

title: Praline Souffle
steps: Whisk flour into 100 grams of the milk and set aside.
Combine the remaining milk with the sugar in a small saucepan; bring to a boil.

Whisk eggs and yolks into flour milk mixture. Temper, by pouring 1/3 of the boiling milk into the egg mixture, whisking. Return the remaining milk mixture to a boil. Whisk the egg mixture into the boiling milk, whisking until the (pastry) cream thickens and returns to a boil.
Boil the pastry cream and continue whisking for 1 minute. Remove from heat and beat in the praline paste and chopped hazelnuts.
Pour the cream into a stainless steel pan. Press plastic wrap directly on the surface. Chill immediately over ice or as instructed.
When cooled, whip whites until frothy and gradually add the remaining sugar, continue whipping until soft peaks are formed.
Fold whites into praline pastry cream mixture and place into prepared molds.
Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 20 minutes.

title: Succes Meringue Layers
steps: Combine the (first) sugar, the almond flour, hazelnut flour and cornstarch in a bowl and mix to combine.
Combine egg whites and salt in a kitchen aid bowl. Whip on medium speed until soft peaks form.
Gradually add the (second) sugar.
Continue whipping until stiff peaks form.
By hand, fold ¼ of the whipped whites into the almond/hazelnut mixture to lighten. Then fold in the rest of the whipped whites until just incorporated.
Pipe into cookie size spiral disks on a parchment paper-lined sheet pan with a
Bake at 300 degrees for about 25 minutes, or until set.

title: Swiss Meringue
steps: Combine well, the egg whites, salt and sugar in a Kitchen Aid mixing bowl. Then place over simmering water.
While over the hot water bath, stir with a hand whip until the egg whites are hot (approximately 130-140° F) and all the sugar is dissolved.
Remove from the heat and place on the mixer.
Whip until cool and stiff.
notes: Optional: Add 675 grams of butter to finished meringue to make buttercream

title: Swiss Meringue Butter Cream
steps: Combine egg whites and sugar in a bowl and heat over a double boiler to approximately 130˚F/55°C, or until the sugar has completely dissolved.
Transfer the egg whites to the bowl of an electric stand mixer and whip until cool.
Add butter in small amounts while whipping until fully incorporated.

title: Swiss Meringue Buttercream
steps: Combine egg whites and sugar in mixing bowl. Stir well with whisk and then place over simmering water.
Heat, while whisking, until egg whites are hot to the touch (approximately 140 ºF) and all the sugar is dissolved.
Remove from the heat and place bowl on mixer. Whisk until cool and stiff.
After meringue is cool and stiff, switch to the paddle attachment of the mixer if desired.
Add the butter, gradually; continue beating until all there are no lumps of butter and the mixture is completely smooth.
Flavor as desired when needed. Suggested flavors: Vanilla, Almond, Praline, Coffee

title: Biscuit Mixing Method
steps: Whip the egg yolks with a portion of the sugar until pale, thick, and at ribbon stage (it should hold a ribbon shape briefly when trailed across the surface). Set aside.
In a separate, clean bowl, whip the egg whites, adding the remaining sugar gradually, to make an ordinary (French) meringue at soft-to-medium peak — don't overwhip it stiff and dry.
Fold the meringue into the ribboned yolks in stages, folding only about three-quarters of the way together.
Finish folding while sifting in the flour (and any nut flour or cocoa) at the same time, so the last few folds combine the dry ingredients and the two egg mixtures together in one motion.
If melted butter is used, fold it in last, once the mixture is almost fully combined, so it doesn't deflate the foam any more than necessary.
Spread or pipe immediately onto parchment and bake in a hot oven for a short time — biscuit bakes fast and can dry out if left in too long.
notes: The defining move is separating the eggs and whipping yolks and whites independently, unlike the [[Genoise Mixing Method|Genoise Mixing]], which whips whole eggs together — this gives biscuit sponges (biscuit cuillère, biscuit joconde, dacquoise-style biscuits) a slightly coarser, more open crumb that’s ideal for piping ladyfingers or rolling.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Biscuit Cuillere]], [[Chocolate Biscuit]], [[Pecan Biscuit]], and [[Walnut Biscuit]].
Biscuit Joconde is a nut-flour variant that whips whole eggs into an almond-flour paste first and folds in the meringue afterward — same family, reordered steps.

title: Chiffon Mixing Method
steps: Whisk together the dry ingredients (flour, part of the sugar, baking powder, salt) in one bowl.
In another bowl, whisk together the liquid ingredients — vegetable oil, egg yolks, water or other liquid, and any flavoring.
Whisk the liquid ingredients into the dry ingredients until smooth; this forms the base batter, which is essentially a lean, oil-based cake batter with no leavening power of its own yet.
Separately, whip the egg whites with the remaining sugar and a little salt to make an ordinary meringue at soft peak.
Fold the meringue into the base batter in two or three additions, scraping the bottom of the bowl each time, until no white streaks remain.

Pour into an ungreased tube pan (traditionally) or prepared loaf/cake pans and bake; chiffon cakes are usually cooled upside down in the pan so the delicate structure doesn't collapse under its own weight.
notes: A hybrid of the [[One-Stage Mixing Method|One-Stage Mixing]] (for the oil/yolk base) and a whipped-meringue foam — oil keeps the crumb moist and tender while the whipped whites do all of the lifting.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Vanilla Chiffon Cake]], [[Chocolate Cinnamon Chiffon Cake]], and [[Walnut Chiffon Cake]].
Because oil (not solid fat) is used, chiffon cakes stay soft even cold from the fridge — solid-fat batters firm up when chilled, oil-based ones don’t.

title: Creaming Dough Mixing Method
steps: Beat softened (room-temperature) butter and sugar together with the paddle attachment until smooth and creamy — there's no need to whip in as much air as for a cake batter, just an even, lump-free base.
Add the flour(s), and any eggs, egg yolks, or flavoring called for, and mix only until just combined, scraping the bottom of the bowl so no pockets of unmixed flour or butter remain.
Gather the dough, flatten into a disk, wrap, and chill thoroughly — the dough will be soft after mixing since the butter was worked at room temperature rather than kept cold.

Roll out cold; because the fat is fully creamed in rather than left in pieces, this dough rolls, cuts, and shapes more like a cookie dough than a pie crust and holds crisp, sharp edges well after baking.
notes: Unlike the Flaky and Mealy Dough Methods, the fat here is fully emulsified into the flour from the start (like the [[Creaming Method]] for cakes) rather than cut in as solid pieces — the result is a short, sandy-tender, cookie-like dough rather than a flaky or mealy one.

Used in this cookbook for: [[Pate Sablee|Pâte Sablée]], [[Almond Pate Sucree|Almond Pâte Sucrée]], [[Breton Dough]], [[Lemon Pound Loaf Cake]], [[Sour Cherry Crumbcake Squares]], and [[Apple Walnut Muffins]].

Note this course’s terminology can differ from other pastry references, some of which describe pâte sucrée as the creamed dough and pâte sablée as the cut-in one — follow the specific recipe’s mixing method note on this site rather than assuming by name alone.

title: Creaming Method
steps: Have the butter and any solid fat at cool room temperature (about 65–68°F) — pliable but not soft or greasy.

Beat the butter and sugar together on medium speed with the paddle attachment until light in color and fluffy, 3–5 minutes. This is the step that matters most: the sugar crystals cut tiny air cells into the fat, and those cells are what leaven the cake in the oven.
Add eggs a little at a time (one at a time for whole eggs), beating well and scraping down after each addition so the mixture stays emulsified instead of curdling.

Add the sifted dry ingredients and the liquid (milk, buttermilk, etc.) alternately in 3–4 additions, starting and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix only until each addition is just combined — overmixing after the flour goes in develops gluten and toughens the cake.
Scrape the bowl well, give it one final short mix to even out the batter, and pan it immediately.
notes: Produces a taller, more open crumb than the two-stage/high-ratio method, but takes longer and is more sensitive to overmixing.

Used in this cookbook for: [[Basic Yellow Cake]], [[Chocolate Cake]], [[Yellow Buttermilk Cake]], [[Coconut Cake]], [[Chocolate Cupcakes]], [[Basic Sugar Cookie]], [[Chocolate Chip Cookies]], [[Spritz Cookies]], [[Tres Leches Cake]], [[Basque Cake]], and [[Apple Quick Bread]].
If the batter looks curdled after the eggs go in, it usually means the butter or eggs were too cold — warm the bowl slightly or add a spoonful of the measured flour to bring it back together.

title: Flaky Dough Mixing Method
steps: Start with cold fat (butter, shortening, or a blend) cut into small cubes, and cold liquid held in reserve.

Cut the fat into the flour (and salt/sugar) using a pastry blender, your fingertips, or the mixer's paddle on low speed, stopping while the pieces of fat are still pea-to-almond sized — you want visible flecks of fat, not a uniform crumb.
Add the cold liquid gradually, tossing or mixing gently, adding only enough for the dough to just come together — it should still look shaggy and a little dry, not smooth.
Gather the dough, pat it into a disk without kneading it, wrap, and chill thoroughly before rolling.
Roll and use as needed; the large, intact pieces of cold fat melt in the oven and release steam, separating the dough into the distinct flaky layers this method is named for.
notes: Best for top crusts, lattice tops, turnovers, and any pastry you want visibly flaky and shattering rather than crumbly.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Pate Brisee|Pâte Brisée]], [[Sour Cream Dough]], [[Galette Dough]], [[Biscuits]], and [[Shortcakes]].
Keeping everything cold (fat, liquid, even the bowl) is the whole method — if the fat softens and blends in too far, you lose the flakiness and end up with a mealy dough instead.

title: Genoise Mixing Method
steps: Whisk whole eggs, sugar, and a pinch of salt together in the mixer bowl, then set the bowl over a pan of barely simmering water (a bain-marie).
Whisk constantly by hand while the mixture warms to just lukewarm (about body temperature) and the sugar fully dissolves — don't let it get hot enough to scramble the eggs.

Move the bowl to the mixer and whip on high speed until the mixture has cooled back to room temperature and tripled in volume, reaching the “ribbon stage” (it falls off the whisk in a slowly dissolving ribbon).
Sift the flour a second time directly over the whipped eggs, then fold it in quickly but gently with a rubber spatula, scraping to the bottom of the bowl each pass so no dry flour hides there.
Finish by folding in cooled melted butter (if used) just until incorporated, then spread or pan immediately — the foam starts losing volume the moment folding stops.
notes: A whole-egg foam method: all of the leavening is whipped-in air, with no chemical leavener and (unlike Genoise Mousseline) no separate meringue.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Plain Genoise]], [[Chocolate Genoise Cake]], and the sponge layer in [[Strawberry Fraisier|Strawbery Fraisier]].
Warming the eggs before whipping lets them hold far more air than whipping cold — skipping the bain-marie step is the most common cause of a dense genoise.

title: Genoise Mousseline Mixing Method
steps: Whisk the eggs and sugar together over a bain-marie until the sugar dissolves and the mixture feels warm, exactly as in the plain Genoise Method.
Transfer to the mixer and whip on medium-high speed until thickened and increased in volume — it doesn't need to fully cool and triple the way a classic genoise does before you move on.
While the eggs whip, melt the butter and combine it with any flavoring liquid (rum, coffee, etc.), and sift the dry ingredients.
As soon as the egg foam has thickened enough, sift the dry ingredients directly over it while folding gently, then fold in the warm butter mixture just until no streaks remain.
Spread the batter thinly and bake right away, since it holds less air than a fully-whipped genoise and deflates faster.
notes: A lighter-touch variant of the [[Genoise Mixing Method|Genoise Mixing]] — the eggs are whipped to a looser, less-set foam and the butter is folded in as a separate warm mixture rather than added cold at the very end.
Used in this cookbook for the [[Chocolate Genoise Mousseline]] layer in the Milk Chocolate Coconut entremet.
Bakes thin (sheet-pan) rather than in a deep cake pan — it’s built to be cut into entremet layers, not stand alone.

title: High-Ratio (Two-Stage) Mixing Method
steps: Combine all of the dry ingredients (flour, sugar, leavener, salt) with the emulsified shortening or softened butter in the mixer bowl fitted with the paddle.

Mix on low speed until the fat has coated the flour and the mixture looks sandy, then increase to medium speed and beat until light — this is stage one, and it's what waterproofs the flour's protein and keeps the eventual crumb tender.

With the mixer running, add about half of the combined liquid ingredients (eggs, milk, flavorings). Mix until no streaks of fat remain, then continue mixing for about two more minutes to build the batter's structure.
Add the remaining liquid in a slow, steady stream with the mixer still running, then mix for a further two minutes. The finished batter should be thin enough to pour, not scrape, into the pan.
Scrape down, pour into prepared pans, and bake right away.
notes: Requires a recipe with a "high ratio" of sugar and liquid to flour (sugar ≥ 100% of flour weight) and an emulsified shortening — it was developed specifically for that combination and won’t work with a lean, low-sugar formula.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Basic Vanilla Cake]], [[Basic Chocolate Cupcake]], [[Basic White Cupcake]], [[Bourbon Pecan Pound Cake]], and [[High-Ratio Fresh Ginger Pound Cake with Lemon Glaze]].
Trades some of the height you get from the [[Creaming Method]] for a very fine, velvety, moist crumb and a longer shelf life.

title: Mealy Dough Mixing Method
steps: Start with cold fat cut into the flour, exactly as for the Flaky Dough Method, but keep working it — by pastry blender, fingertips, or paddle — until the mixture looks like coarse cornmeal or sand, with no pea-sized pieces left.
Add the cold liquid gradually, mixing only until the dough just comes together into a cohesive mass.
Gather into a disk, wrap, and chill before rolling.

Roll and dock or blind-bake as the recipe calls for; because the fat is worked in so finely, this dough won't puff into distinct layers, but it also won't let liquid filling soak through it the way a flakier dough can.
notes: The finer the fat is worked in, the more thoroughly it coats the flour’s protein, which both blocks gluten development (for a tender, short bite) and blocks moisture (for a crust that resists getting soggy).

Used in this cookbook for: [[Pate Sucree|Pâte Sucrée]], [[Pasta Frolla]], [[Dark Linzer Dough]] and [[Light Linzer Dough]], [[Shirley O. Corriher’s Biscuits|Shirley O. Corriher's Biscuits]], and the base for [[Scones]] (plain, [[Currant Tea Scones|currant]], [[Chocolate Chunk Scones|chocolate chunk]], [[Lemon Cornmeal Scones|lemon-cornmeal]], and [[Rock Scones|rock]] scones).
Reach for this method for bottom crusts under wet or custardy fillings, and for any dough (like scones) where a short, tender crumb matters more than visible layers.

title: Modified Creaming Method
steps: Cream the butter and the first portion of sugar until light and smooth, then beat in the egg yolks gradually until pale and fully incorporated.
Fold in any nut flour, ground nuts, or breadcrumbs called for in the base.
Separately, whip the egg whites to soft peaks and add the second portion of sugar to make an ordinary (French) meringue.

Lighten the creamed base with about a quarter of the meringue first, stirring it in fully, then gently fold in the remaining meringue in two additions — fold just until no streaks remain to keep as much air as possible.
Pan and bake immediately; this batter deflates faster than a straight creaming-method batter because so much of its lift comes from the whipped whites.
notes: A hybrid of the [[Creaming Method]] and a whipped-egg-foam method — richer and more tender than a plain sponge, lighter than a straight creamed cake.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Klimt-Torte]] and [[Lemon Ricotta Sponge]].
Because the structure leans on the meringue, fold gently and bake right away — letting the batter sit deflates it.

title: One-Stage Mixing Method
steps: Whisk together the wet ingredients (eggs, oil, buttermilk, melted butter, purees like carrot or banana) in the mixer bowl.
Sift the dry ingredients together separately, then add them all at once to the wet ingredients.
Mix on low speed just until combined — there's no creaming, no ribbon stage, and no separate egg-foam step, so this is the fastest of the mixing methods.
Fold in any mix-ins (nuts, grated vegetables, chocolate chips) by hand or on the lowest speed.
Pan and bake immediately.
notes: Also called the "muffin method" outside this curriculum — it’s built for oil-based batters (rather than solid-fat batters) where there’s no fat to cream in the first place.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Carrot Cake Layer]], [[Gluten-Free Vanilla Loaf Cake]], [[Salted Chocolate Cake]], [[Chocolate “Brownie” Entremet Cake]], and [[Brown Sugar Mixture]].
Overmixing is the main risk — stop as soon as the streaks of flour disappear, or the cake turns rubbery and tunnels.

title: Pain de Gênes Mixing Method
steps: Cream the almond paste (or almond/pistachio paste) with butter and sugar on the paddle attachment, beating until completely smooth — any lumps of paste will stay lumps in the baked cake, so take the time here.
Add the eggs gradually, beating well after each addition; continue beating past that point until the mixture reaches ribbon stage (thick, pale, and holding a trail when the whisk is lifted).
Sift the flour (and baking powder, salt, or starch if used) separately.
Fold the sifted dry ingredients into the emulsified egg-and-nut-paste base.

If using melted butter separately (rather than creaming all the butter in step 1), temper a small amount of the batter into it first, then fold that back into the remaining batter so the butter incorporates evenly instead of pooling.
Spread thinly on a lined sheet pan and bake — pain de gênes bakes flatter and denser than a genoise, so don't expect the same rise.
notes: Almond-paste-based cousin of the [[Genoise Mixing Method|Genoise Mixing]]: the emulsion of almond paste and egg replaces most of the whipped-air structure, so the cake is moister and denser than a classic genoise.
Used in this cookbook for: [[Almond Pear Cake]] and the [[Pistachio Pain De Gene]] layer in Lemon Strawberry Pistachio Cake.
Good almond paste (smooth, not gritty, not overly stiff) matters more here than in almost any other mixing method — it can’t be creamed smooth if it starts out dry or grainy.

title: Pâte à Bombe Method
steps: Cook sugar and water to soft-ball stage (about 118–121°C / 245–250°F) in a small saucepan, washing down the sides of the pan with a wet pastry brush to prevent crystallization.
While the syrup cooks, whip egg yolks (whole eggs, for some variations) in a stand mixer on medium speed until light, pale, and frothy.

Once the syrup is at temperature, reduce the mixer to medium-low and pour the hot syrup into the whipping yolks in a thin, steady stream, aiming between the whisk and the side of the bowl so it doesn't splash onto the whisk and spin off as threads.
