Increase to medium-high speed and continue whipping until the mixture has roughly doubled in volume and the outside of the bowl has cooled to just warm — this can take several minutes.

Use the pâte à bombe right away as the aerating base for mousses, buttercreams, parfaits, or semifreddo, folding it into whipped cream, softened butter, or other components while it's still light and mousse-like.
notes: The egg-yolk counterpart to Italian meringue — hot syrup pasteurizes the yolks and whips them into a stable, long-lasting foam without cooking them into scrambled egg.
Used in this cookbook as the base for glaze/mousse preparations such as the [[Glaze]] recipe (orange-chocolate mousse base).
Pour the syrup carefully and slowly — too fast, and it seizes into hard threads instead of emulsifying into the yolks; splashed onto the moving whisk, it flings out as unusable strands.

title: Apricot–Thyme Miroir
steps: Line a 6-inch entremet ring with acetate and set the Vanilla Biscuit in the base; brush generously with vanilla Stock Syrup.
Soak the 7.5 sheets of gelatin in cold water until soft.
Warm one-third of the apricot purée (about 225g) with the sugar and chopped thyme, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the warm purée until fully dissolved.
Stir in the remaining cold apricot purée to bring the mixture down in temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the apricot mixture in two additions until smooth and homogenous.
Pour the mousse over the prepared biscuit within the ring; smooth the top and freeze until fully set.
For the mirror glaze, soak the 3.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water.
Gently warm the apricot purée with the apple jelly, stirring until the jelly is melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and whisk it in until dissolved.
Stir in the nappage and strain the glaze for a smooth finish.
Cool the glaze to just warm (90-95°F) — it should not be hot to the touch.
Pour the glaze evenly over the frozen mousse to coat completely; return to the freezer to set the glaze before unmolding.
notes: Note: To make candied thyme branches, dip thyme, holding from the base of the stalk very briefly in

title: Banana Mousse
steps: Whip the heavy cream (1) to a soft peak and set aside refrigerated.
Bloom the gelatin sheets and set aside.
Make a caramel from the sugar and stop the cooking by adding the heavy cream (2). Return the caramel to the heat if necessary to re-melt the caramel.
Remove the caramel from the heat and add the bloomed gelatin.
Temper the egg yolks with all of the caramel mixture. Add the banana puree, cinnamon and melted milk chocolate. Cool the mixture until it is cooled enough to fold in the heavy cream.
Fold in the heavy cream.

title: Blood Orange- Cinnamon Miroir
steps: Line a 6-inch entremet ring with acetate and set the Vanilla Biscuit in the base; brush generously with vanilla Stock Syrup.
Soak the 16.5 sheets of gelatin in cold water until soft.
Warm one-third of the blood orange purée (about 300g) with the sugar and cinnamon, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the warm purée until fully dissolved.
Stir in the remaining cold blood orange purée to bring the mixture down in temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the blood orange mixture in two additions until smooth and homogenous.
Pour the mousse over the prepared biscuit within the ring; smooth the top and freeze until fully set.
For the mirror glaze, soak the 8 sheets of gelatin in cold water.
Gently warm the blood orange purée with the apricot nappage, stirring until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and whisk it in until dissolved.
Cool the glaze to just warm (90-95°F) — it should not be hot to the touch.
Pour the glaze evenly over the frozen mousse to coat completely; return to the freezer to set the glaze before unmolding.

title: Blueberry- Lemon Miroir
steps: Line a 6-inch entremet ring with acetate and set the Vanilla Biscuit in the base; brush generously with lemon Stock Syrup.
Soak the 9.5 sheets of gelatin in cold water until soft.
Warm one-third of the blueberry purée (about 225g) with the sugar, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the warm purée until fully dissolved.
Stir in the remaining cold blueberry purée to bring the mixture down in temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the blueberry mixture in two additions until smooth and homogenous.
Pour the mousse over the prepared biscuit within the ring; smooth the top and freeze until fully set.
For the mirror glaze, soak the 3.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water.
Gently warm the blueberry purée with the currant jelly, stirring until the jelly is melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and whisk it in until dissolved.
Cool the glaze to just warm (90-95°F) — it should not be hot to the touch.
Pour the glaze evenly over the frozen mousse to coat completely; return to the freezer to set the glaze before unmolding.

title: Caramel Mousse
steps: Whip heavy cream (1) to soft peak; reserve refrigerated.
Soak the gelatin sheets in cold water.
Warm the milk and heavy cream (2) together; keep warm.
In a heavy saucepan, melt sugar (1) over medium heat, swirling occasionally, until it reaches a deep amber caramel.
Off heat, carefully whisk in the warm milk-cream mixture in stages (the caramel will bubble vigorously); return to heat and stir until smooth.
Whisk egg yolks with sugar (2) until pale.
Temper the yolks by gradually whisking in the hot caramel-cream mixture.
Return the mixture to the pan and cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until it thickens to nappe consistency (coats the back of a spoon).
Remove from heat; squeeze excess water from the gelatin and whisk it in until dissolved.
Stir in the Maldon salt.
Strain the caramel anglaise and cool to room temperature.
Fold in the whipped cream in two additions until fully incorporated.
Pour into molds and chill/freeze until set.

title: Charlotte Royale Biscuit Layers Raspberry Mousse Biscuit Layers
steps: In a KitchenAid mixing bowl combine the eggs, yolks and the sugar (1).
Warm over double boiler until sugar is dissolved and mixture is warm.
Whip until ribboned.
Once ribboned, add the sifted flour and corn starch and vanilla extract. Whip for 1 more minute at high speed until light and fluffy.
Separately, make an ordinary meringue from the egg whites and sugar (2) and whip to a soft peak.
Fold together both mixes just until combined.
Spread evenly onto flat parchment lined half sheet pans.
Bake in a 375 F. convection oven for 8-10 minutes.

title: Chocolate Mirror Glaze
steps: Bloom gelatin sheets.
Sift together the sugar and cocoa powder, then whisk them together with the water and heavy cream in a pot. Bring to a boil.
Remove from the heat and pour over the chocolate. Stir until combined.
Add the bloomed gelatin and stir until dissolved. Strain mixture through a chinois
The glaze is ready to use when it reaches 85 F.

title: Chocolate Mousse
steps: Whip cream (1) to soft peak and reserve in refrigerator.
Melt chocolate and set aside.
Whisk sugar, egg yolks and heavy cream (2) over double boiler until thickened.
Remove from heat, fold in melted chocolate into yolk mixture.
Cool to room temperature, and then fold in reserved whipped cream.
Pipe a layer into dome molds, making sure to line the mold completely with an even layer, (approximately 110g per mold).
Freeze immediately.

title: Chocolate “Brownie” Entremet Cake
steps: Melt finely chopped chocolate over a hot water bath and then combine with water. Whisk until smooth and set aside.
In a bowl, whisk the eggs, oil, vanilla and sour cream until smooth. Then, combine with melted chocolate mixture.
In a large bowl, combine sugar, flour, salt and baking soda and whisk together to combine.
Gradually pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients while whisking to ensure there are no lumps.
Portion the batter into two (10”) pans that have been sprayed and parchment lined. Bake at 325 F. until slightly firm.
Cool in freezer as soon as possible.
notes: Utilize the One Step mixing method

title: Coconut-Lime & Chocolate Miroir
steps: Line a 6-inch entremet ring with acetate and set the Chocolate Biscuit in the base; brush generously with rum Stock Syrup.
Soak the 9.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water until soft.
Warm one-third of the coconut purée (about 225g) with the sugar, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the warm purée until fully dissolved.
Stir in the remaining cold coconut purée to bring the mixture down in temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the coconut mixture in two additions until smooth and homogenous.
Pour the mousse over the prepared biscuit within the ring; smooth the top and freeze until fully set.
For the mirror glaze, soak the 3.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water.
Gently warm the coconut purée with the apple jelly, stirring until the jelly is melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and whisk it in until dissolved.
Cool the glaze to just warm (90-95°F) — it should not be hot to the touch.
Pour the glaze evenly over the frozen mousse to coat completely; return to the freezer to set the glaze before unmolding.

title: Coffee Mousse
steps: Whip cream to soft peak and reserve in refrigerator.
Bloom gelatin powder with the 30 grams of water and set aside.
Boil the next four ingredients, cover with plastic wrap and steep for 10 minutes.
After 10 minutes, strain the boiled mixture through a chinois and add more heavy cream if necessary to get a total of 675 grams.
Return mixture to a boil and make an anglaise with the egg yolks and sugar.
Remove from heat and add the bloomed gelatin.
Strain and allow to cool over an ice bath and then fold in whipped cream.
Pour onto cake frame on top of the coffee creme and freeze in the blast freezer or freeze overnight.

title: Dark Chocolate & Raspberry Mousse
steps: Whip the heavy cream to soft peak; set aside refrigerated.
Melt the semisweet chocolate with water (1) over a bain-marie, stirring until smooth; keep warm.
Combine sugar and water (2) in a saucepan and cook to soft-ball stage (240°F/116°C).
Begin whipping the egg yolks in a mixer.
With the mixer running, stream in the hot syrup and whip on high speed until pale, thick, and cooled to room temperature (pâte à bombe).
Fold the pâte à bombe into the warm melted chocolate until smooth.
Fold in the whipped cream in two additions until fully incorporated.
Gently ripple the raspberry jam through the mousse, leaving visible streaks rather than blending fully.
Pour into molds and freeze/chill until set.

title: Five Spice Milk Chocolate Mousse
steps: Whip the heavy cream to soft peak; set aside refrigerated.
Soak the gelatin in cold water.
Warm the crème anglaise with the five spice powder.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and stir it into the warm crème anglaise until dissolved.
Gradually whisk the warm crème anglaise into the melted milk chocolate in thirds, stirring until smooth and glossy after each addition.
Cool the mixture to about body temperature.
Fold in the whipped cream in two additions until homogenous.
Pour into molds and chill/freeze until set.

title: Lemon Mousse
steps: Whip cream until soft peak and set aside refrigerated.
Bloom gelatin and set aside.
Meanwhile, make the Italian meringue and set aside125 grams that is needed for the recipe.
Heat the lemon juice and add the bloomed gelatin.
Add the lemon cream to the gelatin mixture and stir until combined.
With a whisk, fold the Italian meringue into the lemon cream gently making sure it is smooth.
Fold in the whipped cream.

title: Mandarin Gelee
steps: Bloom the gelatin in cold water until bloomed.
Combine the mandarin purée and sugar in a small saucepan; heat until the sucrose has dissolved.
Remove from heat and add the bloomed gelatin; stir until dissolved.
Cool slightly and deposit a thin layer onto the previously set panna cotta. Chill to further set.

title: Milk Chocolate Mousse
steps: Whip heavy cream to soft peak, set aside.
Bloom gelatin, set aside.
Combine the remaining pate a bombe ingredients in a pot, whisk until mixture reaches 179 F.
Remove gelatin from water and add to the hot pate a bomb and stir until dissolved.
Transfer to a KitchenAid bowl and whip until cooled.
In a large bowl, make a ganache with boiled heavy cream, milk chocolate and salt, making sure it is well emulsified.
Fold cooled pate a bombe into the ganache in three stages.
Fold in whipped cream.

title: Orange White Chocolate Mousse
steps: Whip the heavy cream to soft peak; set aside refrigerated.
Soak the gelatin in cold water.
Melt the white chocolate over a bain-marie; keep warm.
Warm the orange juice, remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and stir it into the juice until dissolved.
Gradually whisk the warm orange juice into the melted white chocolate until smooth and fully emulsified.
Combine the sugar and water in a saucepan and cook to soft-ball stage (240°F/116°C).
Whip the egg yolks while streaming in the hot syrup; whip until thick, pale, and cooled (pâte à bombe).
Fold the pâte à bombe into the white chocolate-orange mixture.
Fold in the whipped cream in two additions until fully incorporated.
Pour into molds and chill/freeze until set.

title: Peanut Butter & Jelly Mousse
steps: Soak the gelatin in cold water.
Warm the crème anglaise and whisk in the peanut butter until completely smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and stir it in until dissolved.
Cool the peanut butter anglaise to room temperature.
Fold in the whipped cream in two additions until fully incorporated.
Spoon half of the mousse into molds.
Add a layer or swirl of grape jelly over the mousse.
Top with the remaining mousse, covering the jelly completely.
Chill/freeze until fully set.

title: Pineapple Gelee
steps: Bloom the gelatin in cold water until bloomed.
Combine the pineapple purée and sugar in a small saucepan and heat just until the sucrose has dissolved.
Remove from heat and add bloomed gelatin, followed by the rum; stir until dissolved.
Cool slightly and deposit a thin layer onto the previously set panna cotta. Chill to further set.

title: Pistachio Cherry Mousse
steps: Soak the gelatin in cold water.
Warm the crème anglaise and whisk in the pistachio paste until smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and stir it in until dissolved.
Stir in the kirsch.
Cool the pistachio anglaise to room temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the pistachio mixture in two additions until homogenous.
Fold in the drained Amarena cherries.
Pour into molds and chill/freeze until set.

title: Pomegranate Gelee
steps: Bloom the gelatin in cold water until bloomed.
Combine the pomegranate juice and sugar in a small saucepan; heat until the sucrose has dissolved.
Remove from heat and add the bloomed gelatin and balsamic vinegar; stir until dissolved.
Cool slightly and deposit a thin layer onto the previously set panna cotta. Chill to further set.

title: Raspberry & Chocolate Miroir
steps: Line a 6-inch entremet ring with acetate and set the Chocolate Biscuit in the base; brush generously with Framboise Stock Syrup.
Soak the 7.5 sheets of gelatin in cold water until soft.
Warm one-third of the raspberry purée (about 225g) with the sugar, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the warm purée until fully dissolved.
Stir in the remaining cold raspberry purée to bring the mixture down in temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the raspberry mixture in two additions until smooth and homogenous.
Pour the mousse over the prepared biscuit within the ring; smooth the top and freeze until fully set.
For the mirror glaze, soak the 3.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water.
Gently warm the raspberry purée with the currant jelly, stirring until the jelly is melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and whisk it in until dissolved.
Cool the glaze to just warm (90-95°F) — it should not be hot to the touch.
Pour the glaze evenly over the frozen mousse to coat completely; return to the freezer to set the glaze before unmolding.

title: Raspberry Mousse
steps: Bloom the gelatin and set aside.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak and set aside refrigerated.
Heat the raspberry puree and sugar together until the sugar is dissolved. Remove from the heat; allow to cool slightly and mix in the bloomed gelatin. Allow this mixture to cool before use.

While the puree is cooling, mix the yolks and sugar in the bowl of a KitchenAid mixer and place over a bain marie and whip until thick and the sugar dissolved. Remove from the heat and whip on a mixer until ribboned and cooled.
Add the cooled raspberry puree mixture to the ribboned eggs and mix until cool enough to fold in the whipped cream.
Fold in the whipped cream quickly and all at once. Charlotte Assembly:
Unmold biscuit onto parchment that has been lightly sprinkled with 10X.
Spread one half sheet of biscuit evenly with 200 grams of seedless raspberry jam and roll into a tight roulade. Freeze until solid so that ¼” thick slices can be
Fill with raspberry mousse. *Note the next instructions before filling the mold
Cut 6” cake disc from remaining (1/2) sheet cake (or larger depending on bowl
Freeze overnight or place in a blast freezer before unmolding.
Glaze with nappage if desired.

title: Raspberry Verrine Raspberry Gelee
steps: Combine the raspberry purée and fresh raspberries in a sauce pan. Bring to a simmer.
Whisk in the gelatin and invert sugar. Deposit as instructed into serving dishes and chill to set.

title: Red Wine Gelee
steps: Bloom the gelatin in cold water until bloomed.

Combine the red wine and sugar in a small saucepan and bring to a boil; reduce heat and continue to simmer for several minutes until the mixture has reduced slightly, to a weight of approximately 225g.
Remove from heat and add bloomed gelatin; stir until dissolved.
Cool slightly and deposit a thin layer onto the previously set panna cotta. Chill to further set.

title: Sour Cherry & Chocolate Miroir
steps: Line a 6-inch entremet ring with acetate and set the Chocolate Biscuit in the base; brush generously with cherry-juice Stock Syrup.
Soak the 9.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water until soft.
Warm one-third of the sour cherry purée (about 225g) with the sugar, stirring until the sugar dissolves and the mixture is hot.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the bloomed gelatin, and whisk it into the warm purée until fully dissolved.
Stir in the remaining cold sour cherry purée to bring the mixture down in temperature.
Whip the heavy cream to soft peak.
Fold the whipped cream into the cherry mixture in two additions until smooth and homogenous.
Pour the mousse over the prepared biscuit within the ring; smooth the top and freeze until fully set.
For the mirror glaze, soak the 3.75 sheets of gelatin in cold water.
Gently warm the cherry purée with the currant jelly, stirring until the jelly is melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat, squeeze excess water from the gelatin, and whisk it in until dissolved.
Cool the glaze to just warm (90-95°F) — it should not be hot to the touch.
Pour the glaze evenly over the frozen mousse to coat completely; return to the freezer to set the glaze before unmolding.

title: Strawberry Gelee
steps: Bloom gelatin.
In a small sauce pan heat the lemon juice with the sugar to dissolve and add the bloomed gelatin and stir to melt.
Temper the gelatin mixture with some of the strawberry puree to make sure the gelatin is not setting and then pour the gelatin base into the remaining puree and stir to incorporate.
Allow gelee to cool before pouring onto ½ sheet pan flexi mold frame.
Put in blast freezer or freeze overnight until ready to assemble the cake.

title: Apple Cranberry Pie

ingredients.name: Pâte Sucrée or Sour Cream Dough

steps: Roll half the dough and line the pie pan; trim overhang and chill.
Peel, core and slice apples into wedges.
Toss apples with sugar, cranberries, flour, cinnamon and lemon zest.
Fill the lined shell with the apple-cranberry mixture, mounding slightly in the center.
Roll remaining dough and top the pie (full round or lattice); seal and crimp the edges.
Cut vents in the top crust if using a full round.
Chill the filled pie until the dough is firm.
Bake until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbling through the vents, tenting with foil if the edges brown too quickly.
Cool completely on a rack before slicing.

title: Apple Crumb Pie

ingredients.name: Pâte Sucrée or Sour Cream Dough

steps: Roll the dough and line the pie pan; trim and crimp the edge, then chill.
Peel, core and slice apples into wedges.
Toss apples with sugar, flour, cinnamon and lemon zest.
Fill the shell with the apple mixture, mounding slightly in the center.
Cover the apples generously and evenly with the crumb topping.
Bake until the topping is golden brown and the apples are tender when pierced, tenting with foil if the topping browns too quickly.
Cool completely before slicing.

title: Apple Pie

ingredients.name: Pâte Sucrée or Sour Cream Dough

steps: Roll half the dough and line the pie pan; chill.
Fill the shell with the cooked apple filling, spreading evenly.
Dot the filling with butter.
Roll the remaining dough and top the pie (full round or lattice); seal and crimp the edges.
Cut vents in the top crust if using a full round.
Chill the filled pie until the dough is firm.
Bake until the crust is golden brown and the filling bubbles through the vents.
Cool completely on a rack before slicing.

title: Apple Tart

ingredients.name: Gluten Free Flaky Pie Crust, above

steps: Peel, core, halve and slice the apples, keeping halves intact; set aside
Combine sugar, water and lemon juice in a sauté pan with a 10-inch. Bring to a boil and cook syrup to a light caramel. Add butter off the heat.
Arrange apple halves in pan, rounded sides face down, crowding together. If there are any leftover halves, separate slices to form a layer over the halves in the pan.
Cook, covered over medium heat for about 15 minutes or until apples are tender.
Uncover. Allow syrup in the pan to reduce until thick. Cool.

title: Apple Walnut Lattice Tart
steps: Roll 700 grams of the dough on a floured surface and line a buttered 12- x 18- inch pan with it. Chill the dough-lined pan. Reserve the remaining pound of dough in the refrigerator.
Prepare the Walnut Frangipane and spread it evenly on the dough.
Peel, core and slice the apples and fan them out on the filling. Paint the apples with the butter and sprinkle evenly with the cinnamon and sugar.

title: Apricot Cognac Tart
steps: Prepare the dough and chill it. Roll the dough on a floured surface and
Beat the pastry cream to soften it and beat in the vanilla, toasted ground almonds and one ounce of Cognac.

Rinse, halve and stone the apricots. Spread the almond pastry cream evenly on the dough and arrange the apricot halves on it, cut side down. Bake the tart at 350 degrees about 30 - 40 minutes. Cool the tart.
Combine Apricot Glaze and remaining ounce of Cognac. Brush the glaze evenly over the cooled tart. Strew with the sliced almond

title: Banana Walnut Tart
steps: Roll the Pâte Sucrée and line the tart pan; blind-bake the shell until lightly golden and let cool.
Spread the Walnut Frangipane evenly in the baked shell.
Peel and slice the bananas; toss gently with the dark rum.
Arrange the banana slices over the frangipane.
Scatter the chopped walnut pieces over the top.
Bake until the frangipane is puffed, set and golden brown.
Cool completely before unmolding and slicing.

title: Bistro Apple Tart

ingredients.name: Gluten Free Flaky Pie Crust, above

steps: Slice apple thinly and fan in concentric circle over dough.
Brush with melted butter and sprinkle with cinnamon sugar.
Bake at 375°F for about 20 minutes until the dough is cooked through and apples are tender.

title: Blueberry Pie

ingredients.name: Pâte Sucrée or Sour Cream Dough

steps: Roll the dough and line the pie pan; chill.
Whisk the cornstarch and water together to make a slurry.
In a saucepan, cook a portion of the blueberries with the sugar and lemon zest over medium heat until the berries release their juices.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook until the mixture thickens and turns glossy.
Remove from heat and fold in the remaining raw blueberries; cool slightly.
Fill the shell with the blueberry mixture and dot with butter.
Top with a lattice of rolled dough, or cover with the crumb topping, per the desired finish.
Bake until the filling is bubbling and the crust or topping is golden brown.
Cool completely to allow the filling to set before slicing.

title: Blueberry and Walnut Tart
steps: Roll the Pâte Sucrée and line the tart pan; blind-bake the shell until lightly golden and let cool.
Spread the Walnut Frangipane evenly in the baked shell.
Scatter the blueberries evenly over the frangipane.
Bake until the frangipane is set, puffed and golden brown.
Cool slightly, then brush the warm tart with Apricot Glaze.
Finish by pressing ground walnuts around the edge of the tart.
Cool completely before unmolding and slicing.

title: Breton Dough
steps: Prepare using Creaming Dough Mixing Method.
Use immediately. This dough is very soft.

title: Cannoli

ingredients.name: dry white wine or dry vermouth

steps: Combine the flour, sugar, salt and cinnamon in a medium mixing bowl.
Rub the lard or butter by hand until it is finely incorporated.

Whisk the egg, yolk and wine together and use a large rubber spatula to stir the liquid into the flour mixture. Continue stirring until all the flour is evenly moistened, and then scrape the dough out onto a floured surface.

Knead the dough briefly by folding it over onto itself repeatedly until it becomes too firm and elastic to knead further. Use a bench scraper or a knife to divide the dough into 3 pieces. Flour 1 piece and pass it through the widest setting on a pasta machine.
Fold the dough in thirds and pass it through again from the open side. Repeat until the dough is smooth and elastic and no longer shreds as you are putting it through the machine.
Fold the dough in thirds again, wrap in plastic wrap and repeat with the remaining two pieces of dough.
After all the pieces have been passed through the machine, fold each in thirds again, wrap them in plastic wrap and refrigerate them for at least an hour or as long as overnight before continuing.
To form the cannoli shells, remove the dough from the refrigerator and flour 1 piece.
Use a plain round cutter or a stiff cardboard pattern and the point of a pairing knife
Cut straight down without twisting the utter, which would seal the cut edge of the
Roll over 1 disk of dough with a rolling pin to make it slightly oval. Position 1
Draw the other edge of the dough disk up to meet the first one and overlap them
In a large Dutch oven, heat the oil to 375 degrees. Fry 3 or 4 cannoli at a time to a
Use an oven mitt to grasp the end of the tube and use a towel or several
Repeat with remaining pieces of dough and tubes. If you have only a few tubes
While the shells are cooling, prepare the filling.
Place the ricotta in the bowl of an electric mixer. Using the paddle attachment
Beat in the confectioner's sugar and continue beating just until it is absorbed.
Beat in the vanilla and cinnamon. Remove the bowl from the mixer and use a

title: Citrus-Cornmeal Breton Cookies
steps: Whip yolks and sugar to heavy ribbon.
Switch to paddle and add butter.
Add citrus zests and dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
Scoop dough with medium or large cookie scoop and freeze.
Cut frozen scoops into quarters. Bake with cut side facing up at 350F.

title: Dark Linzer Dough
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method.
Scrape dough out of bowl. Do not refrigerate before using. Dough will be very soft.

title: Galette Dough
steps: Prepare using Flaky Dough Mixing Method.

title: Galette Fillings Apple Cinnamon Filling
steps: Peel, core, and halve the apples.
Cut each half into 5 wedges.
Combine apple slices with remaining ingredients in a heavy saucepan over medium heat.
Cover; cook 10 minutes. Uncover; lower heat, cook until liquid evaporates. Cool.

title: Gluten Free Flaky Pie Crust
steps: Mix all dry ingredients in the bowl of a standing mixer fitted with a paddle attachment to combine well.
Add cold butter to the bowl in large pieces and mix briefly.
Combine liquid ingredients. Add all at once to the mixer and beat until dough is formed.
Wrap dough and chill until ready to use. Dough can be used right away.
When ready to use, roll on parchment or between two pieces of parchment. Invert into a pan and peel away parchment. If rolling directly on the bench, make sure to use a gluten free starch
Bake at 375° F until a deep golden color.

title: Grigliata Umbra
steps: Peel, core, and grate the apples coarsely by hand or in a food processor fitted with the metal blade. Combine with the sugar and water in a non-reactive saucepan that has a tight-fitting lid. Place over medium heat. When the mixture begins to simmer, lower the heat, cover the pan, and cook 5 minutes, until the apples are swimming in water. Uncover, add the remaining filling ingredients, and cook over low heat, stirring often, about 15 to 20 minutes, until the filling is reduced to a thick paste. Cool the filling in a bowl at room temperature, stirring occasionally.

Roll 700 grams of the dough on a floured surface and line a buttered 12- x 18- inch pan with it. Chill the dough-lined pan until the dough is firm, about 30 minutes. Reserve the remaining pound of dough in the refrigerator.

Spread the cooled filling evenly over the dough.. Roll the remaining dough the strips on the filling, 4 in each direction, to make a "grill," or perpendicular lattice. Trim away the excess dough at the edges. Bake the grigliata at 350 degrees for about 35 minutes, until the pastry is baked through. Cool in the pan on a rack.

For the glaze, place the apple jelly in a small saucepan and bring to a boil over low heat, stirring occasionally. Reduce the glaze until it is sticky. Brush on the filling and cool a few minutes to allow the glaze to set. Cut the grigliata into 3-

title: Light Linzer Dough
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method.
Scrape dough out of bowl. Do not refrigerate before using. Dough will be very soft.

title: Linzer Cookies
steps: Cream butter and sugar.
Add yolks, zest and extracts and mix until combined, scraping down the sides as needed.
Add dry ingredients and scrape from the bottom just until combined. Chill dough for use.
Roll dough out and use a round cookie cutter to cut out the cookies. Cut a hole in the center of half the cookies only.
Bake both cookies at 350ºF.
Spread preserves on the solid cookies and sift 10x on the other half of the cookies with the holes cut out. Then sandwich together.
notes: Utilize the Creaming mixing method.

title: Pastiera Napoletana

ingredients.name: candied orange peel, rinsed and chopped

steps: Soak the wheat overnight in water if possible. Drain and rinse the wheat and until tender, about 2 hours. Add water as necessary to keep the wheat from drying out and sticking. Cool the wheat and refrigerate until needed.
To assemble the filling, beat ricotta to soften it. Beat in the sugar. Beat in eggs, orange flower water and candied orange peel. Stir in the cooked wheat and the pastry cream.

title: Peach Pie

ingredients.name: Pâte Sucrée or Sour Cream Dough
ripe yellow-fleshed freestone peaches (4 large or 6medium)

steps: To make the topping, combine the flour, sugar, and nutmeg in a mixing bowl; stir well to combine and stir in the sliced almonds. Stir in the melted butter evenly. Set aside for 5 minutes. Break into crumbs and set aside until ready to use.

title: Pear and Walnut Tart
steps: Roll the Pâte Sucrée and line the tart pan; blind-bake the shell until lightly golden and let cool.
Spread the Walnut Frangipane evenly in the baked shell.
Peel, core and thinly slice the pears; arrange over the frangipane in a decorative pattern.
Bake until the frangipane is set, puffed and golden brown and the pears are tender.
Cool slightly, then brush the warm tart with apricot glaze.
Finish by pressing ground walnuts around the edge of the tart.
Cool completely before unmolding and slicing.

title: Pecan Pie
steps: Roll the Pâte Sucrée and line the pie pan, crimping the edge; chill.
Scatter the pecan halves evenly over the bottom of the shell.
Whisk together the corn syrup, sugar, melted butter, eggs and Bourbon until smooth.
Pour the syrup mixture over the pecans, allowing them to float to the surface.
Bake until the filling is set around the edges with a slight jiggle in the center, tenting the edges with foil if the crust browns too quickly.
Cool completely on a rack before slicing, allowing the filling to fully set.

title: Pizza Rustica Alla Napoletana

ingredients.name: sweet dried sausage, peeled and diced

steps: For the filling, place ricotta in a bowl and stir smooth with a rubber spatula. Stir in eggs one at a time; stir in remaining filling ingredients in order.

Divide the Pasta Frolla into 2 pieces. Roll 1 of the pieces thinly to line a 12- inch straight-sided cake pan. Pour in filling and smooth top. Roll the and salt together and paint dough strips with egg wash. Arrange six strips on filling. Arrange remaining strips at a 45-degree angle to the first ones. Press strips at rim of pan to adhere and trim away excess dough even with top of pan.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 45 minutes, until the filling is set and the dough is baked through. Cool in the pan on a rack. Serve at room temperature. Refrigerate leftovers.

title: Raspberry Almond Tart
steps: Roll the Pâte Sucrée and line the tart pan; blind-bake the shell until lightly golden and let cool.
Spread the Almond Frangipane evenly in the baked shell.
Scatter the raspberries evenly over the frangipane.
Bake until the frangipane is set, puffed and golden brown.
Cool completely before unmolding and slicing.

title: Rhubarb Cheese Tart with Crumb Topping
steps: Prepare the dough and chill it. Roll the dough on a floured surface and small hotel pan.
Bring the sugar and water to a boil and pour it over the rhubarb. Allow the rhubarb to cool in the syrup and chill. Drain the rhubarb on paper towels.
Arrange the rhubarb on the dough. Beat the cream cheese until it is light and beat in the remaining ingredients in order.
Pour the cheese custard over the rhubarb and strew the surface with the crumbs.
Bake the tart at 350 degrees 30 - 40 minutes. Cool the tart and dust it with confectioners' sugar.

title: Sfogliatelle Napoletane
steps: For the dough, combine the flour and salt in a mixing bowl and stir in the water. The dough will be very dry. Scrape the contents of the bowl onto the work surface and press and knead the dough together so that all the dry bits are through the widest setting of the pasta machine to work the dough smooth. Fold the dough in half every time after passing it through the machine and change the direction of inserting the dough occasionally. After passing the dough through about 12 or 15 times, it should be smooth. Knead the dough into a ball, wrap in plastic wrap and allow to rest in the refrigerator about 2 hours.

For the filling, combine the water and sugar in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and sift the semolina over the boiling water gradually, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Lower the heat and cook, for a minute or two stirring often, until very smooth and thick. Press the ricotta through a fine sieve or puree it in the food processor and stir into the cooked semolina mixture. Cook several minutes longer, stirring often. Remove from the heat and stir in the yolks, vanilla, cinnamon and candied orange peel. Scrape the filling into a shallow bowl or glass pie pan and press plastic wrap against the surface. Refrigerate until cold. The filling will become very firm.

Combine the lard and butter in a bowl and beat by machine until well mixed and soft. Flour the dough and divide it into 4 pieces. Flour each piece of dough and pass through the pasta machine at the widest setting. Make sure the dough emerges in a neat, rectangular strip as wide as the opening of the machine. If the dough is uneven, fold it over on itself so that it is as wide as the opening and
Place one of the strips of dough on a lightly floured surface and paint it with the
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees and set a rack in the middle level. Line 2 half-

title: Tart Tatin Apples
steps: Peel, core and cut apples evenly into 4th's and set aside.

Caramelize first 115 grams of sugar to a light golden color. Add only half of the apples (as it may crowd the pan and cause the apples to steam if you add too many), and first115 grams of butter and combine to stop the caramel from darkening.
Sauté the apples in the caramel until cooked. The apples should be well colored but still be slightly firm as they will be further baked in the oven. Place them on a tray to cool.
Repeat these steps with the other half of the apples and the second sugar and butter.
When the apple slices are cool enough to handle, place a layer very tightly,
Cut the frozen puff pastry to fit the top of the 6” cake pan and place on top of the
Once removed from the oven, allow the Tart Tatin to sit for several minutes and

title: Tarte Croustade Pate Croustade
steps: Thoroughly combine salt and flour in mixer bowl.
Separately, combine water and butter; add egg yolks.
Add liquid mixture to flour in three additions, mixing to produce a homogenous dough.
Portion into four flat rectangles and rest for at least two hours, chilled.
Roll to 1mm thickness and cut rectangles measuring 6cm by 10cm.
Gently transfer cut rectangles to stainless steel tube molds and then tightly wrap in foil (24cm in length). Bake at 150˚C/300˚F for approximately16 minutes.

title: Thanksgiving Day Pumpkin Pie
steps: Roll the Pâte Sucrée and line the pie pan, crimping the edge; chill.
Whisk together the pumpkin, eggs, yolks, sugar, salt, cinnamon, ginger and nutmeg until smooth.
Whisk in the half and half until fully incorporated.
Pour the filling into the chilled shell.
Bake until the filling is set around the edges with a slight jiggle in the center.
Cool completely before slicing.

title: Almond Dentelle
steps: In a stand mixer bowl, combine the flour, sucrose, almond flour, and salt using the paddle attachment.
Add the orange juice, followed by the melted butter.
Mix until thoroughly incorporated. Chill and rest, refrigerated, until the dough is firm.
Using a small spoon or scoop, portion and roll small balls of dough (approximately 10-20g each); place them on a Silpat-lined sheet pan, allowing ample room for spreading.

Bake at 150°C/300°F until golden, but not excessively browned, approximately 10-15 minutes. Remove from the oven, allow to cool one to two minutes, and then trim each dentelle cookie with an appropriate sized ring cutter. Allow to cool and store in a covered container.

title: Apricot Puree
steps: Combine all ingredients (discard or use the scraped vanilla pod for another use) and blend well. Strain and chill.

title: Apricot Sphere
yield: (Yield: approximately 36 filled almost to the top)
steps: Thoroughly blend the apricot purée, sucrose (1), and calcium lactate. Deposit into silicon dome molds and freeze.
Thoroughly blend the warm water (1), sodium alginate, and sodium citrate in a blender. Transfer to a nine pan and allow to stand at room temperature for at least one hour or overnight.
Combine the water (2) and sucrose (2) in a small sauce pan and bring just to a boil. Transfer to a shallow pan and cool to room temperature.
Drop frozen apricot purée into the alginate bath and set, about 5 minutes. Carefully transfer to a water rinse, and then to the syrup solution.

title: Avocado Puree
steps: Combine all the ingredients and thoroughly puree with a blender. Transfer to a squeeze bottle.
Prepare close to the time needed, to avoid browning of the avocado purée.

title: Banana, Chocolate and Walnut Dome Walnut Biscuit Praline Krispy Chocolate Cream Insert Banana Mousse Caramel Bananas Walnut Biscuit
steps: Combine the flour and walnuts and set aside.
Whip the yolks and brown sugar to ribbon.
Separately, make a French meringue with the egg whites and sugar and whip to soft peak.
Fold the meringue into the yolks three quarters of the way and finish the folding by adding in the flour and walnuts.
Spread onto parchment lined half sheet pan.
Bake at 375 F. for approximately 10-15 minutes until set.
Allow cake to cool and place in the freezer.
notes: Utilize the biscuit mixing method

title: Basil Foam
steps: Combine all the ingredients in a blender until pureed. Cover and reserve in the refrigerator until ready to use.
For plating, puree the basil foam with an immersion blender and spoon the foam onto the plate.

title: Basil Seeds
steps: Combine water and sugar in a sauce pan and cook over medium heat just until the sugar has dissolved.
Remove from the heat and add the grapefruit juice and basil seeds.
Chill and allow to hydrate about 1 hour.

title: Brown Sugar Mixture
steps: Combine the first 5 (dry) ingredients and mix to combine. Set aside.
Combine the next six ingredients and mix to combine. Then add to the dry ingredients.
Pan mixture into two (9”) loaf pans which have been sprayed and lined with parchment up the sides.
Make a cut down the center top of each batter and add half of the combined brown sugar mixture to each loaf and swirl with a fork to marble through the top.
Bake at 350 F. for approximately 50 minutes.
notes: Utilize the One Step method:

title: Caramel Bananas
steps: Make a dry caramel from the sugar and lemon juice.
Stop the caramel from cooking by adding the water. Heat again if necessary to fully melt the caramel.

Separately, slice the bananas very thinly, evenly and diagonally. Place the bananas in a hotel pan and pour the caramel over the bananas. Allow to cool. Drain for use. Assemble as illustrated in a fleximold domed mold: Shingled Caramel Bananas Banana Mousse Chocolate Cream Insert Praline Crispy Walnut Biscuit

title: Caramel Decorations
steps: Combine the sugar and lemon juice in a sauté pan.
Place the pan over medium heat to begin melting the sugar,
When the sugar begins to caramelize, stir the uncaramelized sugar into the caramelized sugar so the sugar melts and caramelizes evenly.
Lower the heat when most of the sugar has melted so all the sugar crystals dissolve into the caramel without the caramel getting too dark.
Cook the caramel until it is a light golden color.
On a silpat, carefully use a spoon to form, drizzle, or manipulate caramel into desired decoration. Cool until set.
Reheat caramel if needed.

title: Caramelized Cashews
steps: Roast cashews at 150˚C/300°F oven for approximately 10 minutes, or until thoroughly toasted, and reserve warm.
Cook the sucrose, scraped vanilla bean and water to 115°C/240°.
Add cashews to the sugar mixture and continue to cook, stirring constantly. The sugar will begin to crystallize. Continue to stir to coat the cashews completely.
Reduce heat and continue to stir until cashews are coated with caramelized sugar.
Pour caramelized cashews onto a parchment lined sheet pan, separate the nuts if necessary, and allow to cool completely.

title: Caramelized Hazelnuts
steps: Roast almonds at 150˚C/300°F oven for approximately 10 minutes, or until thoroughly toasted, and reserve warm.
Cook the sucrose, scraped vanilla bean and water to 115°C/240°.
Add hazelnuts to the sugar mixture and continue to cook, stirring constantly. The sugar will begin to crystallize. Continue to stir to coat the hazelnuts completely.
Reduce heat and continue to stir until hazelnuts are coated with caramelized sugar.
Pour caramelized hazelnuts onto a parchment lined sheet pan, separate the nuts if necessary, and allow to cool completely.

title: Caramelized Pecans
steps: Roast pecans and reserve warm.
Cook the sugar, scraped vanilla bean and water to 240°F.
Add pecans to the sugar mixture and continue to cook, stirring constantly. The sugar will begin to crystallize. Continue to stir to coat the pecans completely.
Reduce heat and continue to stir until pecans are coated with caramelized sugar.
Pour caramelized pecans onto a parchment lined sheet pan, separate the nuts if necessary, and allow to cool completely.

title: Caramelized Pistachios
steps: Gently warm the pistachios in a low oven. Place sucrose in a medium sauté pan and moisten with water.
Over medium heat, cook sucrose until nearly all water has evaporated and but before it begins to color.
Add the pistachios and stir to coat with sugar.
Continue cooking and stirring as sugar crystallizes. Sugar will begin to melt and caramelize.
When most of the sugar has re-melted and coated the nuts, remove from heat and stir in the butter, gently yet thoroughly. Transfer to a Silpat to cool and store in sealed containers.

title: Caramelized Rice
steps: Place sucrose in a medium sauté pan and moisten with water.
Over medium heat, cook sucrose until nearly all water has evaporated and but before it begins to color.
Add cereal and gently stir to coat with sugar.
Continue cooking and stirring as sugar crystallizes. Sugar will begin to melt and caramelize.

title: Caramelized Rice Cereal
steps: Place sucrose in a medium sauté pan and moisten with water.
Over medium heat, cook sucrose until nearly all water has evaporated and but before it begins to color.
Add cereal and gently stir to coat with sugar.
Continue cooking and stirring as sugar crystallizes. Sugar will begin to melt and caramelize.
When most of the sugar has re-melted and coated the cereal, remove from heat and stir in the butter, gently yet thoroughly. Transfer to a Silpat to cool and store in sealed containers.

title: Chocolate Glacage
steps: Bloom gelatin in cold water and set aside.
Bring sugar and coconut milk to a boil.
Whisk in cocoa powder and bloomed gelatin to boiled mixture.
Strain and reserve. Allow to cool before glazing panna cotta. Assemble as illustrated: Chocolate Glacage Maple Panna Cotta ( 1/2 way ) Pecan Dacqouise Maple Panna Cotta ( 1/2 way ) Pecan Biscuit

title: Crystallized or Caramelized Almonds
steps: Roast almonds at 150˚C/300°F oven for approximately 10 minutes, or until thoroughly toasted, and reserve warm.
Cook the sucrose, scraped vanilla bean and water to 115°C/240°.
Add almonds to the sugar mixture and continue to cook, stirring constantly. The sugar will begin to crystallize. Continue to stir to coat the almonds completely.
Alternatively, if desired to coat the almonds with caramel, reduce heat and continue to stir until almonds are coated with caramelized sugar.
Pour crystallized or caramelized almonds onto a parchment lined sheet pan, separate the nuts if necessary, and allow to cool completely.
Place the nuts into a chilled mixing bowl, set into a lager bowl filled with ice. Slowly pour the tempered chocolate into the nuts, stirring vigorously to evenly coat.
Just as the chocolate coating begins to set, rapidly sift cocoa powder or confectioners' sugar onto the nuts while stirring, until well coated and separated.

title: Lemon Fluid Gel
steps: Combine water and agar in a small sauce pan. Gently bring to a boil; reduce heat while maintaining a simmer for two minutes.
Remove from heat and whisk in the sucrose and lemon juice. Allow to cool and set.
Process in blender until smooth. Transfer to a squeeze bottle.

title: Mango Pearls
steps: Combine the mango purée, lime juice, and sucrose; warm slightly to dissolve the sugar. Reserve warm.
Combine the water, agar, and locust bean gum in a small sauce pan.
Bring this mixture to a boil, whisking occasionally, and then reduce heat and gently simmer for 1 minute.
Remove from heat and incorporate into the mango base. Transfer to a squeeze bottle.
Drop the mixture into cold vegetable oil, allowing 5-10 minutes to set; drain.

title: Milk Chocolate Coconut Chocolate Genoise Mousseline Coconut Caramel Pressed Coconut Caremelized Cashews Milk Chocolate Mousse Chocolate Genoise Mousseline
steps: Combine the eggs and the sugar and whisk over a bain marie until the sugar is dissolved and warm.
Whip eggs and sugar on medium high speed until thickened for a genoise.
While the eggs are whipping, combine the melted butter and rum and set aside.
Sift the dry ingredients together and set aside.
When the egg mixture has thickened enough, sift the dry ingredients into the whipped eggs while folding carefully.
notes: Utilize the Genoise Mousseline mixing method

title: Passion Fruit Gel
steps: Combine water and agar in a small sauce pan. Gently bring to a boil; reduce heat while maintaining a simmer for two minutes.
Remove from heat and whisk in the sucrose and passion fruit purée. Allow to cool and set.
Process in blender until smooth. Transfer to a squeeze bottle.

title: Peanut Butter Powder
steps: Place peanut butter into the work bowl of a food processor. Slowly incorporate the maltodextrin, pulsing the machine until a powdery consistency is achieved.
Transfer to a dry covered container.

title: Pecan Maple Petit Gauteux (Gluten Free, Dairy Free) Pecan Biscuit Pecan Dacquoise Layer Maple Panna Cotta Chocolate Glacage Pecan Biscuit
steps: Melt chocolate over double boiler and add shortening, stir to combine and allow mixture to cool.
Combine the pecans and the dry ingredients in a Robot Coupe, process until pecans are chopped into fine flour.
Whip egg yolks until ribbon.
Separately, make an ordinary meringue with egg whites and sugar.
Fold together meringue and yolks, fold in dry ingredients and melted chocolate mixture.
Spread mixture onto parchment lined half sheet pan and bake at 350 F.
Once cooled, cut cake into ovals to fit oval shaped flexi mold.
notes: Utilize the Biscuit mixing method

title: Praline Krispy
steps: Melt chocolate, stir in praline paste.
Gently mix in feuilletine.
Spread onto the cooled walnut biscuit.
Put in fridge to set, cut into circles to fit the base of the dome fleximolds.

title: Pressed Coconut
steps: Combine the first 5 ingredients into a pot and bring to a boil.
Add the coconut and lower the heat to the lowest setting.
Stir often with a rubber spatula, making sure nothing sticks to the bottom.
Cook until the mixture forms a dough and does not stick to your fingers.
Roll in between two pieces of parchment paper until very thin.
Freeze until firm.

title: Strawberry Puree
steps: Combine the strawberries, syrup, and xanthan gum and process in a blender; strain through a fine mesh sieve. Chill.

title: White Chocolate Pate a Glace
steps: Melt the white chocolate and shortening together gently over a double boiler or in the microwave in short bursts.
Stir until completely smooth.
Use warm as a dipping or glazing coating.

title: Apple Quick Bread

ingredients.name: apples (peeled, cored and cut into thin slices)

steps: In a KitchenAid bowl with a paddle, cream the butter and sugar.
Add the dry ingredients (which have been mixed together well), and the liquid ingredients on top of the dry. Mix all ingredients together until just combined. lined pans and spread evenly.
On top of the batter, spread all of the apples which has been liberally tossed with the cinnamon sugar.
Place the other half of the batter on top of the apples and spread to cover (The batter will barely cover the apples but will expand during baking).
Bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Creaming method:

title: Apple Walnut Muffins
steps: In a KitchenAid mixer with a paddle, cream the butter with the sugar, salt and lemon zest until lightened. Scrape the bowl and the paddle several times to make sure there are no lumps of butter.
Combine the dry ingredients together in a bowl, then add the apples and walnuts and toss to coat.
Whisk together the wet ingredients.
Add the dry ingredients and the liquid ingredients to the creamed butter/ sugar. Mix until just combined, making sure to scrape from the bottom.
Place the batter 2/3 full in 12 paper muffin cups and cover with muffin crumb topping on the previous page if desired.
Bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Dough mixing method:

title: Banana Nut Bread
steps: Whisk together the mashed banana, sugar, melted butter, eggs, and vanilla extract.
In a separate bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, salt, cinnamon, and cloves.
Fold the dry ingredients into the wet ingredients just until combined.
Fold in the chopped walnuts.
Divide the batter into prepared loaf pan(s).
Bake at 350 F until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean.

title: Biscuits
steps: Prepare using Flaky Dough Mixing Method. Gradually stir in milk, adding only enough to make a soft dough.

Flour work surface and scrape dough onto it. Knead lightly several times to make dough smooth, without over-mixing, adding extra flour if the dough is too soft or sticky. Cut with a round cutter and transfer to paper-lined pan. Dust tops of biscuits with flour or paint with milk.

Bake at 450 degrees about 10 minutes, until well risen, a deep golden and baked through. Serve immediately or cool and reheat at 350 degrees before serving. Variation: Cheese Biscuits Add 200 grams grated sharp cheddar cheese, mixing it in well, before adding the liquid to the biscuit dough. Variation: Chive and Cheese Biscuits Add 200 grams grated sharp cheddar cheese and 55 grams finely chopped chives, mixing it in well, before adding the liquid to the biscuit dough. beginning to mix.
notes: *When using buttermilk, add 2 grams baking soda to the dry ingredients before

title: Blueberry Muffins Leavening Exercise
steps: In a Kitchen Aid mixer with a paddle, cream the butter with the sugar and salt until lightened. Scrape the bowl and the paddle several times to make sure there are no lumps of butter.
Beat in the eggs, gradually, until smooth.
Mix the flour and baking powder together well and stir into the batter alternating with the milk, scraping again to ensure the batter is well mixed.
Fold in the remaining berries whole so as not to crush the berries.
Deposit the entire batter into twelve muffin pans lined with paper cups.
Bake muffins at 375 degrees. Cool the muffins in the pan.  No chemical leavening  2x baking powder  3x baking powder  4x baking powder  Substitute baking soda for baking powder  2x baking soda
notes: *Alternate leavening assigned by instructor:

title: Cherry Lemon Scones
steps: Combine the first 6 ingredients in a Kitchen Aid bowl and mix to combine using the paddle attachment.
Add the butter and mix just until the butter disappears. Then add the dried cherries and mix just until combined.
Add the 3 liquid ingredients and mix until just combined.
Place the dough on a lightly floured surface.
Divide dough into 70 gram pieces and shape round. Egg wash and sprinkle sugar on top.
Bake at 400 degrees for about 15 minutes. Variation: Substitute dried apricot or other dried fruit for the cherries.

title: Chocolate Chunk Scones
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method, adding chocolate before adding liquid. Divide each disk into four wedges.
Bake at 450 degrees for about 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Corn Muffins
yield: *Note: make sure to record the corn muffin yield for use in worksheets to follow.
steps: In a bowl, combine well the first 6 (dry) ingredients.
Gradually add the next 6 (liquid) ingredients which have been combined and mixed well.
Mix just until thoroughly combined.
Place the batter 2/3 full in paper muffin cups and bake at 350 F.

title: Corn Muffins
steps: Cover ears of corn with husks in salted, cool water and bring to a boil.
Remove corn from water and remove husks.
Cut kernels from cob and reserve.
In a bowl, combine well the first 6 (dry) ingredients
Combine the next 6 (liquid) ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Mix the wet ingredients into the dry until just thoroughly combined.
Add corn kernels and mix until just combined.
Place the batter 2/3 full in paper muffin cups and bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the One Step mixing method.

title: Cranberry Orange Crumb Muffins Mmmmmmmuffmuffins Muffins
steps: In a KitchenAid mixer with a paddle, cream the butter with the sugar, salt and orange zest until lightened. Scrape the bowl and the paddle several times to make sure there are no lumps of butter.
Add the eggs gradually and mix until smooth, wiping down the sides of the bowl as needed
Mix the flour and baking powder together well and stir into the batter, alternating with the milk, scraping again to ensure the batter is well mixed.
Fold in the cranberries making sure not to crush them too much.
Place the batter 2/3 full in 12 paper muffin cups and cover with all of the muffin crumb topping in the following recipe.
Bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Dough mixing method:

title: Crumble Topping
steps: Place all of the ingredients into a large mixing bowl and mix by hand until a loose, pebble-like texture is achieved; reserve chilled.
Place the cooked apple filling into individual-sized serving dishes or one large, shallow baking dish as instructed.

Liberally cover the surface of the apple filling with the crumble topping and place in a pre-heated 176˚C/350˚F oven. Bake until the crumble topping is crisp and golden brown, about 15 to 30 minutes, depending upon the size and dimensions of the baking dish.
Remove from the oven and cool briefly before serving.

title: Currant Tea Scones
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method, adding currants before adding liquid.
Form a smooth dough. Knead lightly once or twice to complete mixing. to paper-lined pan and egg wash tops carefully. Allow wash to dry and apply a second coat.
Bake scones at 425 degrees about 10 to 15 minutes, being careful that they do not color too deeply, until they are firm but not dry.

title: Gluten-Free Flour Blend
steps: Whisk all ingredients together until well combined.

title: Jordan Marsh Blueberry Muffins
steps: Cream the butter with the sugar and salt until light. Beat in the eggs, one at a time, until smooth. Mix the flour and baking powder together well and stir into the batter alternating with the milk or buttermilk.
Crush 1/4 of the berries and stir into the batter; fold in the remaining berries whole.
Deposit the batter into twelve muffin pans lined with paper cups. Sprinkle tops of muffins with sugar, or crumb topping, below.
Bake muffins at 375 degrees about 30 minutes, until well risen and deep golden. Cool the muffins in the pan.

title: Lemon Cornmeal Scones
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method, adding dried cherries before adding liquid. Divide each disk into four wedges.
Bake at 450 degrees for about 10 to 15 minutes.

title: Rock Scones
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method, adding currants, raisins, and candied orange before adding liquid.
Whisk eggs and milk together and stir into flour and butter mixture to form a rough dough.
Divide dough into scant 2-ounce pieces and roll between palms gently to round.
Bake scones at 450 degrees about 10 to 15 minutes, being careful that they do not color too deeply, or until they are firm, but not dry.

title: Scones
steps: Combine flours, baking powder and salt in a bowl.
Beat butter and sugar together until soft and light. Scrape into flour mixture and rub in evenly. Add optional raisins, if used.
Whisk eggs and milk together and stir into flour and butter mixture to form smooth dough.
Cut the dough into 70 gram units with plastic scraper or bench scraper. Egg wash evenly, allow wash to dry several minutes, then wash again.
Bake scones at 450 degrees about 10 to 15 minutes, being careful that they do not color too deeply, or until they are firm, but not dry.

title: Scones
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method, adding raisins before adding liquid. paper-lined pans and mark in quarters with plastic scraper or bench scraper. Egg wash evenly, allow wash to dry several minutes, and then wash again.
Bake scones at 450 degrees about 10 to 15 minutes, being careful that they do not color too deeply, until they are firm but not dry.

title: Shirley O. Corriher'S Biscuits
steps: Prepare using Mealy Dough Mixing Method.
After adding the cream, add enough buttermilk so the mixture looks likes cottage cheese.
Using a large ice cream scoop, drop biscuits into bowl of flour to coat them. the biscuits to rise and not to spread.
Bake at 425 degrees for approximately 20 minutes, until golden and cooked through.

title: Shortcakes
steps: Prepare using the Flaky Dough Mixing Method
Combine the dry ingredients in the bowl of a standing mixer.
Add butter, and mix with a paddle attachment until the butter is pea size pieces.
Add the liquid ingredients and mix until just combined.
Roll on a lightly floured surface until dough is about ¾ inch thick.
Bake at 375° F until lightly colored.

title: Sour Cherry Crumbcake Squares

ingredients.name: sour cherries, thawed and well drained

steps: Butter a half sheet pan and line the bottom with parchment. 2 . In a KitchenAid bowl, beat the butter, sugar and vanilla until soft and light. 3 . Add the eggs gradually until well incorporated. Mix together the flour and baking powder and stir into the batter alternating with the yolks and milk, making sure not to over mix. 4 . Spread the batter evenly in the prepared pan and scatter the sour cherries evenly over the batter.
Place all of the ingredients into a mixing bowl and mix by hand or machine until a
Liberally cover the surface with the crumb topping and bake in a 350˚F oven.
notes: Utilize the Creaming Dough mixing method:

title: Zucchini Loaves
steps: In a bowl, whisk together the first 3 ingredients
Combine the next 5 (dry) ingredients in a bowl and mix well.
Add the wet ingredients into the dry and mix until just combined.
Add the zucchini and chopped walnuts and mix until incorporated.
Place the batter approximately 2/3 full (about 220 grams) in paper lined loaf pans and bake at 350 F.
notes: Utilize the One Step mixing method.
